## PAGE 00362: (2026x) Model customization

- page_id: `254421946`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254421946/2026x+Model+customization
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2025-12-30T17:41:31.661+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Model customization allows you to customize certain predefined model components based on your needs and preferences. You can create model-based custom views with customized table columns, view palettes, symbolic view symbol styles, and view creation dialogs. Instead of, or in addition to, using the predefined components, you can model them yourself based on your needs for specific projects.

The following model components are available for model-based customization.

###### Views customizations

Model-based views customization is described under the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Model composition' space=''] section's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Views' space=''] subsection:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Custom symbolic view definitions' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating custom symbolic view definitions' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Custom tabular view definitions' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating custom tabular view definitions' space='']

###### Symbol styles customizations

Model-based symbol styles customization is described under the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Model composition' space=''] section's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Views' space=''] subsection:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Custom style sheets' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating style sheets' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Customizing style sheet components' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating style sheet components' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Synchronizing custom style sheet modifications' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables' space='']

###### Creation dialogs customizations

Model-based creation dialogs customization is described under the section's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Custom creation dialogs' space=''] subsection:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Custom creation dialogs' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Customizing View Creation dialog' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Activating View Creation dialog' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254422027 space=SYSML2P version=3 -->
## PAGE 00363: (2026x) Model management

- page_id: `254422027`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254422027/2026x+Model+management
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-12-03T08:30:48.469+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Model management options

###### Containment tree

The Containment tree displays the hierarchy of your model elements, allowing you to review, organize, and modify the model.By default, the Containment tree has the Essential Elements Only mode enabled, displaying the model's essential elements most relevant to your modeling process. Disabling this option displays all model elements, including those less relevant to your modeling process. 
Learn more on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Working with Containment tree' space=''] page.[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Textual Editor

Textual Editor is akin to a code editor allowing you to view and create your model using textual notation in a code-like format. The Textual Editor displays the model of a specific root namespace. 
Learn more on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Working with Textual Editor' space=''] page. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Views

Views allow you to create a specific view of the model and create and modify model elements and their symbols. They do not display the model in its entirety with all of the elements the model contains. A view represents a particular set of model elements, encompassing a particular view of the model. 
Learn more on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Working with views' space=''] page. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

## PAGE 00364: (2026x) Model Patch

- page_id: `254422072`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254422072/2026x+Model+Patch
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T14:33:07.135+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Project management > (2026x) Working with projects > (2026x) Tools
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Model Patch technology allows you to take a part of a model (e.g., one package), export it to a patch file, and then bring that part of the model into another project or branch.

You can use the Model Patch mechanism:

- As an alternative for model merge when the source of truth is stored in the source version.
- For disconnected team collaboration when changes are made only in a certain scope, and it is not necessary to bring the whole project.
- To bring only necessary parts into the model rather than an entire used project.

To learn about the different types of model patches and how to use them, see:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="309936d7-3604-489b-9b77-a054dd20a079">Model Patch</ac:inline-comment-marker> technology allows you to take a part of a model (e.g., one package), export it to a patch file, and then bring that part of the model into another project or branch.</p><p>You can use the Model Patch mechanism:</p><ul><li>As an alternative for model merge when the source of truth is stored in the source version.</li><li>For disconnected team collaboration when changes are made only in a certain scope, and it is not necessary to bring the whole project.</li><li>To bring only necessary parts into the model rather than an entire used project.</li></ul><p>To learn about the different types of model patches and how to use them, see:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="e1047f72-8459-4182-a39b-8a0cfc48d3dc" /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254422902 space=SYSML2P version=4 -->
## PAGE 00365: (2026x) Model Patch types

- page_id: `254422902`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254422902/2026x+Model+Patch+types
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2025-12-17T14:40:55.469+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Project management > (2026x) Working with projects > (2026x) Tools > (2026x) Model Patch
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When creating a model patch, you have two options to choose from: **All selected content**and **Changes from selected version to version #X**. This topic explains the difference between the two options and when to use each one.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Selecting a model patch type.

##### All selected content

Model patches of the *All selected content* type can be created from and applied to local, Teamwork Cloud, and **3D**EXPERIENCE platform projects.

Using this patch type, you can bring all the selected elements as they are. This patch type is always considered to be the source of truth, meaning that the patch content overrides your existing elements and/or adds elements that exist in the source version but not in the target.

Let's say that your source model contains the *Requirements* package with *maxFuelFlowSpeed* and *minFuelFlowSpeed* requirements, and your target model contains the *Requirements* package with *maxFuelCapacity* and *minFuelCapacity* requirements. If you decide to apply a model patch of the **All selected content** type containing the *Requirements* package to the target model, the *maxFuelFlowSpeed* and *minFuelFlowSpeed* requirements are added to the target model, while the *maxFuelCapacity* and *minFuelCapacity* requirements are deleted. In other words, the whole *Requirements* package in the target model is overwritten with data from the patch.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Applying a model patch of the *All selected content* type.

##### Changes from selected version to version #X

You can only create a model patch of the **Changes from selected version to version #X** type from a Teamwork Cloud project. However, you can apply this type of model patch to any project.

If you do not need to override existing elements in the target model but want to add them as concurrent changes, choose to bring changes from the selected version when creating a patch

Let's say that your source model contains the *Requirements* package with *maxFuelFlowSpeed* and *minFuelFlowSpeed* requirements, and your target model contains the *Requirements* package with *maxFuelCapacity* and *minFuelCapacity* requirements. If you apply a model patch of the **Changes from selected version to version #X** type containing the *Requirements* package, the *maxFuelFlowSpeed* and *minFuelFlowSpeed* requirements are added from the patch while the *maxFuelCapacity* and *minFuelCapacity* requirements are retained the target model. In other words the target model data is not overwritten but complemented with data from the patch.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Applying a model patch of the *Changes from selected version to version #X* type.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

This topic explains the difference between the two options and when to use each one.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="patch_types_sysml_v2.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Selecting a model patch type.</h6><h3>All selected content</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7c883924-9107-43da-af1e-554ccb6e0234"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Model patches of the <em>All selected content</em> type can be created from and applied to local, Teamwork Cloud, and <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform projects.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p style="text-align: left;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Using this patch type, you can bring all the selected elements as they are. This patch type is always considered to be the source of truth, meaning that the patch content overrides your existing elements and/or adds elements that exist in the source version but not in the target.</span></p><p style="text-align: left;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Let's say that your source model contains the <em>Requirements</em> package with <em>maxFuelFlowSpeed</em> and <em>minFuelFlowSpeed</em> requirements, and your target model contains the <em>Requirements</em> package with <em>maxFuelCapacity</em> and <em>minFuelCapacity</em> requirements. If you decide to apply a model patch of the <strong>All selected content</strong> type containing the <em>Requirements</em> package to the target model, the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><em>maxFuelFlowSpeed</em> and <em>minFuelFlowSpeed</em> requirements</span> are added to the target model, while the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><em>maxFuelCapacity</em> and <em>minFuelCapacity</em> requirements</span> are deleted. In other words, the whole <em>Requirements</em> package in the target model is overwritten with data from the patch.</span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="model_patch_all_selected_content.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Applying a model patch of the <em>All selected content</em> type.</h6><h3>Changes from selected version to version #X</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="281f347a-3092-4dc4-ac62-ff660a118565"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can only create a model patch of the <strong>Changes from selected version to version #X</strong> type from a Teamwork Cloud project. <span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">However, you can apply this type of model patch to any project.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>If you do not need to override existing elements in the target model but want to add them as concurrent changes, choose to bring changes from the selected version when creating a patch</p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Let's say that your source model contains the <em>Requirements</em> package with <em>maxFuelFlowSpeed</em> and <em>minFuelFlowSpeed</em> requirements, and your target model contains the <em>Requirements</em> package with <em>maxFuelCapacity</em> and <em>minFuelCapacity</em> requirements. If you apply a model patch of the <strong>Changes from selected version to version #X</strong> type containing the <em>Requirements</em> package, the <em>maxFuelFlowSpeed</em> and <em>minFuelFlowSpeed</em> requirements are added from the patch while the <em>maxFuelCapacity</em> and <em>minFuelCapacity</em> requirements are retained the target model. In other words the target model data is not overwritten but complemented with data from the patch.</span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="model_patch_changes_form_specific_version.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Applying a model patch of the <em>Changes from selected version to version #X</em> type.</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254422978 space=SYSML2P version=7 -->
## PAGE 00366: (2026x) Modeling actions with swimlanes

- page_id: `254422978`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254422978/2026x+Modeling+actions+with+swimlanes
- version_number: 7
- version_date: `2025-12-17T15:40:38.592+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Behavior > (2026x) Actions > (2026x) Swimlane grid
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The swimlane grid symbol allows you to model and organize actions based on the elements that are responsible for performing those actions. For more information on swimlanes, see the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Swimlane grid' space='']page.

Within the context of a part that owns or inherits a perform action, the perform action contains actions that are performed by the subparts owned or inherited by the same owner as the perform action. Thus, the swimlane grid symbol is displayed for the perform action, where each individual swimlane in the swimlane pool represents each subpart. You can use swimlanes to model actions and their sequencing based on the performers, i.e., subparts that perform each action. The subparts names are indicated in the swimlane headers.

###### The part*drone in any mission*performs the action*run mission*. The subpartsof*drone in mission*then perform appropriate subactionsof*run mission*. This is illustrated showing subpartsasswimlaneperformers. The subactionsof*run mission*are connected using succession connection used to control the actionsequence. The output of subactionsare connected by a succession flow connection to the input of another subactionsmeaning that the inputs continue to be consumed and the outputs continue to be produced as the actionexecutes as opposed to streaming flow (flow connection). The sequencing of actionusages is controlled using control nodes such as join, fork, merge, and decide.

##### Managing swimlanes

Prerequisites:

- The perform action whose swimlanes represent the subparts is displayed in the view.

###### Displaying swimlane grid and creating additional swimlanes

To display swimlane grid or create additional swimlanes

- To display the swimlane grid:
  - Right-click the perform action symbol and in the shortcut menu, click [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Displaying elements in symbolic views' space=''] , then Display Swimlanes . The swimlane grid is displayed with swimlanes (along with any actions and connectors that may already be defined in the model), representing subparts owned or inherited by the part that owns or inherits the perform action.

50%

part'drone in any mission'{ 
partdrone; 
part'mission control center'; 
performaction'run mission'; 
}

50%

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- To create additional swimlanes:
  - Select the header of an existing swimlane and click the + button on the left or the right side of the swimlane, to create a swimlane to the left or the right of the existing one. Creating a new swimlane creates a subpart for the part that owns or inherits the perform action, represented by the new swimlane.

50%

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

part'drone in any mission'{ 
partdrone; 
part'mission control center'; 
performaction'run mission'; 
}

50%

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

part'drone in any mission'{ 
partdrone; 
part'mission control center';part part1;// new subpart represented by the newly created swimlaneperformaction'run mission';}

If the part that owns or inherits the perform action owns or inherits parts that are not yet displayed as swimlanes, clicking the + button displays a dropdown menu with the following categories:

- Display that has the following options:
  - part <the name of the part that does not yet have the corresponding swimlane displayed in the view> . Click to create the corresponding swimlane.
  - All . Click to create the corresponding swimlanes for all parts that are not represented in the swimlane symbol.
- Create that has the following option:
  - part . Click to create a part, represented by a new swimlane.

50%

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

50%

part'drone in any mission'{ 
partdrone; 
part'mission control center'; 
part'weather station'; // subpart whose representative swimlane is not displayed yet, thus it is available in the Display dropdown menuperformaction'run mission';}

###### Displaying actions in swimlanes

To display actions in swimlanes

- Select one swimlane by right-clicking its header or all swimlanes by right-clicking the swimlane pool. In the shortcut menu, click [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Displaying elements in symbolic views' space='']**Display** , then Display Actions in the dropdown menu . The specific swimlane's shortcut menu contains the same commands as the part element symbol it represents.

###### Displaying parameters, connectors, or other features for element symbols in swimlanes

To display parameters, connectors, or other features for element symbols in swimlanes

- Right-click element symbol(s) in the swimlane(s). In the shortcut menu, click [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Displaying elements in symbolic views' space='']**Display** , then click the appropriate command in its dropdown menu.

##### Modeling actions with swimlanes

###### Creating actions in swimlanes

You may create actions and the appropriate subparts' perform actions via the Textual Editor and then display the actions in swimlanes via the procedure. However, you can create actions in swimlanes using the view palette. This way, the appropriate perform actions are automatically created for the subparts in whose representative swimlanes the actions are created.

To create actions in swimlanes

1. .
2. In the view palette, under the Actions group, click the button for the needed action.
3. Click on the swimlane representing the part that you want to perform the action. Indicate the start and end of an action sequenceusing start and done actions. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Start and done actions' space=''] page.Connect and sequence actions using successions, flows, and succession flows. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Action succession and flow' space=''] page.Control the sequencing of actions using control nodes. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Control nodes' space=''] page.Start and done actions, as well as control nodes, are not performed, thus parts in whose representative swimlanes they are displayed, do not have perform actions for performing them. These actions and nodes are owned by the perform action that owns the swimlane grid symbol, while their symbols are owned by the swimlane grid symbol.

50%

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

part'drone in any mission'{ 
partdrone; 
part'mission control center'; 
performaction'run mission'; 
}

50%

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

part'drone in any mission'{ 
partdrone{ 
perform'run mission'.'check battery';// perform action with applicable feature chain automatically created for the part once theaction 'check battery'was created in the swimlane that represents the part 'drone' 
} 
part'mission control center'{ 
perform'run mission'.'start mission'; // perform action with applicable feature chain automatically created for the part once theaction 'start mission' was created in theswimlane that represents the part 'mission control center' 
} 
performaction'run mission'{ 
action'start mission'; // action created via view 
action'check battery'; // action created via view 
first'start mission'then'check battery'; // succession created via view 
} 
}

###### Deleting actions in swimlanes

To delete actions in swimlanes

- To delete an action element or symbol in swimlane, see the procedure [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing elements' space=''] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing elements' space=''] page. Deleting an action element via swimlane deletes the appropriate perform action from the part responsible for performing the deleted action. Any associated connectors are deleted as well.

###### Changing which parts are responsible for performing actions via swimlanes

You can move actions between swimlanes to change which parts are responsible for performing these actions. The responsible parts are indicated in the header of the swimlane with the keyword «performer».

To change which parts are responsible for performing actions via swimlanes

- Click and drag the action symbol itself from its source swimlane to the target swimlane of the needed perform action.

**Details regarding the change:**

- If the source and target swimlanes are in the same swimlane grid symbol owned by the common perform action, m oving an action from one swimlane to another updates which part is the owner of the perform action responsible for performing the moved action.

50%

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

part'drone in any mission'{ 
partdrone{ 
perform'run mission'.'start mission'; // perform action is owned by the part 'drone' 
} 
part'mission control center'; 
performaction'run mission'{ 
action'start mission'; 
} 
}

50%

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

part'drone in any mission'{ 
partdrone; 
part'mission control center'{ 
perform'run mission'.'start mission'; // perform action's owner is updated to the part 'mission control center' 
} 
performaction'run mission'{ 
action'start mission'; 
} 
}

- If the source and target swimlanes are in different swimlane grid symbols owned by different perform actions (see example below), m oving an action updates:
  - the moved action's owner to the new perform action;
  - which part is the owner of the perform action responsible for performing the moved action;
    - the feature chain for the referenced feature of the appropriate perform action .
- The same applies ifthe moved action is not inherited or owned by the perform action that owns the target swimlane.

50%

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

part'drone mission'{ 
part'drone in any mission'{ 
partdrone; 
part'mission control center'{ 
perform'run mission'.'start mission'; // the part 'mission control center' is the owner of the perform action responsible for performing the action 'start mission';the feature chain for the referenced action is 'run mission'.'start mission' 
} 
performaction'run mission'{ 
action'start mission'; // the owner of the action 'start mission' is the perform action 'run mission' 
} 
} 
part'drone in delivery mission'{ 
partdrone; 
part'distribution center'; 
performaction'run mission'; 
} 
}

50%

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

part'drone mission'{ 
part'drone in any mission'{ 
partdrone; 
part'mission control center'; // the previously owned perform action is moved out 
performaction'run mission'; // the previously owned action 'start mission' is moved out 
} 
part'drone in delivery mission'{ 
partdrone; 
part'distribution center'{ 
perform'run delivery mission'.'start mission'; // the part 'distribution center' is updated as the owner of the perform action responsible for performing the action 'start mission';the feature chain for the referenced action is updated to 'run delivery mission'.'start mission' 
} 
performaction'run delivery mission'{ 
action'start mission'; // the owner of the action 'start mission' is updated to the perform action 'run delivery mission' 
} 
} 
}

###### Deleting parts responsible for performing actions via swimlanes

To delete parts responsible for performing actions via swimlanes

1. Select the header of the swimlane representing the part you want to delete.
2. Follow the procedure [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing elements' space=''] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing elements' space=''] page.

Deleting parts responsible for performing actions via swimlanes also deletes perform actions contained by the deleted part.

###### Adapting actions to specific context using swimlanes

You can adapt the existing actions to specific contexts without affecting the already modeled elements. You can do this by:

1. First, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Subsetting' space=''] the part that owns the perform action, which in turn owns the actions performed by the subparts. The subsetting part inherits the features of the subsetted part, including the subparts and the perform action.
2. You can then [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Redefinition' space=''] the inherited perform action to adapt the subsetting part and its inherited features to the new context.

Then you can:

1. relevant to this context, which creates subparts for the subsetting part. You can then .
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Redefinition' space=''] . You can then manage actions in these swimlanes: To easily redefine the inherited subparts via the swimlane headers, right-click the header of the swimlane that represents the subpart you want to redefine. In the shortcut menu, click**Refactor**, then**Redefine**. If an action is not owned but simply referenced by the perform action that is an inherited feature of the part represented by the swimlane, the action is displayed with the ^ prefix. See example below.
  1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Redefinition' space=''] the actions in these swimlanes via the Textual Editor.
  2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing elements' space=''] action symbols that are unnecessary in this context.
  3. that would then be owned by the redefining perform action of the subsetting part.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']The part*drone in delivery mission*is created by subsetting the part *drone in any mission*(the example of which is displayed at the top of this page). It inherits the perform action*run mission*. However, since additional procedures should be involved, the inherited perform action*run mission*needs modifications within the context of the part*drone in delivery mission*. This is achieved by redefining the perform action*run mission*specifically for delivery missions. This allows introducing new actions relevant to delivery missions without impacting the *drone in mission*part.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The swimlane grid symbol allows you to model and organize actions based on the elements that are responsible for performing those actions. For more information on swimlanes, see the </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Swimlane grid" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Swimlane grid]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> page.</span></p><p><span>Within the context of a part that owns or inherits a perform action, the perform action contains actions that are performed by the subparts owned or inherited by the same owner as the perform action. Thus, the swimlane grid symbol is displayed for the perform action, where each individual swimlane in the swimlane pool represents each subpart. You can use swimlanes to model actions and their sequencing based on the performers, i.e., subparts that perform each action. The subparts names are indicated in the swimlane headers.</span></p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="swimlanes_example.png" /></ac:image><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">The part</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> </span><em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">drone in any mission</em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> performs the action</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> </span><em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">run mission</em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">. The subparts</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> of </span><em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">drone in mission</em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> then perform appropriate subactions</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> of </span><em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">run mission</em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">. This is illustrated showing subparts</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> as </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">swimlane </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">performers. The subactions</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> of </span><em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">run mission</em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> are connected using succession connection used to control the action</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> sequence. The output of subactions</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> are connected by a succession flow connection to the input of another subactions</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> meaning that the inputs continue to be consumed and the outputs continue to be produced as the action</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> executes as opposed to streaming flow (flow connection). The sequencing of action</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> usages is controlled using control nodes such as join, fork, merge, and decide.</span></h6></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3>Managing swimlanes</h3><p>Prerequisites:</p><ul><li><span>The perform action whose swimlanes represent the subparts is </span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/SYSML2P/Displaying+elements+on+symbolic+views">displayed</a><span> in the view.</span></li></ul><h4>Displaying swimlane grid and creating additional swimlanes</h4><p>To display swimlane grid or create additional swimlanes</p><hr /><ul><li>To display the swimlane grid:<ul><li>Right-click the perform action symbol and in the shortcut menu, click <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying elements in symbolic views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong>, then<strong> Display Swimlanes</strong>. <br />The swimlane grid is displayed with swimlanes (along with any actions and connectors that may already be defined in the model), representing subparts owned or inherited by the part that owns or inherits the perform action.</li></ul></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="section" ac:schema-version="1" create additional swimlanes:<ul><li>Select the header of an existing swimlane and click the <strong>+</strong> button on the left or the right side of the swimlane, to create a swimlane to the left or the right of the existing one.<br />Creating a new swimlane creates a subpart for the part that owns or inherits the perform action, represented by the new ri:filename="create_swimlane_result.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="97dd7a3a-0d3d-4dcc-966c-4054ac644073"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'drone in any mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">drone</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'mission control center'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><br />    <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">part1</span>;                                  </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// new subpart represented by the newly created swimlane<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span>perform</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'run mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b6e37a9b-b22f-4dc4-b1ba-251f9dd62e20"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the part that owns or inherits the perform action owns or inherits parts that are not yet displayed as swimlanes, clicking the + button displays a dropdown menu with the following categories:</p><ul><li><strong>Display </strong>that has the following options:<ul><li><em>part &lt;the name of the part that does not yet have the corresponding swimlane displayed in the view&gt;</em>. Click to create the corresponding swimlane.</li><li><em>All</em>. Click to create the corresponding swimlanes for all parts that are not represented in the swimlane symbol.</li></ul></li><li><strong>Create </strong>that has the following option:<ul><li><em>part</em>. Click to create a part, represented by a new swimlane. </li></ul></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="section" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dcfa6669-6efb-460b-b753-00bd54493835"><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ce4a83b6-a594-469d-8568-977305b8c880"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_or_display_swimlanes.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9f14c0b5-cd33-44a6-ab35-9d0a81945b20"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6aaaa2c7-48cf-4ab7-b67c-3fd8388fbe40"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'drone in any mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">drone</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'mission control center'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'weather station'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;   <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// subpart whose representative swimlane is not displayed yet, thus it is available in the Display dropdown menu</span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span>perform</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'run mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Displaying actions in swimlanes</h4><p>To display actions in swimlanes</p><hr /><ul><li>Select one swimlane by right-clicking its header or all swimlanes by right-clicking the swimlane pool. In the shortcut menu, <span>click </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying elements in symbolic views" /><ac:link-body><strong>Display</strong></ac:link-body></ac:link><span>, then</span><span> </span><strong>Display Actions </strong>in the dropdown menu<span>. <br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3dc1898d-3456-4fd1-8697-4223a6d0f6c7"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The specific swimlane's shortcut menu contains the same commands as the</span> part element symbol it represents<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">. </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul><h4><span>Displaying parameters, connectors, or other features for element symbols in swimlanes</span></h4><p><span>To display parameters, connectors, or other features for element symbols in swimlanes</span></p><hr /><ul><li>Right-click element symbol(s) in the swimlane(s). In the shortcut menu, click <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying elements in symbolic views" /><ac:link-body><strong>Display</strong></ac:link-body></ac:link>, then click the appropriate command in its dropdown menu. </li></ul><h3>Modeling actions with swimlanes</h3><h4>Creating actions in swimlanes</h4><p>You may create actions and the appropriate subparts' perform actions via the Textual Editor and then display the actions in swimlanes via the <ac:link ac:anchor="Displaying actions in swimlanes" /> procedure. However, you can create actions in swimlanes using the view palette. This way, the appropriate perform actions are automatically created for the subparts in whose representative swimlanes the actions are created.</p><p>To create actions in swimlanes </p><hr /><ol><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Displaying swimlane grid and creating additional swimlanes"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display or create swimlanes for the perform action in a view]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>In the view palette, under the Actions group, click the button for the needed action.</li><li>Click on the swimlane representing the part that you want to perform the action.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8538ff03-a6b8-4378-9cb3-b2761aa73c73"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Indicate the start and end of an action sequence </span>using start and done actions<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">. See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Start and done actions" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Start and done actions]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</span></span></li><li><span>Connect and sequence actions using successions, flows, and succession flows. See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Action succession and flow" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Action succession and flow]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</span></li><li><span>Control the sequencing of actions using control nodes. See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Control nodes" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Control nodes]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</span></li></ul><p><span>Start and done actions, as well as control nodes, are not performed, thus parts in whose representative swimlanes they are displayed, do not have perform actions for performing them. These actions and nodes are owned by the perform action that owns the swimlane grid symbol, while their symbols are owned by the swimlane grid symbol. </span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="section" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1c32bdda-ab7d-4ac1-9a35-c3df069876ad"><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f8ec2a9a-e63e-495a-b4e3-214a2e9a0e1b"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_action_in_swimlane_before.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="be524e1a-8eaf-4c26-9488-2b7393181112"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'drone in any mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">drone</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'mission control center'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span>perform</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'run mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4e1d31c9-1f45-4af7-88ae-ca0f11dc2264"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_action_in_swimlane_after.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9cbb8236-6503-4583-a1a7-c5b35723b86e"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'drone in any mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span>part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">drone</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">perform</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'run mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'check battery'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;      </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// perform action with applicable feature chain automatically created for the part once the</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"> action 'check battery'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">was created in the swimlane that represents the part 'drone'</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span>part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'mission control center'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">perform</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'run mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'start mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;      <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// perform action with applicable feature chain automatically created for the part once the<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"> action 'start mission' was created in the </span></span>swimlane that represents the part 'mission control center'</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span>perform</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'run mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'start mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;                               <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// action created via view</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'check battery'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;                              <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// action created via view</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">first</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'start mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">then</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'check battery'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// succession created via view</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h4>Deleting actions in swimlanes</h4><p>To delete actions in swimlanes</p><hr /><ul><li>To delete an action element or symbol in swimlane, see the procedure <ac:link ac:anchor="Deleting an element or its symbol via a view"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Deleting an element or its symbol via a view]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Managing elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="23004a7d-2f45-46df-a439-5977de4cbf46"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Deleting an action element via swimlane deletes the appropriate perform action from the part responsible for performing the deleted action. Any associated connectors are deleted as well.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul><h4>Changing which parts are responsible for performing actions via swimlanes</h4><p>You can move actions between swimlanes to change which parts are responsible for performing these actions. The responsible parts are indicated in the header of the swimlane with the keyword <span>«performer»</span>.</p><p>To change which parts are responsible for performing actions via swimlanes </p><hr /><ul><li>Click and drag the action symbol itself from its source swimlane to the target swimlane of the needed perform action.</li></ul><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Details regarding the change:</strong></p><ul><li>If the source and target <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">swimlanes are in the same swimlane grid symbol owned by the common perform action, m</span>oving an action from one swimlane to another updates which part is the owner of the perform action responsible for performing the moved action. </li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="section" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="748624ae-7832-4d30-a7d2-2960c078083c"><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ae60f251-9b35-45d3-bfaa-417b2f6dbadb"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="move_action_to_another_swimlane_before.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="101feb5c-37b0-496a-89d6-7ec1e7e3701a"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'drone in any mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span>part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">drone</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">perform</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'run mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'start mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;   <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// perform action is owned by the part 'drone'</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'mission control center'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">perform</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'run mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'start mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="85516a69-43af-40b7-af5d-042339d2452c"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="move_action_to_another_swimlane_after.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c9e0de7a-64e3-4018-88af-bed7e66bda57"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'drone in any mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">drone</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'mission control center'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">perform</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'run mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'start mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// perform action's owner is updated to the part 'mission control center'</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">perform</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'run mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'start mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><ul><li>If the source and target <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">swimlanes are in different swimlane grid symbols owned by different perform actions (see example below), m</span>oving an action updates:<ul><li>the moved action's owner to the new perform action;</li><li>which part is the owner of the perform action responsible for performing the moved action;<ul><li>the feature chain <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">for the referenced feature of the appropriate perform action</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">. </span></li></ul></li></ul></li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">The same applies if<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> the moved action is not inherited or owned by the perform action that owns the target swimlane. </span><br /></span></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="section" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9316d807-eecd-432b-9498-6223719c52cb"><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d8209dd7-5f3d-4552-bb0a-13e851859333"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="move_action_to_different_swimlane_symbol_before.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1eae025a-02e8-424d-aefe-dfcb557d00fb"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'drone mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span>part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'drone in any mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span>part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">drone</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'mission control center'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">perform</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'run mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'start mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">the part 'mission control center' is the owner of the perform action responsible for performing the action 'start mission'; </span></span>the feature chain for the referenced action is 'run mission'.'start mission'</span></span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">perform</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'run mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'start mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the owner of the action 'start mission' is the perform action 'run mission'</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'drone in delivery mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">drone</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'distribution center'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">perform</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'run mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ef2a0bb8-e369-4a9b-adf9-9ededc3ee428"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="move_action_to_different_swimlane_symbol_after.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8fefd25d-d556-4a7d-80f6-154339de961b"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'drone mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span>part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'drone in any mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">drone</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'mission control center'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the previously owned perform action is moved out</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">perform</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'run mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the previously owned <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">action 'start mission' is moved out</span></span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'drone in delivery mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">drone</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'distribution center'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">perform</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'run delivery mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'start mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">the part 'distribution center' is updated as the owner of the perform action responsible for performing the action 'start mission'; </span></span>the feature chain for the referenced action is updated to 'run delivery mission'.'start mission'</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">perform</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'run delivery mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'start mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the owner of the action 'start mission' is updated to the perform action 'run delivery mission'</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h4>Deleting parts responsible for performing actions via swimlanes </h4><p>To delete parts responsible for performing actions via swimlanes </p><hr /><ol><li>Select the header of the swimlane representing the part you want to delete. </li><li>Follow the procedure <ac:link ac:anchor="Deleting an element or its symbol via a view"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Deleting an element or its symbol via a view]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> on the </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Managing elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> page.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5fb24ce6-d020-4b1f-b5c6-0516b68bd973"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Deleting parts responsible for performing actions via swimlanes also deletes perform actions contained by the deleted part. </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h4>Adapting actions to specific context using swimlanes</h4><p align="justify"><span>You can adapt the existing actions to specific contexts without affecting the already modeled elements. You can do this by:</span></p><ol><li><span>First, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Subsetting" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[subsetting]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> the part that owns the perform action, which in turn owns the actions performed by the subparts. </span><span>The subsetting part inherits the features of the subsetted part, including the subparts and the perform action.</span></li><li><span>You can then <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Redefinition" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[redefine]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> the inherited perform action to adapt the subsetting part and its inherited features to the new context. </span></li></ol><p align="justify"><span>Then you can:</span></p><ol><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Displaying swimlane grid and creating additional swimlanes"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display the swimlane grid and create new swimlanes]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> relevant to this context, which creates subparts for the subsetting part. You can then <ac:link ac:anchor="Creating actions in swimlanes"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[create actions in these swimlanes]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Redefining a usage via the symbol shortcut menu in a view"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Redefinition" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Redefine the inherited subparts]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. You can then manage actions in these swimlanes:<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9fe048c4-f991-4dc6-8e05-ed47ddfcef16"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To easily redefine the inherited subparts via the swimlane headers, <span>right-click the header of the swimlane that represents the subpart you want to redefine. In the shortcut menu, click </span><strong>Refactor</strong><span>, then </span><strong>Redefine</strong><span>.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2eb949be-e3ae-48b3-936d-0fc3c7518543"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">If an action is not owned but simply referenced by the perform action that is an inherited feature of the part represented by the swimlane, the action is displayed with the ^ prefix. See example below.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ol><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Redefining a usage via the Textual Editor"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Redefinition" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Redefine]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> the actions in these swimlanes via the Textual Editor. </li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Deleting an element or its symbol via a view"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Remove from the view]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> action symbols that are unnecessary in this context.</li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Creating actions in swimlanes"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Create actions]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> that would then be owned by the redefining perform action of the subsetting part.</li></ol></li></ol><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="actions_adapted_to_specific_context_using_swimlanes.png" /></ac:image><span style="font-weight: 600;letter-spacing: 0.0px;text-align: center;color:var(--ds-background-accent-gray-bolder,#626f86);">The part </span><em style="font-weight: 600;letter-spacing: 0.0px;text-align: center;color:var(--ds-background-accent-gray-bolder,#626f86);">drone in delivery mission</em><span style="font-weight: 600;letter-spacing: 0.0px;text-align: center;color:var(--ds-background-accent-gray-bolder,#626f86);"> is created by subsetting the part <em>drone in any mission </em>(the example of which is displayed at the top of this page). It inherits the perform action </span><em style="font-weight: 600;letter-spacing: 0.0px;text-align: center;color:var(--ds-background-accent-gray-bolder,#626f86);">run mission</em><span style="font-weight: 600;letter-spacing: 0.0px;text-align: center;color:var(--ds-background-accent-gray-bolder,#626f86);">. However, since additional procedures should be involved, the inherited perform action </span><em style="font-weight: 600;letter-spacing: 0.0px;text-align: center;color:var(--ds-background-accent-gray-bolder,#626f86);">run mission</em><span style="font-weight: 600;letter-spacing: 0.0px;text-align: center;color:var(--ds-background-accent-gray-bolder,#626f86);"> needs modifications within the context of the part </span><em style="font-weight: 600;letter-spacing: 0.0px;text-align: center;color:var(--ds-background-accent-gray-bolder,#626f86);">drone in delivery mission</em><span style="font-weight: 600;letter-spacing: 0.0px;text-align: center;color:var(--ds-background-accent-gray-bolder,#626f86);">. This is achieved by redefining the perform action </span><em style="font-weight: 600;letter-spacing: 0.0px;text-align: center;color:var(--ds-background-accent-gray-bolder,#626f86);">run mission</em><span style="font-weight: 600;letter-spacing: 0.0px;text-align: center;color:var(--ds-background-accent-gray-bolder,#626f86);"> specifically for delivery missions. This allows introducing new actions relevant to delivery missions without impacting the <em>drone in mission </em>part.</span></h6></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254423132 space=SYSML2P version=6 -->
## PAGE 00367: (2026x) Modeling tabular view columns

- page_id: `254423132`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254423132/2026x+Modeling+tabular+view+columns
- version_number: 6
- version_date: `2026-01-08T11:39:49.646+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Views > (2026x) Creating views > (2026x) Creating tabular views > (2026x) Tabular view columns
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

See the procedures on this page for modeling tabular view columns.

#### Prerequisites

The procedures for modeling tabular view columns described on this page can be used for modeling columns for[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Tabular views' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating custom tabular view definitions' space=''], and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating tabular views' space='']. However, the following procedure, Redefining the inherited renderer,must be done in all cases.

##### Redefining the inherited renderer

To redefine the inherited renderer

1. Ensure the package containing the view usage/definition for which you want to model columns imports KerML , SysML , and DS_Views packages. packageCustomTabularViews{ 
privateimportKerML::*; 
privateimportSysML::*; 
privateimportDS_Views::*; 
}
2. Ensure the following steps are done based on whether you want to model table columns for a view usage or a custom view definition:
  1. For a view usage:
    1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating tabular views' space=''] .
    2. The view has an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Exposing elements for views' space=''] .
    3. (Optional) The view has [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Filtering elements for views' space=''] .
  2. For a custom view definition:
    1. Ensure you have [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating custom tabular view definitions' space=''] .
3. Within the body of the view usage/definition, declare the keywords*render rendering*, (optionally) specify the name for the element,**and do one of the following:
  1. Redefine the inherited *asTable*renderer. This applies to:
    1. View usages: views defined by predefined tabular view definitions.
    2. View definitions: new custom tabular view definitions created from scratch or those reusing predefined tabular view definitions. All standard predefined tabular view definitions use the *DS_Views::CoreViews::asTable***renderer.
  2. R edefine the inherited renderer. This applies to:
    1. View usages: views defined by custom tabular view definitions.
