    2. View definitions: new custom tabular view definitions that reuse custom-created tabular view definitions .
4. Click the Synchronize button. Proceed to .

50%

// ---------- view usages

// a view usage defined by a predefined tabular view definition:

view'Drone Structure Table': TabularViews::gt{ 
exposeDroneStructure::**; 
renderrendering:>>asTable;// *Generic Table*has the rendering *asTable* 
}

// a view usage defined by a custom tabular view definition:

view'VCCU System Behavior Table':'System Behavior Table'{ 
exposeDroneStructure::**; 
renderrendering:>> asNewTable1;// *'System Behavior Table'*has the rendering *asNewTable1*, which redefines the *asTable*rendering 
}

50%

// ---------- view definitions

// a new view definition created from scratch:

viewdef'System Behavior Table':>CoreViews::bt{renderrenderingasNewTable1:>>asTable;}

// a new view definition created by reusing a predefined view definition *Generic Table*:

viewdef'System Components Taxonomy Table':> TabularViews::gt{ 
renderrenderingasNewTable2:>>asTable;

}

// a new view definition created by reusing a custom-created view definition 'System Behavior Table':

viewdef'Variant Table':> 'System Behavior Table'{ 
renderrenderingasNewTable3:>> asNewTable1;// *asNewTable3*redefines the *'System Behavior Table'* view definition's *asNewTable1* rendering, which itself redefines the *Base Table*view definition's *asTable* rendering

}

#### Modeling tabular view columns

##### Removing inherited table columns

To remove inherited table columns

1. Ensure .
2. Within the body of the renderer, declare a view, redefine the column you want to remove, and set its multiplicity to zero. The table columns that are inherited from the standard predefined tabular view definitions are indicated in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Tabular views' space=''] table.
3. Click the Synchronize button.

// ------ a view defined by a predefined tabular view:

view'System Components Taxonomy Table': TabularViews::gt{ 
exposeDroneStructure::**; 
renderrendering:>>asTable{ 
view:>>'Declared Short Name'[0]; // view redefines *Declared Short Name***inherited via *Generic Table,*specifying the redefining column's multiplicity to zero to remove it 
} 
}

// ------ a view defined by a custom tabular view:

view'VCCU System Behavior Table':'System Behavior Table'{ 
exposeDroneStructure::**; 
renderrendering:>> asNewTable1{ 
view:>>'Declared Short Name'[0];// view redefines *Declared Short Name*inherited via *'System Behavior Table',*specifying the redefining column's multiplicity to zero to remove it 
} 
}

##### Adding table columns

###### Adding table columns by feature

For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Tabular view columns' space=''] table on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Tabular view columns' space=''] page.

To add table columns by feature

1. Within the body of the renderer, declare a view, specify the column name, define it by *CoreViews::ColumnByFeatureView*, and subset *column*.
2. Within the column view's body, declare *ref item*, redefine *columnFeature*, and specify the expression for the targeted metadata feature.
3. Click the Synchronize button. view'VCCU System Behavior Table':'System Behavior Table'{exposeDroneStructure::**; 
renderrendering:>> asNewTable1{view'Declared Name':CoreViews::ColumnByFeatureView:> column {// new columnrefitem:>> columnFeature = declaredNamemetaKerML::Core::Feature; // targets *Declared Name*}view'Declared Short Name': CoreViews::ColumnByFeatureView:> column {// new column 
refitem:>> columnFeature = declaredShortNamemetaKerML::Core::Feature;// targets *Declared Short Name* 
} 
viewDocumentation: CoreViews::ColumnByFeatureView:> column {// new column 
refitem:>> columnFeature = documentationmetaKerML::Core::Feature;// targets *Documentation* 
} 
view'Qualified Name': CoreViews::ColumnByFeatureView:> column {// new column 
refitem:>> columnFeature = qualifiedNamemetaKerML::Core::Feature;// targets *Qualified Name* 
} 
viewOwner: CoreViews::ColumnByFeatureView:> column {// new column 
refitem:>> columnFeature = ownermetaKerML::Core::Feature;// targets *Owner* 
} 
view'Owned Element': CoreViews::ColumnByFeatureView:> column {// new column 
refitem:>> columnFeature = ownedElementmetaKerML::Core::Feature;// targets for *Owned Element* 
}} 
}Alternative notation// the following expressions are equivalent: 
refitem:>>columnFeature=declaredShortNamemetaKerML::Core::Feature; 
refitem:>>columnFeature=declaredShortName.metadata as KerML::Core::Feature;

###### Adding table columns by expression

For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Tabular view columns' space=''] table on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Tabular view columns' space=''] page.

To add table columns by expression

1. Within the body of the renderer, declare a view, specify the element name, define it by *CoreViews::ColumnByExpressionView*, and subset *column*.
2. Within the column view's body, d eclarethe keywords*render rendering*, specify the name for the element, define it by the needed cell rendering definition, and subset *CoreViews::asTableCell*. The available table cell rendering can be found in the *CoreViews* package.Click here to view themrenderingdefBooleanCellRendering:> TableCellRendering; 
renderingdefElementCellRendering:> TableCellRendering; 
renderingdefEnumCellRendering:> TableCellRendering; 
renderingdefIntegerCellRendering:> TableCellRendering; 
renderingdefMetaobjectCellRendering:> TableCellRendering; 
renderingdefRealCellRendering:> TableCellRendering; 
renderingdefStringCellRendering:> TableCellRendering;rendering def TableCellRendering { 
 calc getValue { in rowElement [0..1] : Metaobjects::Metaobject; } 
 attribute isMultiValued [0..1] : ScalarValues::Boolean; 
 }
3. Within the body of the rendering, declare a calculation and redefine *getValue*. Specify the *in* direction, redefine the *rowElement*. Specify the needed expression. See the example below.
4. Click the Synchronize button.

viewdef'Drone Structure Table':>TabularViews::gt,SymbolicViewsByExpression::NonStandardLibraryElementFilter{// custom view definition reusing *Generic Table* 
filterhastypeSysML::Systems::PartDefinition|hastypeSysML::Systems::PartUsage; 
renderrenderingnewTable:>>asTable{// redefining the inherited table rendering 
viewWeight:CoreViews::ColumnByExpressionView:>column{// new custom column *Weight* 
renderrenderingasTableCell:CoreViews::RealCellRendering:>CoreViews::asTableCell{// specifying the table column's cell rendering as *Real* 
calc:>>getValue{ 
in:>>rowElement:KerML::Root::Element; 
ifgetWeight(rowElement) @KerML::Kernel::LiteralInteger? (getWeight(rowElement)asKerML::Kernel::LiteralInteger).valueelse 
(getWeight(rowElement)asKerML::Kernel::LiteralRational).value 
} 
calcgetWeight{ 
ine:KerML::Root::Element; 
(((e.ownedElementasSysML::Systems::AttributeUsage)->ControlFunctions::select{ 
inee; 
(eeasKerML::Root::Element).name=="weight" 
}asSysML::Systems::AttributeUsage).ownedRelationshipasKerML::Kernel::FeatureValue).value 
} 
} 
} 
} 
}

##### Modifying table columns

###### Modifying table column attributes

To modify table column attributes

1. (Conditional) For inherited columns, redefine the column whose attributes you want to modify.
2. Within the column view's body, redefine the needed attribute and set its value. Predefined column attributes are:*isMultiline*. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Tabular view columns' space='']>]]>
3. Click the Synchronize button.

view'VCCU System Behavior Table':TabularViews::gt{ 
exposeDroneStructure::**; 
renderrendering:>>asTable{ 
 // new column 
view'Qualified Name':CoreViews::ColumnByFeatureView:>column{ // new column 
attribute:>>isMultilinedefaulttrue;// setting multiline to *true*to wrap long qualified name 
refitem:>>columnFeature=qualifiedNamemetaKerML::Core::Feature;// targets Qualified Name 
} 
// redefining an inherited column 
 view :>> 'Qualified Name' { // redefining an inherited column 
 attribute :>> isMultiline default true;// setting multiline to *true*to wrap a long qualified name 
 } 
} 
}

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">See the procedures on this page for modeling tabular view columns. </span></p><h2><span>Prerequisites</span></h2><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c931d1f2-e291-47de-9b8b-6684503e5178"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>The procedures for modeling tabular view columns described on this page can be used for modeling columns for </span><span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Tabular views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[predefined tabular view definitions]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating custom tabular view definitions" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[custom tabular view definitions]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating tabular views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[individual tabular view usages]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. However, the following procedure</span><span>, <a>Redefining the inherited renderer</a>, </span><span>must be done in all cases.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Redefining the inherited renderer</h3><p>To redefine the inherited renderer</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="74e261ca-4f3c-4c8d-99cd-04ee7aaf6350">Ensure the package containing the view usage/definition for which you want to model columns imports <em>KerML</em>, <em>SysML</em>, and <em>DS_Views </em>packages.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="afd463e3-d9e2-4dc0-8b25-6a5cb3652e41"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CustomTabularViews</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">private</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">import</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">KerML</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">::*;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">private</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">import</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">SysML</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">::*;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">private</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">import</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_Views</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">::*;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="c2a1a51a-9646-47e6-bc7c-5e8175be36a5">Ensure the following steps are done based on whether you want to model table columns for a view usage or a custom view definition:<ol><li>For a view usage:<ol><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating tabular views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[The view is defined by the needed tabular view definition]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>The view has an <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Exposing elements for views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[expose relationship]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. </li><li>(Optional) The view has <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Filtering elements for views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[filters]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li></ol></li><li>For a custom view definition:<ol><li>Ensure you have <ac:link ac:anchor="Creating a custom tabular view definition"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating custom tabular view definitions" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[created the new view definition instance]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li></ol></li></ol></li><li data-uuid="079eeaa9-ccca-4761-b3e7-128de7ff0165">Within the body of the view usage/definition, declare <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">the keywords<span> </span><em>render rendering</em>, (optionally) specify the name for the element,<em><span> </span></em>and do one of the following:</span><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Redefine the inherited <em>asTable </em>renderer. This applies to:</span><ol><li>View usages: views defined by predefined tabular view definitions.</li><li>View definitions: new custom tabular view definitions created <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">from scratch or those </span>reusing predefined tabular view definitions.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b00fbd3a-de63-4494-bfef-0c39cd24dee3"><ac:rich-text-body><p>All standard predefined tabular view definitions use the <em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">DS_Views::CoreViews::asTable</span></em><em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> </em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">renderer.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></li><li>R<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">edefine the inherited renderer. This applies to:</span><ol><li>View usages: views defined by custom tabular view definitions.</li><li>View definitions: new custom tabular view definitions that reuse custom-created tabular view definitions<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">.</span></li></ol></li></ol></li><li data-uuid="f330cbc2-5cc7-46b4-b8ba-4d84a1cf0be8"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Click the<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">Synchronize<span> </span></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">button. Proceed to <ac:link><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[model table columns]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li></ol></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="section" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e98135f4-c626-46ba-8d42-3c965096ad96"><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="46b300fc-7b51-4cc1-88f9-150d0e00a5ab"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="40f2c93a-6f18-4de1-b355-5ca59e6cd394"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// ---------- view usages</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// a view usage defined by a predefined tabular view definition:</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'Drone Structure Table' </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">: <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">TabularViews</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">::gt</span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">expose</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DroneStructure</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">::**;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">render</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">rendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">asTable</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// <em>Generic Table </em>has the rendering <em>asTable</em></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// a view usage defined by a custom tabular view definition:</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'VCCU System Behavior Table'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">'System Behavior Table'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">expose</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DroneStructure</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">::**;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">render</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">rendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; asNewTable1</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// <em>'System Behavior Table' </em>has the rendering <em>asNewTable1</em>, which redefines the <em>asTable </em>rendering</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}<br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3df55f68-d1ad-42e3-9909-27233f5b2aea"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="07e6f2c7-3e19-46e3-a9ac-070c78967525"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// ---------- view definitions</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// a new view definition created from scratch:</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">'System Behavior Table'</span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CoreViews::bt</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   </span><u style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">render</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">rendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> asNewTable1</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">asTable;<br /></span></u><u style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">}</span></u></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// a new view definition created by reusing a predefined view definition <em>Generic Table</em>:</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">'System Components Taxonomy Table'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">TabularViews</span></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">::gt</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">   </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">render</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">rendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> asN</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">ewTable2</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">asTable;</span></u></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">}</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// a new view definition created by reusing a custom-created view definition 'System Behavior Table':</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">'Variant Table'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">'System Behavior Table'</span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">   </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">render</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">rendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> asN</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">ewTable3</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; <u>asN<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">ewTable1</span></u></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">;</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">  </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// <em>asNewTable3 </em>redefines the <em>'System Behavior Table'</em> view definition's <em>asNewTable1</em> rendering, which itself redefines the <em>Base Table </em>view definition's <em>asTable</em> rendering  </span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h2>Modeling tabular view columns</h2><h3><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Removing inherited table columns</span></h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">To remove inherited table columns</span></p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="d6763656-d7db-477b-91b7-ad39080aa30a"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Ensure <ac:link ac:anchor="Redefining the inherited renderer"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[the inherited renderer is redefined]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li data-uuid="cfac2fde-c91e-40ca-b319-4ece21fbab6f"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Within the body of the renderer, declare a view, redefine the column you want to remove, and set its multiplicity to zero.</span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="69a869db-c56f-47bc-9fb5-dd9e448a09bf"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The table columns that are inherited from the standard predefined tabular view definitions are indicated in the <ac:link ac:anchor="Tabular views"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Tabular views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Tabular views]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> table.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="a12a0793-1b44-42c4-b029-43e324cc64bd"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Click the<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">Synchronize<span> </span></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">button.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0da79d01-6c4d-4e59-91f4-1ca0e1f8119b"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// ------ a view defined by a predefined tabular view:</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'System Components Taxonomy Table' </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">: <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">TabularViews</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">::</span></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">gt</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">expose</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DroneStructure</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">::**;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">render</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">rendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">asTable</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">         </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'Declared Short Name'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> [</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">0</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><u>];</u> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// view redefines <em>Declared Short Name</em><em> </em>inherited via <em>Generic Table, </em>specifying the redefining column's multiplicity to zero to remove it</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// ------ a view defined by a custom tabular view:</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'VCCU System Behavior Table'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">'System Behavior Table'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">expose</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DroneStructure</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">::**;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">render</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">rendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; asNewTable1</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'Declared Short Name'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> [</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">0</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">];</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// view redefines <em>Declared Short Name </em>inherited via <em>'System Behavior Table', </em>specifying the redefining column's multiplicity to zero to remove it</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Adding table columns</h3><h4>Adding table columns by feature</h4><p>For more information, see the <span><ac:link ac:anchor="Column kinds"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Tabular view columns" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Column kinds]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> table on the <span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Tabular view columns" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Tabular view columns]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page</span>.</span></p><p>To add table columns by feature</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="c6b4e088-ffa6-4f3e-a8f9-cd10d9d5ae40"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Within the body of the renderer, declare a view, specify the column name, define it by <em>CoreViews::ColumnByFeatureView</em>, and subset <em>column</em>. </span></li><li data-uuid="fa7906bf-8bee-4a52-88b8-577ad0f5e562"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Within the column view's body, declare <em>ref item</em>, redefine <em>columnFeature</em>, and specify the expression for the targeted metadata feature. </span></li><li data-uuid="ad4fc296-c895-4c9c-804b-4dd461ef0fe5"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Click the<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">Synchronize<span> </span></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">button.<br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7aafe6e3-8594-45d9-a734-6baf31f3e660"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'VCCU System Behavior Table'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">'System Behavior Table'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">   expose</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DroneStructure</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">::**;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">render</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">rendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; asNewTable1</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">      view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'Declared Name'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CoreViews::ColumnByFeatureView </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt; column {</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// new column <br /></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">         </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">ref</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; columnFeature = declaredName </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">meta</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> KerML::Core::Feature;  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// targets <em>Declared Name<br /></em></span></span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      }</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'Declared Short Name'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CoreViews::ColumnByFeatureView </span>:&gt; column {<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// new column </span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">         </span><span Name</em></span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">Documentation</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CoreViews::ColumnByFeatureView </span>:&gt; column {<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// new column </span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">         </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">ref</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; columnFeature = documentation </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">meta</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> KerML::Core::Feature;<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// targets <em>Documentation</em></span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'Qualified Name'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// new column </span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">         </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">ref</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; columnFeature = qualifiedName </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">meta</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">Owner</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CoreViews::ColumnByFeatureView </span>:&gt; column {<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// new column </span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">         </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">ref</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; columnFeature = owner </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">meta</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> KerML::Core::Feature;<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// targets <em>Owner</em></span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'Owned Element'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// new column </span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">         </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">ref</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; columnFeature = ownedElement </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">meta</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> KerML::Core::Feature;<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// targets for <em>Owned Element</em></span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      }<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b9fc132d-e9d2-4121-bdd4-98f0433d70cd"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Alternative notation</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="55e92dd5-8bf9-425d-8227-76ad3200dfd0"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the following expressions are equivalent:</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">ref</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">columnFeature</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> = </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">declaredShortName</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">meta</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">KerML::Core::Feature</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">; </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">ref</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">columnFeature</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> = </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">declaredShortName</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">metadata as <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">KerML::Core::Feature</span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h4>Adding table columns by expression</h4><p>For more information, see the <span><ac:link ac:anchor="Column kinds"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Tabular view columns" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Column kinds]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> table on the <span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Tabular view columns" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Tabular view columns]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page</span>.</span></p><p>To add table columns by expression</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="8186e9bc-18a8-437b-a313-b854a6eb44e6"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Within the body of the renderer, declare a view, specify the element name, define it by <em>CoreViews::ColumnByExpressionView</em>, and subset <em>column</em>. </span></li><li data-uuid="40d1ce1b-ac2c-4f56-a253-9da21c61d67d"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Within the column view's body, d</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">eclare<span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">the keywords<span> </span><em>render rendering</em>, specify the name for the element, define it by the needed cell rendering definition, and subset <em>CoreViews::asTableCell</em>.</span></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a7d2b960-d8ba-4118-90c0-ea9614b0f73f"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The available table cell rendering can be found in the <em>CoreViews</em> package. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="de900749-4699-4b89-88bc-cd1f61a6e481"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Click here to view them</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0281730f-df5d-4c82-8975-144ed9c2b265"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">rendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">BooleanCellRendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; TableCellRendering;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">rendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ElementCellRendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; TableCellRendering;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">rendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">EnumCellRendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; TableCellRendering;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">rendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">IntegerCellRendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; TableCellRendering;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">rendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">MetaobjectCellRendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; TableCellRendering;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">rendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">RealCellRendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; TableCellRendering;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">rendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">StringCellRendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; TableCellRendering;</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">rendering</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">TableCellRendering</span> {<br />        <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">calc</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">getValue</span> { <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">in</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">rowElement</span> [<span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">0</span>.<span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">.1</span>] : Metaobjects::Metaobject; }<br />        <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">isMultiValued</span> [<span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">0</span>.<span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">.1</span>] : ScalarValues::Boolean;<br />    }</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="5571ec5c-812a-4b59-bbca-63a61e40ff2d"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Within the body of the rendering, declare a calculation and redefine <em>getValue</em>. Specify the <em>in</em> direction, redefine the <em>rowElement</em>. Specify the needed expression. See the example below.</span></li><li data-uuid="4bbdd351-c0eb-4fe4-8e33-bd84a0b8f680"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Click the<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">Synchronize<span> </span></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">button.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6d95d162-e50b-4052-abe7-1bd7d83f4104"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">'Drone Structure Table'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">TabularViews</span>::gt</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">, </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">SymbolicViewsByExpression::NonStandardLibraryElementFilter </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">{    </span><span <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254422805 space=SYSML2P version=3 -->
````
## PAGE 00369: (2026x) Modifiers

- page_id: `254422805`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254422805/2026x+Modifiers
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-12-03T08:39:32.479+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Element management > (2026x) Working with elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Usages can have additional properties indicated by the keywords added in the usage declaration. The keywords should be specified in the following order:

1. *in, out, inout* – indicates a direction for a directed usage.
  1. *in* – specifies what is input to an instance.
  2. *out* – specifies what is output by an instance.
  3. *inout* – specifies what is both input to and output by an instance.
2. *derived* – specifies a derived usage that is typically expected to have a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Binding' space=''] expression that completely determines its value at all times.
3. *abstract* – specifies an abstract usage whose value must also be a value of a concrete usage that specializes the abstract usage. If a usage is not abstract, it is a concrete usage.
4. *constant –* specifies that the usage is constant. Its values are the same during the entire existence of the featuring instance.
5. *ref* – specifies a referential usage. Directed usages are referential by default, whether the*ref*keyword is specified or not. If a usage is not referential, it is a composite usage, which is deleted once its featuring instances are deleted.

- For more information on using multiple keywords and their order in element declaration, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing textual notation' space=''] section on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing textual notation' space=''] page.
- The modifier keyword's display on the element symbol can be controlled via the Show Modifiers symbol style. See [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Modifying styles of individual symbols' space=''] section on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Modifying styles of individual symbols' space=''] page.

##### Specifying the direction modifier for features

###### Specifying the direction modifier for features via the Textual Editor

To specify the direction modifier for features via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, before the element metaclass keyword, specify the needed direction keyword: *in, out*, or *inout.*
2. Click the Synchronize button. partdefTank:>Container{ 
initemfuelFlow:Fuel; 
}

###### Specifying the direction modifier for features via the element shortcut menu

To specify the direction modifier for features via the element shortcut menu

- Right-click a usage element in the Containment tree or its symbol in a view and in its shortcut menu, in the Properties group, click**Direction**, and in its submenu, clickeither *in*, *out*, or *inout* .

##### Specifying the derived modifier for features

###### Specifying the derived modifier for features via the Textual Editor

To specify the derived modifier for features via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, before the element metaclass keyword, specify the derived keyword.
2. Click the Synchronize button. itemdefTestRecord{ 
//... 
derivedattributeaverageScore[1] :Rational; 
}

##### Specifying the abstract modifier for features

###### Specifying the abstract modifier for features via the Textual Editor

To specify the abstract modifier for features via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, before the element metaclass keyword, specify the abstract keyword.
2. Click the Synchronize button. abstractpartdefVehicle;

##### Specifying the constant modifier for features

###### Specifying the constant modifier for features via the Textual Editor

To specify the constant modifier for features via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, before the element metaclass keyword, specify the constant keyword.
2. Click the Synchronize button. occurrencedefApprovedFlight:>Flight{ 
constant ref partapprovedAircraftredefinesaircraft; 
}

##### Specifying the ref modifier for features

#### NOTE: Referential, composite and reference usages

Referential, composite and reference usages

Referential usage references a usage that is an instance of another composition. If a usage is not referential, it is composite, which is deleted once its featuring instances are deleted.

partdefVehicle{ 
parteng:Engine;// composite 
refpartdriver:Person;// referential 
}

Reference usages are declared without the element kind keyword and are not restricted to being defined by a definition of any specific kind. Its declaration may optionally include the *ref*keyword, however, reference usages are implicitly referential.

part def Container { 
 item content; 
} 
part def Tank :> Container { 
 fuel : Fuel :>> content; // reference usage 
}

###### Specifying usages as composite or referential via the Textual Editor

To specify usages as composite or referential via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, before the element metaclass keyword, specify one of the following:
  1. For a referential usage, specify the keyword ref .
  2. For a composite usage, remove the keyword ref .
2. Click the Synchronize button. partdefVehicle{ 
parteng:Engine;// composite 
refpartdriver:Person;// referential 
}

###### Specifying usages as composite or referential via the element shortcut menu

To specify usages as composite or referential via the element shortcut menu

1. Right-click a usage in the Containment tree or its symbol in a view and in the shortcut menu, and do one of the following:
  1. For a referential usage, deselect the **Is Composite** property.
  2. For a composite usage, select the **Is Composite** property.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Usages can have additional properties indicated by the keywords added in the usage declaration. The keywords should be specified in the following order:</span></p><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><em>in, out, inout</em> – indicates a direction for a directed usage.</span><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><em>in</em> – specifies what is input to an instance.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><em>out</em> – specifies what is output by an instance.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><em>inout</em> – specifies what is both input to and output by an instance.<br /></span></li></ol></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><em>derived</em> – </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">specifies a derived usage that is typically expected to have a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Binding" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[bound value]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> expression that completely determines its value at all times.<br /></span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><em>abstract</em> – specifies an abstract usage whose value must also be a value of a concrete usage that specializes the abstract usage. If a usage is not abstract, it is a concrete usage.<br /></span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><em>constant – </em></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">specifies that the usage is constant. Its values are the same during the entire existence of the featuring instance.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><em>ref</em> – specifies a referential usage. Directed usages are referential by default, whether the<span> </span><em>ref</em><span> </span>keyword is specified or not. If a usage is not referential, it is a composite usage, which is deleted once its featuring instances are deleted.<br /></span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="392a7c75-7a2b-4bf6-8c99-23706bcc3b9d"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="0ceb7dea-5766-4d9a-9a7b-c28b254dcc89">For more information on using multiple keywords and their order in element declaration, see the <ac:link ac:anchor="Keywords"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing textual notation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Keywords]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> section on the <ac:link ac:anchor="Keywords"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing textual notation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Managing textual notation]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page. </li><li data-uuid="10286a9e-3e56-480a-a8c9-bb91e490f3db">The modifier keyword's display on the element symbol can be controlled via the <strong>Show Modifiers</strong> symbol style. See <ac:link ac:anchor="The Symbol Style dialog"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Modifying styles of individual symbols" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[The Symbol Style dialog]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> section on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Modifying styles of individual symbols" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Modifying styles of individual symbols]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Specifying the direction modifier for features</h3><h4>Specifying the direction modifier for features via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To specify the direction modifier for features via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Textual Editor, before the element metaclass keyword, specify the needed direction keyword: <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><em>in, out</em>, or <em>inout.</em></span></li><li>Click the <strong>Synchronize </strong>button.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8ddc396a-e258-4521-a197-59d3a6d0e790"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Tank</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Container</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">in</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fuelFlow</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Fuel</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h4>Specifying the direction modifier for features via the element shortcut menu</h4><p>To specify the direction modifier for features via the element shortcut menu</p><hr /><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Right-click a usage element in the Containment tree or its symbol in a view<span> </span></span><span>and in its shortcut menu, in the<span> </span></span><strong>Properties</strong><span><span> </span>group, click<span> </span><strong>Direction</strong>, and in its submenu, click<span> either <em>in</em>, <em>out</em>, or <em>inout</em></span></span><span>.</span></li></ul><h3>Specifying the derived modifier for features</h3><h4>Specifying the derived modifier for features via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To specify the derived modifier for features via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Textual Editor, before the element metaclass keyword, specify the <em>derived </em>keyword.</li><li>Click the <strong>Synchronize </strong>button.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1d4eb3e3-ad3a-4da9-9bf8-bee7ac3fd8d8"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span features via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To specify the abstract modifier for features via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Textual Editor, before the element metaclass keyword, specify the features via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To specify the constant modifier for features via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Textual Editor, before the element metaclass keyword, specify the <em>constant </em>keyword.</li><li>Click the <strong>Synchronize </strong>button.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="95ed3935-f047-4fec-ad98-00c4bdeaa3f6"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">occurrence</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span
````
