style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">aircraft</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h3>Specifying the ref modifier for features</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1a5b6681-7348-4df6-96e5-58a171173d7f"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Referential, composite and reference usages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Referential usage references a usage that is an instance of another composition. If a usage is not referential, it is composite<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">, which is deleted once its featuring instances are deleted.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bc8328b0-a6a4-4ebd-a726-ed8fbda5199f"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Vehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">eng</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Engine</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;           </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// composite</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">ref</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">driver</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Person</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// referential</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Reference usages are declared without the element kind keyword and are not restricted to being defined by a definition of any specific kind. Its declaration may optionally include the <em>ref </em>keyword, however, reference usages are implicitly referential. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d4b4514f-cfce-439f-a9ca-2f16c931494b"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Container</span> {<br />    <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">content</span>;<br />}<br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Tank</span> :&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Container</span> {<br />    <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fuel</span> : <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Fuel</span> :&gt;&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">content</span>; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// reference usage</span><br />}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Specifying usages as composite or referential via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To specify usages as composite or referential via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Textual Editor, before the element metaclass keyword, specify one of the following:<ol><li>For a referential usage, specify the keyword <em>ref</em>. </li><li>For a composite usage, remove the keyword <em>ref</em>. </li></ol></li><li>Click the <strong>Synchronize </strong>button.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="21b28bad-5999-41f1-b924-1b8bebdbbcf6"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Vehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">eng</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Engine</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;           </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// composite</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">ref</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">driver</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Person</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// referential</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h4>Specifying usages as composite or referential via the element shortcut menu</h4><p>To specify usages as composite or referential via the element shortcut menu</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click a usage in the Containment tree or its symbol in a view and in the shortcut menu, and do one of the following:<ol><li>For a referential usage, <span>deselect the <strong>Is Composite</strong> property.</span></li><li>For a composite usage, <span>select the <strong>Is Composite</strong> property.</span></li></ol></li></ol>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254422725 space=SYSML2P version=4 -->
## PAGE 00370: (2026x) Modifying styles of individual symbols

- page_id: `254422725`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254422725/2026x+Modifying+styles+of+individual+symbols
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2026-06-18T11:04:00.243+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Views > (2026x) Symbol styles
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can customize the styles of individual symbols. Using the Symbol Style dialog, you can specify style attributes for specific symbols, determining their appearance.

Changing the styles for an individual symbol via the Symbol Style dialog overwrites both predefined and custom model-based symbol styles; the modifications persist even after updating [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Activating style sheets' space='']. To reset, see the procedure.

##### Opening the Symbol Style dialog

To open the Symbol Style dialog

Do one of the following:

- Right-click the selected element symbol(s). In the shortcut menu, click **Symbol Style**. To invoke the Symbol Style dialog for a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Docked symbols' space=''], you must right-click the symbol's header.
- Select the element symbol(s) and press Alt+Enter .

##### Modifying individual symbol styles

To modify individual symbol styles

1. Select the symbol(s) whose styles you want to modify and .
2. Locate the attribute you want to modify and change its value to the needed one in the Value column.
3. Click Close .

##### Resetting changes made in the Symbol Style dialog

While changing the styles for an individual symbol via the Symbol Style dialog overwrites both predefined and custom model-based symbol styles, you can easily undo them following the procedure below.

To reset changes made in the Symbol Style dialog

1. .
2. Locate the attribute you want to reset to the style of the currently activated style sheet. Its check box in the Default column is deselected. [ATTACHMENT filename='Resetting_changes.png']
3. Select the check box in the Default column for the needed attribute.
4. Click Close .

##### The Symbol Style dialog

The table below lists the style attributes available for modification and their descriptions.

- Modifying a symbol style changes its Default value to false .
- You can hide unwanted symbol styles from the Symbol Style dialog for specific element kinds. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating style sheet components' space=''] procedure on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating style sheet components' space=''] page.

In the **Symbol Style** dialog, symbol styles are ordered into the following groups:

- **Main**: this attribute group displays symbol styles available to the majority of element kinds.
- **Compartment-based attributes**: the availability of specific symbol style groups depends on the element kind, as [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Compartments' space='']. Some of the symbol styles available to individual compartment-based symbol style groups may be the same as in the Main group, while others are compartment-specific.
  - Compartment-based style groups can be: Documentation, Objective, Subject, Actors, Stakeholders, Include Use Cases, Parts, Ports, Parameters, Attributes, Ends, Satisfy Requirements , Items, Constraints, Require Constraints, Assume Constraints, Assert Constraints, Actions, Perform Actions, Exhibit States, Frames, Enumerations, and Metadata.

| Name | Value options | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Main |  |  |
| Line Width | Positive number | Specify the width of the symbol's outline. |
| Fill Color | SwatchesHSVHSLRGBCMYK | Specify the color of the symbol's header.To enable a gradient for this attribute, enable the view style attribute. |
| Use Fill Color | true/false | Specify whether to use a specific color for the symbol's header. |
| Pen Color | SwatchesHSVHSLRGBCMYK | Specify the color of the symbol's outline. |
| Text Color | SwatchesHSVHSLRGBCMYK | Specify the color of the symbol's text. |
| Font | Multiple font optionsMultiple style options (italic, bold, etc.)Multiple size options | Specify the font style of the symbol's text. |
| Wrap | true/false | Specify whether the text should wrap to fit the width of the element symbol. |
| Compartment Fill Color | SwatchesHSVHSLRGBCMYK | Specify the color of the symbol's compartments. |
| Compartment Text Color | SwatchesHSVHSLRGBCMYK | Specify the color for the text in the symbol's compartments. |
| Show Short Name | true/false | Specify whether the element's should be displayed on the element symbol. |
| Show Name | true/false | Specify whether the element's should be displayed on the element symbol. |
| Show Modifiers | true/false | Specify whether the element's (e.g., in, ref, abstract, etc.) should be displayed on the element symbol. The attribute does not control the individual and portion kind (timeslice, snapshot) modifiers. |
| Show Full Text | true/false | Specify whether element values should be displayed in full or abbreviated. Modifying this attribute affects the values displayed in both the selected symbol's name and its compartments.Modifying this attribute does not affect element values displayed in the metadata compartment. Values in this compartment are always displayed in full.The attribute is available for specific symbols based on the element kind. |
| Content Fill Color | SwatchesHSVHSLRGBCMYK | Specify the color of the symbol's body.To enable a gradient for this attribute, enable the view style attribute.The attribute is available for specific symbols based on the element kind. |
| Text Vertical Position | TopCenterBottom | Specify the element name position for the symbol.The attribute is available for specific symbols based on the element kind.The attribute is only effective for element symbols displayed without their compartments. |
| Show Specialization | true/false | Specify whether the textual symbols should be displayed in the element name (e.g., the textual symbol :> or the keyword specializes, in the case of subclassification). The attribute is available for specific symbols based on the element kind. |
| Show Qualified Name for Specialization | true/false | Specify whether the specialization's full should be displayed in the element name.Enabling the attribute also requires the 'Show Specialization' attribute to be enabled.The attribute is available for specific symbols based on the element kind. |
| Orientation | HorizontalVertical | Specify the orientation for the symbol.The attribute is available for specific symbols based on the element kind. |
| Path Style | RectilinearObliqueBezier | Specify the style of the path.The attribute is available for specific symbols based on the element kind. |
| Show Value | true/false | Specify whether the element's defined value should be displayed.The attribute is available for specific symbols based on the element kind. |
| Show Inherited Sign | true/false | Specify whether the inherited sign should be displayed on the symbol.The attribute is available for specific symbols based on the element kind. |
| Show Payload | true/false | Specify whether the payload name and sign should be displayed on the connector path.The attribute is available for specific symbols based on the element kind. |
| Text Horizontal Position | LeftCenterRight | Specify the text position for the vertical and horizontal separator symbols. |
| Line Style | SolidDashedDottedetc. | Specify the line style for the vertical and horizontal separator symbols. |
| Is Rounded | true/false | Specify whether the rectangular shape symbol's corners should be rounded. |
| Compartment-based symbol styles (in compartment-based symbol style groups, e.g., Documentation, Objective, Subject, Actors, etc.) |  |  |
| Show Compartment | true/false | Specify whether the compartment should be displayed or suppressed. |
| Show Inherited | true/false | Specify whether the inherited elements should be displayed on the symbol. |
| Show Only Owned | true/false | Specify whether only the owned elements or imported ones, too, should be displayed in the symbol compartment.The attribute is only available for the package symbol and applies to its Members compartment. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can customize the styles of individual symbols. Using the Symbol Style dialog, you can specify style attributes for specific symbols, determining their appearance.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e80d6bd3-c74a-45dd-9c7a-65a10a9ee3ff"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Changing the styles for an individual symbol via the Symbol Style dialog overwrites both predefined and custom model-based symbol styles; the modifications persist even after updating <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Activating style sheets" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[active style sheets]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. To reset, see the <ac:link ac:anchor="Resetting changes made in the Symbol Style dialog" /> procedure.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Opening the Symbol Style dialog</h3><p>To open the Symbol Style dialog</p><hr /><p>Do one of the following:</p><ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Right-click the selected element symbol(s). In the shortcut menu, click <strong>Symbol Style</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="01b07579-7af8-4cc3-aacf-48bc421211e3"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To invoke the Symbol Style dialog for a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Docked symbols" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[docked symbol]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, you must right-click the symbol's header.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Select the element symbol(s) and press <strong>Alt+Enter</strong>.</li></ul><h3>Modifying individual symbol styles</h3><p>To modify individual symbol styles</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the symbol(s) whose styles you want to modify and <ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Symbol Style dialog"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[open the Symbol Style dialog]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>Locate the attribute you want to modify and change its value to the needed one in the Value column.</li><li>Click <strong>Close</strong>.</li></ol><h3>Resetting changes made in the Symbol Style dialog</h3><p>While changing the styles for an individual symbol via the Symbol Style dialog overwrites both predefined and custom model-based symbol styles, you can easily undo them following the procedure below.</p><p>To reset changes made in the Symbol Style dialog</p><hr /><ol><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Symbol Style dialog"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Open the Symbol Style dialog]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>Locate the attribute you want to reset to the style of the currently activated style sheet. Its check box in the <strong>Default </strong>column is deselected.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Resetting_changes.png" /></ac:image></li><li>Select the check box in the <strong>Default </strong>column for the needed attribute.</li><li>Click <strong>Close</strong>.</li></ol><h3>The Symbol Style dialog</h3><p>The table below lists the style attributes available for modification and their descriptions. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2f005f26-68b4-4ee7-9f09-947dee503a50"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="4878db54-cf23-45f9-8497-eb4527ddc56d">Modifying a symbol style changes its <strong>Default </strong>value to <em>false</em>.</li><li data-uuid="c01759d3-49a4-4dda-a32d-c5ed3585566e">You can hide unwanted symbol styles from the Symbol Style dialog for specific element kinds. See the <ac:link ac:anchor="Hiding style attributes from the Symbol Style dialog for specific element kinds"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating style sheet components" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Hiding style attributes from the Symbol Style dialog for specific element kinds]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> procedure on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating style sheet components" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Creating style sheet components]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page. </li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the <strong>Symbol Style</strong> dialog, symbol styles are ordered into the following groups:</span></p><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><strong>Main</strong>: this attribute group displays symbol styles available to the majority of element kinds.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><strong>Compartment-based attributes</strong>: the availability of specific symbol style groups depends on the element kind, as <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Compartments" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[each compartment is only available to specific element kinds]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. Some of the symbol styles available to individual compartment-based symbol style groups may be the same as in the Main group, while others are compartment-specific.</span><ul><li data-uuid="22f52dfa-1cc3-4088-bcec-06710565e3a3"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Compartment-based style groups can be: Documentation, Objective, Subject, Actors, Stakeholders, Include Use Cases, Parts, Ports, Parameters, Attributes, Ends, </span>Satisfy Requirements<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">, Items, Constraints, Require Constraints, Assume Constraints, Assert Constraints, </span>Actions, Perform Actions, Exhibit States, Frames, Enumerations, and Metadata.</li></ul></li></ul><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 74.0672%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 22.4322%;" /><col style="width: 25.7747%;" /><col style="width: 51.7722%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Name</th><th>Value options</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><th colspan="3"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Main</span></th></tr><tr><td>Line Width</td><td>Positive number</td><td>Specify the width of the symbol's outline.</td></tr><tr><td>Fill Color</td><td>Swatches<br />HSV<br />HSL<br />RGB<br />CMYK</td><td><p>Specify the color of the symbol's header.</p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To enable a gradient for this attribute, enable the <ac:link ac:anchor="The View Style dialog"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Modifying styles of individual views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Use Gradient Fill]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> view style attribute.</span></p></td></tr><tr><td>Use Fill Color</td><td>true/false</td><td>Specify whether to use a specific color for the symbol's header.</td></tr><tr><td>Pen Color</td><td><p>Swatches<br />HSV<br />HSL<br />RGB<br />CMYK</p></td><td>Specify the color of the symbol's outline.</td></tr><tr><td>Text Color</td><td>Swatches<br />HSV<br />HSL<br />RGB<br />CMYK</td><td>Specify the color of the symbol's text.</td></tr><tr><td><p>Font</p></td><td><p>Multiple font options<br />Multiple style options (italic, bold, etc.)<br />Multiple size options</p></td><td>Specify the font style of the symbol's text.</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Wrap</span></p></td><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">true/false</span></p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Specify whether the text should wrap to fit the width of the element symbol.</span></td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Compartment Fill Color</span></p></td><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Swatches<br />HSV<br />HSL<br />RGB<br />CMYK</span></p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Specify the color of the symbol's compartments.</span></td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Compartment Text Color</span></p></td><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Swatches<br />HSV<br />HSL<br />RGB<br />CMYK</span></p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Specify the color for the text in the symbol's compartments.</span></td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Show Short Name</span></p></td><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">true/false</span></span></p></td><td><span name]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> should be displayed on the element symbol.</span></td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Show Name </span></p></td><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">true/false</span></span></p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[modifiers]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">(e.g., <em>in</em>, <em>ref</em>, <em>abstract, </em>etc.) </span>should be displayed on the element symbol. The attribute does not control the <em>individual </em>and portion kind </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">(<em>timeslice</em>, <em>snapshot</em>) modifiers.</span></span></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Show Full Text</p></td><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">true/false</span></span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Specify whether element values should be displayed in full or abbreviated. Modifying this attribute affects the values displayed in both the selected symbol's name and its compartments.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5633ec45-d80c-4dc5-b058-5b908b207597"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="153f914e-b902-4550-b735-eeadce7538ec">Modifying this attribute does not affect element values displayed in the metadata compartment. Values in this compartment are always displayed in full.</li><li data-uuid="1a005f85-db54-4a0b-b02d-1d664c444292"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The attribute is available for specific symbols based on the element kind.</span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Content Fill Color</span></td><td><span /></span></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Left<br />Center<br />Right</span></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Specify the text position for the vertical and horizontal separator symbols.</span></p></div></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Line Style<br /></span></td><td><p><span separator symbols.</span></p></div></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Is Rounded<br /></span></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Specify whether the inherited elements should be displayed on the symbol.</span></span></div></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">true/false</span></span></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Specify whether only the owned elements or imported ones, too, should be displayed in the symbol compartment.</span></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fe7a65db-dbe1-4e47-bffb-d5f14279bad0"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The attribute is only available for the package symbol and applies to its Members <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254422722 space=SYSML2P version=3 -->
````
## PAGE 00371: (2026x) Modifying styles of individual views

- page_id: `254422722`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254422722/2026x+Modifying+styles+of+individual+views
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-12-03T10:38:30.111+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Views > (2026x) Symbol styles
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The primary ways to customize the styles of individual views:

- Managing style sheets for the specific view.
- Managing style properties for the specific view via the View Style dialog.

Changing the styles for an individual view via the View Style dialog overwrites both predefined and custom model-based style sheets; the modifications persist even after updating [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Activating style sheets' space='']. To reset, see the procedure.

##### Managing style sheets for the specific view

- For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Activating style sheets' space=''] procedures on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Activating style sheets' space=''] page.

##### Managing style properties for the specific view via the View Style dialog

###### Opening the View Style dialog

To open the View Style dialog

- Right-click the selected view. In the shortcut menu, click **View Style**.

###### Modifying an individual view's style

To modify an individual view's style

1. Select the view(s) whose styles you want to modify and .
2. Locate the property you want to modify and change its value to the needed one in the Value column.
3. Click Close .

###### Resetting changes made in the View Style dialog

While changing the styles for an individual view via the View Style dialog overwrites both predefined and custom model-based style sheets, you can easily undo them following the procedure below.

To reset changes made in the View Style dialog

1. .
2. Locate the property you want to reset to the style of the currently activated style sheet. Its check box in the Default column is deselected.
3. Select the check box in the Default column for the needed property.
4. Click Close .

###### The View Style dialog

The table below lists the style properties available for modification and their descriptions.

Modifying a view style property changes its **Default**value to *false*.

Currently, other view style properties displayed in the dialog besides the **Use Gradient Fill**are not functional.

| Name | Value options | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Main |  |  |
| Use Gradient Fill | true/false | Specify whether a gradient should be used for the Fill Color and Content Fill Color . |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The primary ways to customize the styles of individual views:</p><ul><li data-uuid="7abab438-c72a-4ce9-9b23-bb47baf2467c">Managing style sheets for the specific view.</li><li data-uuid="0285e7ca-3e83-4160-8780-397882db4d9d">Managing style properties for the specific view via the <strong>View Style </strong>dialog.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e80d6bd3-c74a-45dd-9c7a-65a10a9ee3ff"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Changing the styles for an individual view via the View Style dialog overwrites both predefined and custom model-based style sheets; the modifications persist even after updating <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Activating style sheets" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[active style sheets]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. To reset, see the <ac:link ac:anchor="Resetting changes made in the View Style dialog" /> procedure. </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Managing style sheets for the specific view</h3><ul><li data-uuid="938edd71-48e6-406d-b931-1ed573cc5ee1">For more information, see the <ac:link ac:anchor="Activating style sheets for a specific view"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Activating style sheets" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Activating style sheets for a specific view]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> procedures on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Activating style sheets" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Activating style sheets]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</li></ul><h3>Managing style properties for the specific view via the View Style dialog</h3><h4>Opening the View Style dialog</h4><p>To open the View Style dialog</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="6f839a2b-f557-4b9b-a825-aef531068d51"><p class="auto-cursor-target">Right-click the selected view. In the shortcut menu, click <strong>View Style</strong>.</p></li></ul><h4>Modifying an individual view's style</h4><p>To modify an individual view's style</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="91062e4c-0440-47ed-8a32-febc95f40303">Select the view(s) whose styles you want to modify and <ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the View Style dialog"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[open the View Style dialog]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li data-uuid="d5268c0f-3ece-4014-a7f3-28a7d3af21cc">Locate the property you want to modify and change its value to the needed one in the Value column.</li><li data-uuid="32d56453-b2f3-442d-b929-d3a20f13581b">Click <strong>Close</strong>.</li></ol><h4>Resetting changes made in the View Style dialog</h4><p>While changing the styles for an individual view via the View Style dialog overwrites both predefined and custom model-based style sheets, you can easily undo them following the procedure below.</p><p>To reset changes made in the View Style dialog</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="7f8ce54d-499f-4145-8c5c-98784a51c798"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the View Style dialog"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Open the View Style dialog]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li data-uuid="3903f485-776d-4cd4-bf2d-6d37263b0599">Locate the property you want to reset to the style of the currently activated style sheet. Its check box in the <strong>Default </strong>column is deselected.</li><li data-uuid="bfdd7237-bda4-47c1-9300-2665a1f0b640">Select the check box in the <strong>Default </strong>column for the needed property.</li><li data-uuid="7b4b7649-5559-4404-b700-7d763d82f3fa">Click <strong>Close</strong>.</li></ol><h4>The View Style dialog</h4><p>The table below lists the style properties available for modification and their descriptions. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2f005f26-68b4-4ee7-9f09-947dee503a50"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Modifying a view style property changes its <strong>Default </strong>value to <em>false</em>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c2ab1c2c-2c25-43f4-a19e-041fe1bab07e"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Currently, other view style properties displayed in the dialog besides the <strong>Use Gradient Fill </strong>are not functional.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 74.0672%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 22.4322%;" /><col style="width: 25.7747%;" /><col style="width: 51.7722%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Name</th><th>Value options</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><th colspan="3"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Main</span></th></tr><tr><td>Use Gradient Fill</td><td>true/false</td><td><p>Specify whether a gradient should be used for the Fill Color and Content Fill Color <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Modifying styles of individual symbols" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[symbol style properties]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254423124 space=SYSML2P version=4 -->
## PAGE 00372: (2026x) Modifying tabular view cells

- page_id: `254423124`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254423124/2026x+Modifying+tabular+view+cells
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2026-01-15T18:37:59.593+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Views > (2026x) Creating views > (2026x) Creating tabular views > (2026x) Tabular view columns
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Tabular view columns' space=''] cells are the intersections of every row and column, which represent element data values. The represented data values depend on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Exposing elements for views' space=''] elements for the tabular view.

Tabular view column cells are modeled via the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Modeling tabular view columns' space=''].

##### Modifying tabular view column cells

###### Modifying cell data via the tabular view

Only the String, Element, Enum, Integer, Boolean, and Real cell values can be edited in the table view's diagrammatic representation. This can only be done for *[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Tabular view columns' space='']* column cells.

To modify cell data via the tabular view

1. In a tabular view, click the cell whose data you want to modify.
2. Specify the new value.
3. Click elsewhere on the table for the changes to be saved.

###### Tabular view cell errors

You may encounter the following icons and buttons in tabular view column cells:

| Icon / Button | Message | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | Click to load the cell. | Cell loading was canceled after exceeding 1000 ms. Click the button to restart loading; the modeling tool will wait until the cell has finished loading.If the table is only partially loaded and its data is modified (e.g., via the Containment tree, Textual Editor, etc.) without refreshing the table afterward, the icon is displayed in the cells that were not loaded. In this case, the cell cannot be reloaded; click the Refresh button in the table toolbar to refresh the entire table. |
|  | Failed to evaluate expression. | Resolve model errors related to the column expression. The error may also appear if the expression specified for the tabular view column and/or cell does not apply to the specific element. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Tabular view columns" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Tabular view column]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> cells are the intersections of every row and column, which represent element data values. The represented data values depend on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Exposing elements for views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[exposed]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> elements for the tabular view.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cd7a175f-a7bd-4bd8-936d-aecaa5791272"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Tabular view column cells are modeled via the <ac:link ac:anchor="Adding table columns"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Modeling tabular view columns" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[tabular view column modeling]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Modifying tabular view column cells</h3><h4>Modifying cell data via the tabular view</h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a2c60a5b-54fe-4ebd-aaed-9b1aa9d26e6f"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>Only the String, Element, Enum, Integer, Boolean, and Real cell values can be edited in the table view's diagrammatic representation. This can only be done for <span><em><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Tabular view columns" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ColumnByFeatureView]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></em> column cells</span>.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To modify cell data via the tabular view</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="a7993a2e-c033-4f5b-8398-8cacbfe9738f">In a tabular view, click the cell whose data you want to modify.</li><li data-uuid="6b131bcc-1dc1-4258-a1f9-01faf2ca9a2f">Specify the new value.</li><li data-uuid="c7cc8032-6344-4847-9c23-03f590e4e89a">Click elsewhere on the table for the changes to be saved.</li></ol><h4>Tabular view cell errors</h4><p>You may encounter the following icons and buttons in tabular view column cells:</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th scope="col">Icon / Button</th><th scope="col">Message</th><th scope="col">Description</th></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Click to load the cell icon.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Click to load the cell.</td><td><p>Cell loading was canceled after exceeding 1000 ms. Click the button to restart loading; the modeling tool will wait until the cell has finished loading.</p><p>If the table is only partially loaded and its data is modified (e.g., via the Containment tree, Textual Editor, etc.) without refreshing the table afterward, the icon is displayed in the cells that were not loaded. In this case, the cell cannot be reloaded; click the <strong>Refresh</strong> button in the table toolbar to refresh the entire table. </p></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="20"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Failed to evaluate expression icon.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Failed to evaluate expression.</td><td>Resolve model errors related to the column expression. The error may also appear if the expression specified for the tabular view column and/or cell does not apply to the specific element.</td></tr></tbody></table>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254422813 space=SYSML2P version=3 -->
## PAGE 00373: (2026x) Multiplicity

- page_id: `254422813`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254422813/2026x+Multiplicity
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-10-14T15:13:26.527+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Element management > (2026x) Working with elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Usages can have a multiplicity specifying how many values they can have for the instances of theirfeaturing definition or usage.The multiplicity is indicated as a range of natural numbers in square brackets, e.g., [lowerBound..upperBound].

- The lower bound must be finite. If the lower bound is unspecified, then it is taken to be equal to the upper bound, except when the upper bound is *, in which case it is treated as 0.
- The upper bound must be equal to or greater than the lower bound number. It can be unbounded, indicated by the * sign.

If a multiplicity is not declared for a usage, then the usage inherits the multiplicity constraints of any other usages it subsets or redefines. If no tighter constraint is inherited, the effective default is the most general multiplicity [0..*]. However, a tighter default of [1..1] is implicitly declared for the usage if all of the following conditions hold:

- The usage is an attribute usage, an item usage (including a part usage, except if it is a connection usage), or a port usage.
- The usage is owned by a definition or another usage (not a package).
- The usage does not have any explicit owned subsettings or owned redefinitions.

The following keywords can also be used after the multiplicity or in place of it:

- *nonunique*– a non-unique usage indicates that the same usage value can appear more than once. Usages are unique by default, i.e., two usage values can be the same.
- *ordered*– an ordered usage indicates that the usage values can be placed in an order from 1 to the number of values. The features are unordered by default.

For information on cross multiplicity, which is used only within the context of cross features and is usually specified for the*end*elements of a connector, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Cross features' space='']page.

##### Specifying usage multiplicity

###### Specifying usage multiplicity via the Textual Editor

To specify usage multiplicity via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, follow the part name with brackets.
2. Specify a range of natural numbers in square brackets, e.g., [lowerBound..upperBound] .
3. Click the Synchronize button.

partbodyAssy:BodyAssy{ 
partdoors[2..4] :Door; 
partbody; 
partsunroof[0..1] :Sunroof; 
}

###### Specifying usage multiplicity via the Specification panel

To specify usage multiplicity via the Specification panel

- See the procedure [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification panel' space=''] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification panel' space=''] page.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254422344 space=SYSML2P version=4 -->
## PAGE 00374: (2026x) N-ary connection

- page_id: `254422344`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254422344/2026x+N-ary+connection
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2025-12-03T08:54:40.208+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Structure > (2026x) Connection
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An n-ary connection is a complex connector between three or more elements. The n-ary connection is displayed as a dot symbol with segments connecting the ends of the connection.

The kind keyword (e.g., *part*) is optional for connection ends. The keyword is mandatory if the ends have owned [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Cross features' space=''].

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Creating n-ary connections

###### Creating n-ary connections via the Textual Editor

To create n-ary connections via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the connection keyword.
2. Specify the element name (optional), (optional), then the connect keyword, then state the features explicitly (using the connect...to syntax ) or in a comma-separated list of related features. If the connection has no name and no specialization, the keyword *connection*can be omitted.You can also name connection ends, associating them to features via[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specializations' space=''].
3. Click the Synchronize button.

connectiondefCanBus{ 
endparttransmission:Transmission; 
endpartelectricalPowerController:ElectricalPowerController; 
endpartinternalCombustionEngine:InternalCombustionEngine; 
}

part'power subsystem'{ 
partelectricalPowerController; 
parttransmission; 
partinternalCombustionEngine;

// named specialized (feature typing) connection usage with named endsconnecting features in a comma-separated list: 
connection'can bus':CanBusconnect(t::>transmission,epc::>electricalPowerController,ice::>internalCombustionEngine);

// unnamed unspecialized connection usage connecting featuresin a comma-separated list: 
connect(transmission,electricalPowerController,internalCombustionEngine);}

###### Creating n-ary connections via the view palette

To create n-ary connections via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click the **n-ary connection** button. If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to the **connection** or the **connection****def**button and click the **n-ary connection** button in the dropdown menu.
2. Click in the view pane where you want the n-ary connection symbol to be created.
3. Select the n-ary connection symbol and in its smart manipulator, click the end command.
4. Draw the end from the n-ary connection symbol to the symbol of the element you want to connect. Repeat for all elements you want to connect.

##### Converting binary and n-ary connectors

###### Converting binary connectors to n-ary via the view

To convert binary connectors to n-ary via the view

1. In the view pane, right-click the binary connector you want to convert to an n-ary.
2. In the shortcut menu, click Refactor , then Convert to N-ary Dot . The binary connector is converted and the n-ary dot symbol is displayed.

###### Converting n-aryconnectorstobinaryvia the view

To convert n-ary connectors to binary via the view

1. In the view pane, right-click the n-ary dot symbol of the connector you want to convert to a binary.
2. In the shortcut menu, click Refactor , then Convert to Path . The n-ary connector dot symbol is converted and the binary path is displayed. The **Convert to Path**command is only available if the connector has no more than two ends.

##### Creating an n-ary connection definition

###### Creating n-ary connection definitions via the Textual Editor

To create n-ary connection definitions via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the connection def keyword.
2. Name the element, then specify the connection definition features in the element body:
  1. Specify the connection definition ends by declaring the end keyword, followed by the end name and/or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specializations' space=''] of the needed element.
3. Click the **Synchronize**button. connectiondefCanBus{ 
endparttransmission:Transmission; 
endpartelectricalPowerController:ElectricalPowerController; 
endpartinternalCombustionEngine:InternalCombustionEngine; 
}

###### Auto-creating n-ary connection definition elements via the Extract Definition command

To auto-create n-ary connection definition elements via the Extract Definition command

- See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Extract Definition and Usage' space='']page.

###### Creating n-ary connection definitions via the view palette

To create n-ary connection definitions via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click the**connection def** button. If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to either the **connection**or the **n-ary connection******button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.
2. Click in the view pane where you want to create the connection def symbol. The connection def symbol is created containing two end element symbols.
  1. Create additional ends via the Create Element button's end command. See the procedure [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Compartments' space=''] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Compartments' space=''] page.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">An n-ary connection is a complex connector between three or more elements. The n-ary connection is displayed as a dot symbol with segments connecting the ends of the connection.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f4624f90-33ce-4490-9077-d120e7335029"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The kind keyword (e.g., <em>part</em>) is optional for connection ends. The keyword is mandatory if the ends have owned <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Cross features" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[cross features]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="n-ary_connections.png" /></ac:image></p><h3>Creating n-ary connections</h3><h4><span>Creating n-ary connections via the Textual Editor</span></h4><p>To create n-ary connections via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the <em>connection</em><em> </em><span>keyword.</span></li><li>Specify the element name (optional),<span> </span><ac:link><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[specialization]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span> </span>(optional), then the <em>connect </em>keyword, then state the features explicitly (using the <em>connect...to </em>syntax<span>) </span>or in a comma-separated list of related features.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c8e09d36-c69a-43b8-8818-03ae38dc91f2"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>If the connection has no name and no specialization, the keyword <em>connection<span> </span></em>can be omitted.</li><li>You can also name connection ends, <span>associating them to features via </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Specializations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[reference subsetting]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span>.</span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Click the <strong>Synchronize </strong>button.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fdacc45a-201e-4138-96ea-ae8a7aff5659"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span connection definitions via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the <em>connection def</em> keyword.</li><li>Name the element, then specify the connection definition features in the element body:<ol><li>Specify the connection definition ends by declaring the <em>end</em> keyword, followed by <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254422378 space=SYSML2P version=4 -->
````
## PAGE 00375: (2026x) N-ary interface

- page_id: `254422378`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254422378/2026x+N-ary+interface
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2025-12-03T08:55:17.892+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Structure > (2026x) Interface
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An n-ary interface is a complex connector between three or more ports. The n-ary interface is displayed as a dot symbol with segments connecting the ends of the interface.

- The ends of an interface definition or usage must be port usages.
- The port keyword is optional for interface ends. The keyword is mandatory if the ends have owned [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Cross features' space=''] .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Creating n-ary interfaces

###### Creating n-ary interfaces via the Textual Editor

To create n-ary interfaces via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the interface keyword.
2. Specify the element name (optional), [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specializations' space=''] (optional), then the connect keyword, then state the feature ports explicitly (using the connect...to syntax ) or in a comma-separated list of related features. If the interface has no name and no specialization, the keyword *connect*can be omitted.You can also name interface end ports, associating them to feature ports via[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Reference subsetting' space=''].
3. Click the Synchronize button.

interfacedefCanBusInterface{endporttransmissionPort:CanBusPorts; 
endportelectricalPowerControllerPort:CanBusPorts; 
endportinternalCombustionEnginePort:CanBusPorts; 
}

part'power subsystem'{ 
partelectricalPowerController{portfp;} 
parttransmission{portfp;} 
partinternalCombustionEngine{portfp;}

//named specialized (feature typing) interface usage with named end portsconnecting feature portsin a comma-separated list:interface'can bus':CanBusInterfaceconnect(transmissionPort::>transmission.fp,electricalPowerControllerPort::>electricalPowerController.fp,internalCombustionEnginePort::>internalCombustionEngine.fp);

// unnamed unspecialized interface usage connecting feature portsin a comma-separated list:interface(transmission.fp,electricalPowerController.fp,internalCombustionEngine.fp);}

###### Creating n-ary interfaces via the view palette

To create n-ary interfaces via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click the n-ary interface button. If it is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to the interface button and click the n-ary interface button in the dropdown menu.
2. Click in the view pane where you want the n-ary interface symbol to be created.
3. Select the n-ary interface symbol and in its smart manipulator, click the end command.
4. Draw the end from the n-ary interface symbol to the symbol of the port you want to connect. Repeat for all ports you want to connect.

##### Converting binary and n-ary interfaces

###### Converting binary interface to n-ary via the view

To convert binary interface to n-ary via the view

1. In the view pane, right-click the binary interface you want to convert to an n-ary.
2. In the shortcut menu, click Refactor , then Convert to N-ary Dot . The binary interface is converted and the n-ary dot symbol is displayed.

###### Converting n-aryinterfacetobinaryvia the view

To convert n-ary interface to binary via the view

1. In the view pane, right-click the n-ary dot symbol of the interface you want to convert to a binary.
2. In the shortcut menu, click Refactor , then Convert to Path . The n-ary interface dot symbol is converted and the binary path is displayed. The **Convert to Path**command is only available if the dependency has no more than two ends.

##### Creating n-ary interface definition

###### Creating n-ary interfaces definition via the Textual Editor

To create n-ary interfaces definition via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the interface def keyword followed by its name.
2. Name the element, then specify the interface definition features in the element body:
  1. Specify the interface definition ends by declaring the end keyword, followed by the end name and/or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specializations' space=''] of the needed port.
3. Click the **Synchronize**button. interfacedefCanBusInterface{endporttransmissionPort:CanBusPorts; 
endportelectricalPowerControllerPort:CanBusPorts; 
endportinternalCombustionEnginePort:CanBusPorts; 
}

###### Auto-creating n-ary interfacedefinition elements via the Extract Definition command

To auto-create n-ary interface definition elements via the Extract Definition command

- See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Extract Definition and Usage' space='']page.

###### Creating n-ary interface definitions via the view palette

To create n-ary interface definitions via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click the**interface def** button. If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to either the **interface**or the **n-ary interface**button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.
2. Click in the view pane where you want to create the interface def symbol. The interface def symbol is created containing two end element symbols.
  1. Create additional ends:
    1. Via the Create Element button's end command. See the procedure [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Compartments' space=''] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Compartments' space=''] page.
    2. In the view palette, under the Items/Ports/Attributes group, click the port button. Click on the interface def symbol to create a port for it.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2355d5e1-cbe0-4a8a-b013-3e88479219d8"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">An n-ary interface is a complex connector between three or more ports. The n-ary interface is displayed as a dot symbol with segments connecting the ends of the interface.</span></ac:inline-comment-marker></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c2f2972b-2429-464d-9553-0401e35800cc"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="0cd106ae-0c9d-4643-9d42-f5db7aed3b21">The ends of an interface definition or usage must be port usages.</li><li data-uuid="60b055b1-889f-4a77-afb6-6e9ad045e7b9">The <em>port</em> keyword is optional for interface ends. The keyword is mandatory if the ends have owned <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Cross features" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[cross features]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="n-ary_interface.png" /></ac:image></p><h3>Creating n-ary interfaces </h3><h4><span>Creating n-ary interfaces via the Textual Editor</span></h4><p>To create n-ary interfaces via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the <em>interface</em><em> </em><span>keyword.</span></li><li>Specify the element name (optional),<span> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Specializations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[specialization]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span> </span>(optional), then the <em>connect </em>keyword, then state the feature ports explicitly (using the <em>connect...to </em>syntax<span>) </span>or in a comma-separated list of related features.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a49f0da9-50d0-4d38-836b-c10c88756e75"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>If the interface has no name and no specialization, the keyword <em>connect<span> </span></em>can be omitted.</li><li>You can also name interface end ports, <span>associating them to feature ports via </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Reference subsetting" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[reference subsetting]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span>.</span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Click the <strong>Synchronize </strong>button.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5881b76a-1f9e-4ff4-a3f0-bed25db0873b"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Creating n-ary interfaces via the view palette</h4><p>To create n-ary interfaces via the view palette</p><hr /><ol><li>In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click the <strong>n-ary interface</strong> button. If it is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to the <strong>interface </strong>button and click the <strong>n-ary interface </strong>button in the dropdown menu.</li><li>Click in the view pane where you want the n-ary interface symbol to be created.</li><li>Select the n-ary interface symbol and in its smart manipulator, click the <strong>end </strong>command. </li><li>Draw the end from the n-ary interface symbol to the symbol of the port you want to connect. Repeat for all ports you want to connect.</li></ol><h3>Converting binary and n-ary interfaces</h3><h4>Converting binary interface to n-ary via the view</h4><p>To convert binary interface to n-ary via the view</p><hr /><ol><li>In the view pane, right-click the binary interface you want to convert to an n-ary.</li><li>In the shortcut menu, click <strong>Refactor</strong>, then <strong>Convert to N-ary Dot</strong>. The binary interface is converted and the n-ary dot symbol is displayed.</li></ol><h4>Converting <span>n-ary </span><span>interface </span><span>to </span><span>binary </span><span>via the view</span></h4><p>To convert n-ary interface to binary via the view</p><hr /><ol><li>In the view pane, right-click the n-ary dot symbol of the interface you want to convert to a binary.</li><li>In the shortcut menu, click <strong>Refactor</strong>, then <strong>Convert to Path</strong>. The n-ary interface dot symbol is converted and the binary path is displayed.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0dcc5e6d-b75e-4dc3-9a76-b8575964a7c5"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <strong>Convert to Path </strong>command is only available if the dependency has no more than two ends. </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h3>Creating n-ary interface definition</h3><h4>Creating n-ary interfaces definition via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To create n-ary interfaces definition via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the <em>interface def</em> keyword followed by its name.</li><li>Name the element, then specify the interface definition features in the element body:<ol><li>Specify the interface definition ends by declaring the <em>end</em> keyword, followed by the end name and/or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Specializations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[specialization]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> of the needed port.</li></ol></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span>Click the <strong>Synchronize</strong></span><span> button. </span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="802b05e2-76b4-4c33-bb2a-fa228b066a7c"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">interface<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span>def </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CanBusInterface</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">end<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">port</span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">transmissionPort</span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CanBusPorts</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   </span>end<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">port</span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">electricalPowerControllerPort </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">: </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CanBusPorts</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   </span>end<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">port</span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">internalCombustionEnginePort</span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CanBusPorts</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h4>Auto-creating <span>n-ary interface </span>definition elements via the Extract Definition command</h4><p><span>To auto-create n-ary interface definition elements via the Extract Definition command</span></p><hr /><ul><li><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Extract Definition and Usage" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Extract Definition and Usage]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">page.</span></span></li></ul><h4>Creating n-ary interface definitions via the view palette</h4><p>To create n-ary interface definitions via the view palette</p><hr /><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click the<strong> interface def</strong> button. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="769c3a3a-7b27-471d-b151-165748c95ab6"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to either the <span><strong>interface </strong>or the <strong>n-ary interface </strong></span>button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Click in the view pane where you want to create the interface def symbol. The interface def symbol is created containing two <strong>end </strong>element symbols.<ol><li data-uuid="c08d6faa-201e-42d6-af51-9f20bd1e4288">Create additional ends:<ol><li data-uuid="7a64217e-9f55-4afd-8470-2e739d6f2e35">Via the <strong>Create Element </strong>button's <strong>end </strong>command. See the procedure<span> </span><ac:link ac:anchor="Creating elements via compartments"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Compartments" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Creating elements via compartments]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span> </span>on the<span> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Compartments" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Compartments]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span> </span>page. </li><li data-uuid="39d6b358-66ce-4d79-a18c-5d49d4e43a4f">In the view palette, under the Items/Ports/Attributes group, click the<strong> port</strong> button. Click on the interface def symbol to create a port for it.</li></ol></li></ol></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254422948 space=SYSML2P version=5 -->
## PAGE 00376: (2026x) Occurrences

- page_id: `254422948`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254422948/2026x+Occurrences
- version_number: 5
- version_date: `2026-07-01T14:57:19.920+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Common elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An occurrence is used to designate something that has an extent in time, which is the occurrence's lifetime. Occurrences can be structural (parts, items) and behavioral (actions).

##### Creating occurrence usage and definition elements

###### Creating occurrence usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor

To create occurrence usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare one of the following keywords:
  1. *occurrence def*for an occurrence definition.
  2. occurrence for an occurrence usage.
2. Name the element.
3. (Optional) Specify the occurrence features in the element body.
4. Click the Synchronize button. occurrencedefCruiseControlInteraction;// occurrence definition 
occurrencecruiseController;// occurrence usage

###### Creating occurrence usage and definition elements via the Create Element command

To create occurrence usage and definition elements via the Create Element command

1. In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click **Create Element**.
2. In the Create Element dialog, select one of the following:
  1. occurrence def to create an occurrence definition element.
  2. occurrence to create an occurrence usage element.
3. Name the element.
4. (Optional) Specify the occurrence features via the Textual Editor.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>An occurrence is used to designate something that has an extent in time, which is the occurrence's lifetime. Occurrences can be structural (parts, items) and behavioral (actions). </p><h3>Creating occurrence usage and definition elements</h3><h4>Creating occurrence usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To create occurrence usage and definition elements via the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Textual Editor</span></p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="1371ba61-71fc-4240-a5dc-1a46d34d79f2">In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare one of the following keywords:<ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span> </span><em>occurrence def</em><span> </span>for an occurrence definition.</p></li><li><span> </span><em>occurrence </em>for an occurrence usage.</li></ol></li><li data-uuid="323deaed-b300-4159-bdc5-a6f0cccdf297">Name the element.</li><li data-uuid="edd606ae-5d7a-4562-a501-9249c96e9f3a">(Optional) Specify the occurrence features in the element body.</li><li data-uuid="6dbaf4c1-21c7-4d8c-a6ba-195f552c3527">Click the <strong>Synchronize </strong>button.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="03f18383-07ac-491a-8792-cb0c1f4eda9a"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">occurrence</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CruiseControlInteraction</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;  </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// occurrence definition</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">occurrence</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">cruiseController</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;              </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// occurrence usage</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h4>Creating occurrence usage and definition elements via the Create Element command</h4><p>To create occurrence usage and definition elements via the Create Element command</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="48d68334-5b20-4d97-8689-09fc2db69ae9"><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click <strong>Create Element</strong>.</p></li><li data-uuid="9977f5bc-2ba3-410b-b001-13f7ff926f3f">In the <strong>Create Element</strong> dialog, select one of the following:<ol><li><strong>occurrence def</strong> to create an occurrence definition element.</li><li><strong>occurrence </strong>to create an occurrence usage element.</li></ol></li><li data-uuid="b0cb5a26-8238-46e3-994d-fb07cd715bf6">Name the element.</li><li data-uuid="4b9754c4-c571-4658-9eae-a4e4bf33b21e">(Optional) Specify the occurrence features via the Textual Editor.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=312247298 space=SYSML2P version=5 -->
## PAGE 00377: (2026x) Opening used projects

- page_id: `312247298`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/312247298/2026x+Opening+used+projects
- version_number: 5
- version_date: `2026-06-10T11:53:08.867+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Project management > (2026x) Working with projects > (2026x) Managing local used projects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### EXPAND: Click here to expand the documentation for the 2026x version...

Click here to expand the documentation for the 2026x version...

Projects can only use other projects in read-only mode. If you want to edit a used project, you must open it as a stand-alone. You can do this from within the main project in which the project is used by following the procedures listed below.

###### Opening a used project via the Containment tree 2026xOpening a used project via the Containment tree

To open a used project via the Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, expand the Used Projects node.
2. Right-click a used project and select one of the following:
  1. Open This Version .
  2. Open Latest Version . The used project is opened.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Opening a used project via the Project Usages dialog 2026xOpening a used project via the Project Usages dialog

To open a used project via the Project Usages dialog

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local used projects' space='']Open the **Project Usages**dialog .
2. In the Project Usages dialog, select a used project, then on the right side section of the dialog, click the Open button.
3. Click the OK button to close the Project Usages dialog. The used project is opened. [ATTACHMENT filename='Opening a used project via the Project Usages dialog 2026x.png']

#### NOTE: 2026x HotFix1 version

2026x HotFix1 version

This documentation page is for the 2026x HotFix1 version of the SysML v2 Plugin. If you are working with the 2026x version, expand the section above. However, we highly advise upgrading to the 2026x HotFix1 version.

Projects can only use other projects in read-only mode. If you want to edit a used project, you must open it as a stand-alone. You can do this from within the main project in which the project is used by following the procedures listed below.

###### Opening a used project via the Containment tree

To open a used project via the Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, expand the Used Projects node.
2. Right-click a used project and click Open Latest Version . The used project is opened. [ATTACHMENT filename='Opening a used project via the Containment tree.png']

###### Opening a used project via the Project Usages dialog

To open a used project via the Project Usages dialog

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local used projects' space='']Open the **Project Usages**dialog .
2. In the Project Usages dialog, select a used project, then on the right side section of the dialog, click the Open Latest Version button.
3. Click the OK button to close the Project Usages dialog. The used project is opened. [ATTACHMENT filename='Opening a used project via the Project Usages dialog.png']
