<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254423176 space=SYSML2P version=3 -->
## PAGE 00378: (2026x) Opening views

- page_id: `254423176`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254423176/2026x+Opening+views
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-11-10T18:04:10.896+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Views
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

See the procedures on this page for opening views.

##### Opening views

###### Opening views via the Containment tree

Opening vews via the Containment tree

1. Select the view element in the Containment tree and do one of the following:
  1. Double-click the view element.
  2. Right-click the view element and in the shortcut menu, click Open or Open in New Tab .
  3. On the keyboard, press Ctrl+Enter or Enter . **Open**is equivalent to **Ctrl+Enter**. The commands open the selected view in place of the currently active view.**Open in New Tab**is equivalent to **Enter**. The commands open the selected view in a new view tab.

###### Opening views via the Textual Editor

Opening views via the Textual Editor

- In the Textual Editor, right-click the view you want to open and in the shortcut menu, click Open View .

###### Opening views via the view element symbol in a view

Opening views via the view element symbol in a view

1. Select the view element symbol in a view and do one of the following:
  1. Double-click the view element symbol.
  2. Right-click the view element symbol and in the shortcut menu, click Open or Open in New Tab .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>See the procedures on this page for opening views.</p><h3>Opening views</h3><h4>Opening views via the Containment tree</h4><p>Opening vews via the Containment tree</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the view element in the Containment tree and do one of the following:<ol><li>Double-click the view element.</li><li>Right-click the view element and in the shortcut menu, click <strong>Open</strong> or <strong>Open in New Tab</strong>.</li><li>On the keyboard, press <strong>Ctrl+Enter </strong>or <strong>Enter</strong>. <br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6392ba39-8ce6-4a63-97fc-6dc431efd7dd"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><strong>Open </strong>is equivalent to <strong>Ctrl+Enter</strong>. The commands open the selected view in place of the currently active view. </li><li><strong>Open in New Tab </strong>is equivalent to <strong>Enter</strong>. The commands open the selected view in a new view tab.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></li></ol><h4>Opening views via the Textual Editor</h4><p>Opening views via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="ededb690-d68f-49f7-af40-8046bad289af">In the Textual Editor, right-click the view you want to open and in the shortcut menu, click <strong>Open View</strong>.</li></ul><h4>Opening views via the view element symbol in a view</h4><p>Opening views via the view element symbol in a view</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the view element symbol in a view and do one of the following:<ol><li>Double-click the view element symbol.</li><li>Right-click the view element symbol and in the shortcut menu, click <strong>Open</strong> or <strong>Open in New Tab</strong>.</li></ol></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254422930 space=SYSML2P version=7 -->
## PAGE 00379: (2026x) Package

- page_id: `254422930`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254422930/2026x+Package
- version_number: 7
- version_date: `2025-12-03T08:48:55.959+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Common elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Packages are namespaces that contain member elements that may be owned or unowned. It is used as a container for organizing other elements within the model. For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Key concepts' space=''] page.Additionally, a package can import members of another package as unowned members. You can also filter imported elements based on certain conditions.

##### Creating package elements

###### Creating a package element via the Textual Editor

To create a package element via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the keyword:
  1. *package*.
  2. *library package* .
2. Specify the element name.
3. (Optional) .
4. Click the Synchronize button. library package Policies; // library package elementpackage'Vehicle Features'{ // package element 
privateimportPolicies::**;filter@SysML::Systems::PartUsage;}

###### Creating a package element via the Create Element command

To create a package element via the Create Element command

1. In the Containment tree, right-click a root namespace, and in the shortcut menu, click Create Element .
2. In the Create Element dialog, select one of the following:
  1. package to create a package element.
  2. library package to create a library package element.
3. Name the element.
4. (Optional) .

##### Specifying packages

###### Importing elements to packages

An import relationship allows one package to import memberships from another package. The member elements from imported memberships become (unowned) members of the importing package in addition to being members of the imported package. In particular, this allows members of the imported package to be referenced in textual notations within the scope of the importing package without having to qualify the member names with the name of the imported package.

- For more information and procedures, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Importing and filtering elements' space=''] procedure on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Importing and filtering elements' space=''] page.

###### Specifying element import filters

Packages have the capability to filter elements upon import based on certain conditions, usually defined in terms of the metadata provided by annotations of those elements. Only elements that meet all filter conditions become imported members of the packages.

- For more information and procedures, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Importing and filtering elements' space=''] section on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Importing and filtering elements' space=''] page.

###### Package members display in symbolic views

- To display package members in the Members compartment of the package symbol, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Compartments' space=''].
- To control whether the Members compartment displays only owned members or imported ones as well, use the **Show Only Owned** [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Modifying styles of individual symbols' space=''].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Packages are namespaces that contain member elements that may be owned or unowned. It is used as a container for organizing other elements within the model. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">For more information, see the <ac:link ac:anchor="Package"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Key concepts" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Key concepts]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page. </span>Additionally, a package can import members of another package as unowned members.  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// library package element</span>  </span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">'Vehicle Features'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span> </span>{  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// package element</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   <span> </span></span>private<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span> </span></span>import<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Policies</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">::**; <br /></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   <span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">filter</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span> </span>@</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">SysML::Systems::PartUsage</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;<span> <br /></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">} </span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h4>Creating a package element via the Create Element command</h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To create a package element via the Create Element command</span></p><hr /><ol><li>In the Containment tree, right-click a root namespace, and in the shortcut menu, click <strong>Create Element</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Create Element</strong> dialog, select one of the following:<ol><li><strong>package </strong>to create a package element.</li><li><strong>library package </strong>to create a library package element.</li></ol></li><li>Name the element.</li><li>(Optional) <ac:link ac:anchor="Specifying packages"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specify the package]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li></ol><h3 class="auto-cursor-target">Specifying packages</h3><h4>Importing elements to packages</h4><p><span>An import relationship allows one package to import memberships from another package. The member elements from imported memberships become (unowned) members of the importing package in addition to being members of the imported package. In particular, this allows members of the imported package to be referenced in textual notations within the scope of the importing package without having to qualify the member names with the name of the imported package. </span></p><ul><li><span>For more information and procedures, see the </span><ac:link ac:anchor="Importing elements"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Importing and filtering elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Importing elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> procedure on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Importing and filtering elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Importing and filtering elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</li></ul><h4>Specifying element import filters </h4><p>Packages have the capability to filter elements upon import based on certain conditions, usually defined in terms of the metadata provided by annotations of those elements. Only elements that meet all filter conditions become imported members of the packages. </p><ul><li><span>For more information and procedures, see the <ac:link ac:anchor="Element import filtering"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Importing and filtering elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Element import filtering]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> section on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Importing and filtering elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Importing and filtering elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</span></li></ul><h4><span>Package members display in symbolic views</span></h4><ul><li data-uuid="7d57743d-bdc0-4eed-8626-602323185313"><span>To display package members in the Members compartment of the package symbol, <ac:link ac:anchor="Displaying and suppressing compartments"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Compartments" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[display the compartment]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li data-uuid="0f7b1a13-9ae9-458c-9368-ceec5c91e2c7"><span>To control whether the Members compartment displays only owned members or imported ones as well, use the <strong>Show Only Owned</strong> <ac:link ac:anchor="The Symbol Style dialog"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Modifying styles of individual symbols" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[symbol style]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li></ul><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254422583 space=SYSML2P version=2 -->
## PAGE 00380: (2026x) Parallel states

- page_id: `254422583`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254422583/2026x+Parallel+states
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-10-14T15:23:07.138+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Behavior > (2026x) States
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A state definition or usage may hierarchically contain state usages in its body. The performance of these substates depends on whether the containing state is parallel or non-parallel:

- In non-parallel states (the default), substate performances occur sequentially, meaning only one substate is active at any given time. Transitions control the activation of these substates.
- In parallel states (triggered by adding the keyword parallel before the body), substates run concurrently, with their performances overlapping in time. No transitions are allowed between concurrent states.

Contained substates may also decompose further, employing transitions in sequential states.

##### Specifying parallel states

###### Specifying parallel states via the Textual Editor

To specify parallel states via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, declare the keyword parallel before the state's body.
2. Click the Synchronize button.

stateonparallel{ // The following substates are performed concurrentlystatespeed{ ...} // Any contained substates are performed sequentially 
stateheat{ ...} // Any contained substates are performed sequentially 
}

###### Specifying parallel states via the element shortcut menu

To specify parallel states via the element shortcut menu

- Right-click a state element in the Containment tree or its symbol in a view and in its shortcut menu, in the Properties group, select the Is Parallel property.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A state definition or usage may hierarchically contain state usages in its body. The performance of these substates depends on whether the containing state is parallel or non-parallel:</p><ul><li>In <strong>non-parallel states</strong> (the default), substate performances occur sequentially, meaning only one substate is active at any given time. Transitions control the activation of these substates.</li><li>In <strong>parallel states</strong> (triggered by adding the keyword <em>parallel</em> before the body), substates run concurrently, with their performances overlapping in time. No transitions are allowed between concurrent states.</li></ul><p>Contained substates may also decompose further, employing transitions in sequential states.</p><h3>Specifying parallel states</h3><h4>Specifying parallel states via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To specify parallel states via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Textual Editor, declare the keyword <em>parallel</em> before the state's body.</li><li>Click the <strong>Synchronize<span> </span></strong>button.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="584d9e6c-3fed-4086-9c45-16ad582d0d71"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">state</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">on</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">parallel</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {       <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// The following substates are performed concurrently<br /></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">      </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">state</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">speed</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> { ... </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// Any contained substates are <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">performed sequentially </span></span></span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">      </span>state</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">heat</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">{ ... </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}    <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// Any contained substates are <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">performed sequentially </span></span></span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Specifying parallel states via the element shortcut menu</h4><p>To specify parallel states via the element <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">shortcut menu</span></p><hr /><ul><li>Right-click a state element in the Containment tree or its symbol in a view and in its shortcut menu, in the<span> </span><strong>Properties</strong><span> </span>group, select the<span> </span><strong>Is Parallel<span> </span></strong>property.</li></ul><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254422318 space=SYSML2P version=2 -->
## PAGE 00381: (2026x) Part decomposition

- page_id: `254422318`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254422318/2026x+Part+decomposition
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-10-14T15:18:09.996+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Structure > (2026x) Parts
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Part definitions and part usages can be decomposed allowing you to build elaborate model structures composed of related parts. You can create a parts tree representing a composition of a part from other parts.

##### Decomposing part definitions

You can represent the next level of decomposition of a part definition using the feature typing (*defined by*) relationship.

###### Decomposing part definitions via the Textual Editor

To decompose part definitions via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, identify the part usage that you want to define, nested within a part definition.
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Feature typing' space=''] .

partdefVehicle{ 
partengine:Engine; 
parttransmission:Transmission; 
partdriveshaft:Driveshaft; 
partrearAxleAssembly:RearAxleAssembly;// part usage, owned by a part definition, defined by another part definitionpartfuelTank:FuelTank; 
} 
partdefRearAxleAssembly{ 
partdifferential:Differential; 
partrearAxle:Axle; 
partrearWheel1:Wheel; 
partrearWheel2:Wheel; 
}

###### Displaying decompositions of part definitions in a view

To display decompositions of part definitions in a view

- In a view, select the part definition symbol and the part usage symbol it defines, then use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Displaying elements in symbolic views' space=''] command under the In-Between Relationships category . The feature typing relationship is displayed.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Decomposing part usages

You can display a parts tree representing a composition of a part from other parts.

###### Decomposing part usages via the Textual Editor

To decompose part usages via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, within the body of the part you want to decompose, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating parts' space=''] .
2. Click the Synchronize button.

partvehicle_b : Vehicle{ // the owning part usage with nested part usages 
partengine:Engine{ 
partcyl:Cylinder; 
} 
parttransmission:Transmission; 
partfuelTank:FuelTank; 
partrearAxleAssembly:AxleAssembly; 
}

###### Decomposing part usages via a view by creating a composite part

To decompose part usages via a view by creating a composite part

1. In a view, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating parts' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Displaying elements in symbolic views' space=''] the part for which you want to create a composite part.
2. Create a composite part in one of the following ways:
  1. Via the view palette (part symbol option):
    1. In the view palette, under the parts group, click the part button to create a part usage element.
    2. Click on the part symbol displayed in the view pane to create a part owned by another.
  2. Via the view palette (feature membership option):
    1. In the view palette, under the Other Relationships group, click the feature membership button.
    2. First, click on the part symbol displayed in the view pane and then click elsewhere in the view pane to create a part owned by another.
  3. Via the smart manipulator (feature membership option):
    1. Click the part symbol for which you want to create a composite part.
    2. In its smart manipulator, click the feature membership icon , then click elsewhere in the view pane to create a part owned by another.

###### Decomposing part usages via a view by designating an existing part as a composite one

To decompose part usages via a view by designating an existing part as a composite one

1. In a view, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating parts' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Displaying elements in symbolic views' space=''] the parts you want to designate as the owner and the owned.
2. Designate an existing part as owned by another in one of the following ways:
  1. Via the view palette (feature membership option):
    1. In the view palette, under the Other Relationships group, click the feature membership button.
    2. First, click on the part symbol you want to designate as the owner, then the one you want to designate as the owned one.
  2. Via the smart manipulator (feature membership option):
    1. Click the part symbol you want to designate as the owner.
    2. In its smart manipulator, click the feature membership icon, then click on the part you want to designate as the owned one.

###### Displaying decompositions of part usages in a view

To display decompositions of part usages in a view

- In a view, select the owning part usage symbol and use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Displaying elements in symbolic views' space=''] command under the Tree-View Style category. The feature membership and the owned features (part usages) are displayed.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Reusing and modifying the parts tree

A part can subset another part, facilitating parts tree reuse and modification. The subsetting part inherits the part decomposition and other features. You can then modify the inherited parts and features through redefinition and subsetting in addition to adding new parts.

###### Reusing and modifying the parts tree via the Textual Editor

To reuse and modify the parts tree via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating parts' space=''] you want to reuse, then [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Subsetting' space=''] it.
2. (Optional) [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Redefinition' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Subsetting' space=''] the part features in the element body.
3. Click the Synchronize button.

partvehicle_b: Vehicle{// subsetted part 
partengine:Engine{ 
partcyl[4..8] :Cylinder; 
} 
parttransmission:Transmission; 
partfuelTank:FuelTank; 
partrearAxleAssembly:AxleAssembly; 
} 
partvehicle_c:> vehicle_b{ // a part reuses the parts tree by subsetting another part 
parteng:>engine{ 
partcy:>cyl[4]; 
} 
}

###### Reusing the parts tree via the view palette

To reuse the parts tree via the view palette

1. In a view, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating parts' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Displaying elements in symbolic views' space=''] the part whose decomposition you want to reuse.
2. In the view palette, under the Specializations group, click the subsetting button.
3. Click on the part you want to denote as the subsetting one and then click on the part you want to denote as the subsetted (reused) one.

###### Displaying the parts tree reuse in a view

To display the parts tree reuse in a view

1. In a view, select the subsetting and the subsetted part usage symbols, then u se the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Displaying elements in symbolic views' space=''] command under the In-Between Relationships category . The subsetting relationship is displayed.
2. (Optional) To display owned features of the subsetting or subsetted parts, use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Displaying elements in symbolic views' space=''] command. The owned features (part usages) are displayed. [ATTACHMENT filename='Parts tree_reuse_and_modification.png']

<strong>Tree-View Style</strong> category. <br />The feature membership and the owned features (part usages) are displayed. </li></ul><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Decomposing_a_part_usage .png" /></ac:image></p><h3>Reusing and modifying the parts tree</h3><p>A part can subset another part, facilitating parts tree reuse and modification. The subsetting part inherits the part decomposition and other features. You can then modify the inherited parts and features through redefinition and subsetting in addition to adding new parts.</p><h4>Reusing and modifying the parts tree via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To reuse and modify the parts tree via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Textual Editor, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating parts" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[declare the part usage]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> you want to reuse, then <ac:link ac:anchor="Subsetting a usage via the Textual Editor"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Subsetting" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[subset]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> it.</li><li>(Optional) <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Redefinition" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Redefine]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Subsetting" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[subset]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> the part features in the element body.</li><li>Click the <strong>Synchronize<span> </span></strong>button.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8811e49e-ed80-4671-a9c5-db96a9276d25"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Vehicle </span>{<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">                                     <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// subsetted part</span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Engine</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">FuelTank</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">rearAxleAssembly</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">AxleAssembly</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">vehicle_c</span><span        <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// a part reuses the parts tree by subsetting another part</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">eng</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">cy</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">4</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">];</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Reusing the parts tree via the view palette</h4><p>To reuse the parts tree via the view palette</p><hr /><ol><li>In a view, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating parts" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[create]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying elements in symbolic views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[display]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> the part whose decomposition you want to reuse. </li><li>In the view palette, under the Specializations group, click the <strong>subsetting </strong>button.</li><li>Click on the part you want to denote as the subsetting one and then click on the part you want to denote as the subsetted (reused) one.</li></ol><h4>Displaying the parts tree reuse in a view</h4><p>To display the parts tree reuse in a view</p><hr /><ol><li>In a view, select the subsetting and the subsetted part usage symbols, then u<span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">se the </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying elements in symbolic views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Specializations]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">command under the </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">In-Between Relationships category</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">. The subsetting relationship is displayed.</span></li><li>(Optional) To display owned features of the subsetting or subsetted parts, use the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying elements in symbolic views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Features]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </strong>command. The owned features (part usages) are displayed.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Parts tree_reuse_and_modification.png" /></ac:image></li></ol>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254422313 space=SYSML2P version=2 -->
## PAGE 00382: (2026x) Part interconnection

- page_id: `254422313`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254422313/2026x+Part+interconnection
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-12-03T08:54:06.215+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Structure > (2026x) Parts
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Parts can be connected to define their interactions and relationships within a model. The primary methods for connecting parts are:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Connection' space=''] (i.e., without ports) that specify a higher level of abstraction, likely establishing physical or logical connections.
  - Parts may also be connected using:
    - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Flow' space=''] connections
    - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Binding' space=''] connections

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Parts connected via a connection.

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Interface' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Port' space=''] (i.e., with ports) that specify more detailed, formalized interactions.
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Port' space=''] on parts must be compatible with the interface definition typing the interface usage that connects the parts via ports.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Parts connected through ports via an interface.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Parts can be connected to define their interactions and relationships within a model. The primary methods for connecting parts are: </p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Connection" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Connections]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (i.e., without ports) that specify a higher level of abstraction, likely establishing physical or logical connections. <ul><li>Parts may also be connected using: <ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Flow" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Flow]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> connections</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Binding" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Binding]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> connections</li></ul></li></ul></li></ul><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_connection.png" /></ac:image>Parts connected via a connection.</h6><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Interfaces]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Port" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (i.e., with ports) that specify more detailed, formalized interactions.<ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Port" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Port]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> on parts must be compatible with the interface definition typing the interface usage that connects the parts via ports.</li></ul></li></ul><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_interface.png" /></ac:image>Parts connected through ports via an interface.</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254422504 space=SYSML2P version=8 -->
## PAGE 00384: (2026x) Perform action

- page_id: `254422504`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254422504/2026x+Perform+action
- version_number: 8
- version_date: `2025-12-17T15:37:05.002+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Behavior > (2026x) Actions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A perform action usage specifies that an action is performed by the owner of the performed action usage. A perform action usage is referential, which allows the performed action behavior to be defined in a different context than that of the performer.

A perform action usage can be a feature of:

- a part definition or usage, specifying that the referenced action is performed by the containing part.
- an action definition or usage, whereby the perform action usage represents a referential "call" from the containing action to the performed action.

There are two ways an action can be performed by another element:

50%

- by referencing the action usage defined elsewhere in the model.
  - The element symbol is displayed with the perform keyword (unnamed element) or the perform action keyword (named element). [ATTACHMENT filename='perform_symbol.png'] [ATTACHMENT filename='perform_action_symbol_2.png']
  - See the procedures:
    - :
      - 
      - 
      - 

50%

- by containing an owned perform action usage, i.e., the performed action is the perform action usage itself.
  - The element symbol is displayed with the perform action keyword. [ATTACHMENT filename='perform_action_symbol.png']
  - See the procedures:
    - :
      - 
      - 
      - 

##### Performing an action by referencing an action usage defined elsewhere in the model

###### Performing actions via the Textual Editor1Performing actions via the Textual Editor

To perform actions via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, within the body of the element that you want to perform an action, do one of the following:
  1. For an unnamed perform action, declare the perform keyword followed by the name of the performed action.
  2. For a named one, declare the perform action keyword, specify the element name, then [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Reference subsetting' space=''] the performed action.
2. Click the Synchronize button.

action 'run mission'; // referenced action

part'drone in any mission'{

// unnamed perform action referencing an action defined elsewhere in the modelperform'run mission';

// named perform action referencing an action defined elsewhere in the modelperformaction'run delivery mission' references'run mission';performaction'run delivery mission'::>'run mission';

}

###### Performing actions via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

To perform actions via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

- Drag an action element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Drag and Drop handler' space=''] and drop it on:
  - the target element symbol that you want to perform the action by referencing it using the **Create Perform Action** command.
  - the perform action usage symbol that you want to perform the action by referencing it using the **Set Performed Action** command.

###### Performing actionsvia the Specification panel

To perform actions via the Specification panel

- Drag and drop an action element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Drag and Drop handler' space=''] and drop it on the **Performed Action** field in the Properties section of the Specification panel for the perform action element. The appropriate action is specified for the element.

##### Performing an action via the owned perform action usageitselfas the performed action

###### Performing actions via the Textual Editor

To perform actions via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, within the body of the element that you want to perform an action, declare the perform action keyword followed by its name.
2. Click the Synchronize button.

part'drone in any mission'{

// performed action is the defined perform action usage itself contained by the parentperformaction'run mission';

}

###### Performing actions via the Create Element command

To perform actions via the Create Element command

1. In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click **Create Element**.
2. In the Create Element dialog, select the perform action element.
3. Name the element.
4. Specify the perform action's features.

###### Performing actions via the view palette

To perform actions via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Actions group, click the perform action button.
2. Click in the view pane where you want to create the perform action symbol.
3. Name the element.
4. Specify the perform action's features.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Performing actions via the Drag and Drop handler in a view </h4><p>To perform actions via the Drag and Drop handler in a view</p><hr /><ul><li><span>Drag an action element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Drag and Drop handler" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Drag and Drop handler]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and drop it on:</span><ul><li><span>the target element symbol that you want to perform the action </span><span>by referencing it </span><span>using the</span><span> <strong>Create Perform Action </strong></span><span>command.</span><span> </span></li><li><span>the perform action usage symbol that you want to perform the action </span><span>by referencing it </span><span>using the</span><span> <strong>Set Performed Action </strong></span><span>command.</span><span> </span></li></ul></li></ul><h4>Performing actions<span> via the Specification panel</span></h4><p>To perform actions via the Specification panel</p><hr /><ul><li>Drag and drop an action element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Drag and Drop handler" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Drag and Drop handler]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and drop it on the<span> <strong>Performed Action</strong></span><strong><span> </span></strong>field in the Properties section of the Specification panel for the perform action element. <span><br /></span>The appropriate action is specified for the element.</li></ul><h3><span style="letter-spacing: -0.006em;">Performing an action via the owned perform action usage </span><span>itself </span><span style="letter-spacing: -0.006em;">as the performed action </span></h3><h4>Performing actions via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To perform actions via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ol><li class="auto-cursor-target">In the Textual Editor, within the body of the element that you want to perform an action, declare the <em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">perform action </em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">keyword followed by its name.</span></li><li>Click the <strong>Synchronize </strong>button.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e8e3dcb4-b540-4205-af85-28339128c8cb"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'drone in any mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// performed action is the defined perform action usage itself contained by the parent<br /></span></span></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span>perform</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'run mission'</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span></p><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Performing actions via the Create Element command</h4><p>To perform actions via the Create Element command</p><hr /><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click <strong>Create Element</strong>.</p></li><li>In the <strong>Create Element</strong> dialog, select the <strong>perform action </strong>element.</li><li>Name the element.</li><li>Specify the perform action's features.</li></ol><h4 class="auto-cursor-target">Performing actions via the view palette</h4><p>To perform actions via the view palette</p><hr /><ol><li>In the view palette, under the Actions group, click the <strong>perform action </strong><span>button.</span></li><li>Click in the view pane where you want to create the perform action symbol.</li><li>Name the element.</li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Specify the perform action's features. </span></li></ol></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254423137 space=SYSML2P version=5 -->
## PAGE 00385: (2026x) Port

- page_id: `254423137`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254423137/2026x+Port
- version_number: 5
- version_date: `2025-12-03T08:54:54.145+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Structure
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A port is a connection point through which a part definition makes some of its features available in a limited way. A port definition defines features that can be made available via ports. Two ports are compatible for connection if they have directed features that match with inverse directions, which can be established using port conjugation.

##### Creating port usage and definition elements

###### Creating port usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor

To create port usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the keyword:
  1. *port def*for a port definition.
  2. port for a port usage.
2. Specify the element name.
3. (Optional) Specify the port features in the element body.
4. Click the Synchronize button.

portdefOnOffCmdPort{outonOffCmd:OnOffCmd;}portonOffCmdPort:OnOffCmdPort;

###### Creating port usage and definition elements via the Create Element command

To create port usage and definition elements via the Create Element command

1. In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click **Create Element**.
2. In the Create Element dialog, select one of the following:
  1. port for port usage.
  2. port def for port definition.
3. Name the element.
4. (Optional) Specify the port's features.

###### Creating port usage and definition elements via the view palette

To create port usage and definition elements via the view palette

- To create an interface definition:
  1. In the view palette, under the Items/Ports/Attributes group, click the **port** def button to create an interface definition element.
  2. Click in the view pane where you want to create the port definition symbol.
- To create an interface usage:
  1. In the view palette, under the Items/Ports/Attributes group, click the **port** button to create an interface usage.
  2. In a view, click on an element symbol for which you want to create a port. If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to either the **port**or the **port****def**button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.

###### Creating port usage elements via the smart manipulator in a view

To create port usage elements via the smart manipulator in a view

- In a view, select an element symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the port command. A port is created for the element.

###### Creating port usage elements via the symbol compartment in a view

For more information see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Compartments' space=''] page.

To create port usage elements via the symbol compartment in a view

- Do one of the following:
  - Select an element symbol and click the Create Element button on it. In the dropdown menu, click the port element.
  - Select an element symbol and on the right side of the ports compartment, click the Create port button. The new element is created and displayed in the ports compartment.

###### Creating port usage elements via the Specification panel

To create port usage elements via the Specification panel

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification panel' space=''].
2. Do one of the following:
  1. In the Properties section bar, click the **Create Element** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The shortcut menu appears. Click the **port**element in the shortcut menu.
  2. In the **ports**compartment section's bar, click the **Create port**[IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The element is created in the **ports** compartment.

###### Auto-creating port definition elements via the Extract Definition command

To auto-create a port definition element via the Extract Definition command

- See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Extract Definition and Usage' space='']page.

##### Conjugating ports

Every port definition has an implicit conjugate port definition that reverses input and output features. You can easily use the conjugate port definitions by prepending the ~ symbol to the port definition name.

###### Using conjugated ports definitions via the Textual Editor

To use conjugate port definitions via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want the element to be created and declare the port keyword.
2. (Optional) Follow with the port name.
3. Follow with the feature typing relationship.
4. Specify the conjugated port definition:
  1. Full conjugation notation:
    1. Port definition name followed by :: (qualified name notation), followed by the port definition name with the ~ symbol prepended.
  2. Shorthand notation:
    1. Port definition name with the ~ symbol prepended.
5. Click the Synchronize button.

portdefFuelPort{ // Port definition 
attributetemperature:Temp; 
outitemfuelSupply:Fuel; 
initemfuelReturn:Fuel; 
} 
partdefFuelTankAssembly{ 
portfuelTankPort:FuelPort; 
} 
partdefEngine{ 
 port engineFuelPort : FuelPort::'~FuelPort'; //Conjugate port definition 
portengineFuelPort: ~FuelPort; // Using a ~ symbol on the port type is a shorthand for using the conjugate port definition 
}

##### Directed port features

A feature with a direction (in, out or inout) is a directed feature. Two ports are compatible for connection if they have directed features that match with inverse directions.

###### Creating directed port features

To create directed port features

1. In the Textual Editor, prepend the direction keyword - in , out , inout - to the feature.
2. Click the Synchronize button. portdefFuelOutPort{ 
attributetemperature:Temp; 
outitemfuelSupply:Fuel;// feature fuelSupply with direction out 
initemfuelReturn:Fuel;// feature fuelReturn with compatible direction in 
} 
portdefFuelInPort{ 
attributetemperature:Temp; 
initemfuelSupply:Fuel;// feature fuelSupply with compatible direction in 
outitemfuelReturn:Fuel;// feature fuelReturn with compatible direction out 
}

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254422764 space=SYSML2P version=5 -->
## PAGE 00386: (2026x) Predefined style sheets

- page_id: `254422764`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254422764/2026x+Predefined+style+sheets
- version_number: 5
- version_date: `2025-12-17T15:51:13.363+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Views > (2026x) Symbol styles
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Each SysML v2 project is automatically loaded with the *3DS**SysML Customization* library that contains predefined style sheets comprised of sets of rules defining symbol styles based on element metaclass.The predefined style sheets are located in the *StyleSheets* package in the *<DS_Styles> DassaultSystemesStyles*namespace of the *3DS**SysML Customization* library.

There are two predefined style sheets in the *DS_Styles::StyleSheets*package:

50%

- DefaultStyleSheet The DefaultStyleSheet style sheet contains rule-based styles that apply to all element symbols in SysML v2 projects by default based on element metaclass.
  - You can view the predefined style definitions used for symbol styles in the 'Predefined style definitions' column in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables' space=''] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables' space=''] page, and the DS_Styles::StyleSheets::ColorDefinitions package. [ATTACHMENT filename='defaultstylesheetpartdefinition.png']

50%

- BlackAndWhiteStyleSheet The BlackAndWhiteStyleSheet style sheet contains rule-based styles for representing all element symbols in black and white style. [ATTACHMENT filename='blackandwhitestylesheetpartdef.png']

You can:

- Use the default style sheet (default functionality).
- Switch between style sheets (predefined and/or custom ones). See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Activating style sheets' space=''] page.
- Create your own style sheets either by reusing and customizing an existing (predefined or custom) style sheet (e.g., DefaultStyleSheet ) or creating your own. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Custom style sheets' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating style sheets' space=''] pages.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Symbol styles represented by the *DefaultStyleSheet*(left)**and *BlackAndWhiteStyle*Sheet**(right) style sheets.

##### Accessing predefined style sheets

To access the predefined style sheets

1. In the Containment tree, expand the following nodes: Used Projects > 3DS SysML Customization > DassaultSystemesStyles>StyleSheets .
2. Right-click the StyleSheets package and in the shortcut menu, click Open under Textual Editor . The style sheets' textual notation is opened in the Textual Editor.

##### *DefaultStyleSheet*style sheet components

The *DefaultStyleSheet*style sheet contains multiple rules for symbol styles conditioned by element metaclass. The styles applied to the rules use predefined style definitions. You can view them once you access the style sheet.

###### *DefaultStyleSheet*rules

The rules are structured with comments based on the element metaclass and look like the following:

/* -------------PART/PORT---------*/ 
partpartPortRule :> rule { 
part:>> condition : ByElementMetaclassCondition { 
refitem:>> elementMetaclass = (SysML::Systems::PartUsage.metadata, SysML::Systems::PartDefinition.metadata, SysML::Systems::PortUsage.metadata, 
SysML::Systems::PortDefinition.metadata); 
} 
view:>> style : AbstractGreenStyle; 
}

###### *DefaultStyleSheet*predefined style definitions

You can view the full list of predefined style definitions in the 'Predefined style definitions' column on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables' space=''] page.

The predefined style definitions are listed in the *DS_Styles::StyleSheets::ColorDefinitions*packageand look like the following:

/* ------------------colors---------------------- */

viewdefAbstractGreenStyle:>ShapeStyle{ 
attribute:>>fillColordefault"#E3F2E3"; 
attribute:>>compartmentFillColordefault"#F6FBF6"; 
attribute:>>penColordefault"#123D15"; 
}

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254423029 space=SYSML2P version=3 -->
## PAGE 00387: (2026x) Prerequisites

- page_id: `254423029`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254423029/2026x+Prerequisites
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2026-06-25T11:47:09.610+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Getting started
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To work with all of the SysML v2 features, the following software should be [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Installation and system requirements' space='']:

- One of the following CATIA Magic/No Magic modeling tools:
  - Cameo Systems Modeler (Enterprise Edition only)
  - Cameo Enterprise Architecture
  - Magic Cyber Systems Engineer
  - Magic Systems of Systems Architect
- SysML v1 Plugin and Cameo Requirements Modeler. The plugins must be installed in addition to the following plugins to use SysML v2 in the modeling tool.
- SysML v2 Plugin. It provides the modeling tool with the majority of the SysML v2 features. It also includes the SysML v2 Textual Editor, allowing you to work with the SysML v2 features using textual notation via a textual editor.
- (Optional) [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML v2 Evaluation Plugin Documentation' space='SYSML2EP']. It allows you to conduct static SysML v2 model evaluations (mathematical calculations, calculation usage evaluation, constraints evaluation, and requirements evaluation).
- (Optional)[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Migration from SysML v1 to SysML v2' space='']. It enables model transformation from SysML v1 to SysML v2 features.

#### NOTE: Font Compatibility on macOS

Font Compatibility on macOS

The default font used in the modeling tool for SysML v2 is Roboto. However, because Roboto is not pre-installed on macOS, certain non-Latin characters may not render correctly when using the tool on macOS. To avoid character rendering issues, please [download](https://fonts.google.com/specimen/Roboto) and [install](https://support.apple.com/guide/font-book/install-and-validate-fonts-fntbk1000/mac) the Roboto font on your machine.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To work with all of the SysML v2 features, the following software should be <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Installation and system requirements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[installed]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>:</p><ul><li data-uuid="b4e417ca-7c0b-42ce-b1ce-e75a9e2fccef"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">One of the following CATIA Magic/No Magic modeling tools:</span><ul><li>Cameo Systems Modeler (Enterprise Edition only)</li><li>Cameo Enterprise Architecture</li><li>Magic Cyber Systems Engineer</li><li>Magic Systems of Systems Architect</li></ul></li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2856abbc-c3f8-496c-8c09-435ed730c927">SysML v1 Plugin and Cameo Requirements Modeler. The plugins must be installed in addition to the following plugins to use SysML v2 in the modeling tool.</ac:inline-comment-marker> </li><li>SysML v2 Plugin. It provides the modeling tool with the majority of the SysML v2 features. It also includes the SysML v2 Textual Editor, allowing you to work with the SysML v2 features using textual notation via a textual editor.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">(Optional) <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSML2EP" ri:content-title="SysML v2 Evaluation Plugin Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SysML v2 Evaluation Plugin]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. It allows you to conduct static SysML v2 model evaluations (mathematical calculations, calculation usage evaluation, constraints evaluation, and requirements evaluation).</span></p></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">(Optional)<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Migration from SysML v1 to SysML v2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ SysML v1 to SysML v2 Migration Plugin]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. It enables model transformation from SysML v1 to SysML v2 features.</span></li></ul><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="01f4567c-73fc-466b-9eb4-7d700ccaadd9"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Font Compatibility on macOS</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The default font used in the modeling tool for SysML v2 is Roboto. However, because Roboto is not pre-installed on macOS, certain non-Latin characters may not render correctly when using the tool on macOS. To avoid character rendering issues, please <span><a href="https://fonts.google.com/specimen/Roboto">download</a> and <a href="https://support.apple.com/guide/font-book/install-and-validate-fonts-fntbk1000/mac">install</a></span> the Roboto font on your machine.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254422273 space=SYSML2P version=3 -->
## PAGE 00388: (2026x) Printing

- page_id: `254422273`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254422273/2026x+Printing
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-12-17T15:00:27.218+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model management > (2026x) Working with views > (2026x) View image export
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can print:

- Active diagram
- Any diagram(s)
- All open diagrams
- Selected symbols of active diagram

You can specify the printing options or preview pages by using the following dialogs:

- Print Dialog
- Print Options Dialog
- Print Preview Window

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Print dialog

##### Printing

###### Printing an active diagram

To print an active diagram

1. Open a diagram you want to print.
2. Do one of the following:
  - In the main toolbar, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='print.png'] Print Active Diagram button.
  - In the main menu, click File > Print or press Ctrl+P on the keyboard . On the Print dialog, Print what area, select Active diagram , and click the Print button.

###### Printing any diagram(s)

To print any diagram(s)

1. In the main menu, click File > Print or press Ctrl+P on the keyboard . The Print dialog opens.
2. In the Print what area, choose Selected diagram(s) . The Select Diagram dialog opens.
3. Select the diagram(s) you want to print.
4. Click OK .
5. Click the Print button.

###### Printing all open diagrams

To print all open diagrams

1. In the main menu, click File > Print or press Ctrl+P on the keyboard . The Print dialog opens.
2. In the Print what area, select All opened diagram(s) .
3. Click the Print button.

###### Printing selected symbols of active diagram

To print selected symbols of active diagram

1. On the diagram, select the symbols you want to print.
2. In the main menu, click File > Print or press Ctrl+P on the keyboard. The Print dialog opens.
3. In the Print dialog, Print what area, choose Selected symbols of active diagram
4. Click the Print button.

##### Print preview

You can preview diagrams before printing them using the **Print Preview**window.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Print Preview window

###### Previewing diagrams before printing

To preview diagrams before printing

1. Open the Print Preview window in one of the following ways:
  1. In the main toolbar, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='print_preview_button.png'] Print Preview button.
  2. In the main menu, click File > Print Preview .
2. Select the diagram you want to preview from the list on the left side of the window. The diagram is previewed on the right side of the window.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254422667 space=SYSML2P version=5 -->
## PAGE 00390: (2026x) Project options

- page_id: `254422667`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254422667/2026x+Project+options
- version_number: 5
- version_date: `2026-05-06T12:21:58.327+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Project management > (2026x) Working with projects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Use the **Project Options** dialog to specify general project-specific options.The **Project Options** dialog includes option groups, each designated for specific features and containing lists of corresponding options. Groups are represented using the tree structure.

##### Opening the Project Options dialog

To open the Project Options dialog

- In the main menu, click Options > Project . [ATTACHMENT filename='project_options_dialog.png']

##### Modifying project options

To modify project options

1. Open the **Project Options** dialog .
2. Locate the option you want to modify. Use the quick filter to find the needed option in the list. Filtering is performed in all available options, not only the selected option group. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']Click the **Show Description** button to display the project option description section at the bottom of the dialog to read about the option functionality. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. An option value can be changed by typing a new value, setting a value to true / false , or selecting a value from the list. Click the **Reset to Defaults** button to restore default option values.
4. Click OK .

##### Project Options

The table below displays the default project options. Additional options may appear based on installed plugins.

| Option | Default value | Value options | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| General |  |  |  |
| Diagrams |  |  |  |
| Image Export |  |  |  |
| Time Limit to Build Diagram | 60 | Number | Specify the maximum time (in seconds) allotted for diagram building while exporting a diagram. If the time limit is exceeded, the diagram is not exported. Set to 0 to export the diagram without the time limit. |
| Image Height Limit | 10000 | Number | Specify the maximum image height (in pixels) for diagram export. If the height limit is exceeded, the exported diagram is cropped, or the diagram is not exported at all, depending on the selected preference. Set to 0 to export the diagram without the image height limit. |
| Image Width Limit | 10000 | Number | Specify the maximum image width (in pixels) for diagram export. If the width limit is exceeded, the exported diagram is cropped, or the diagram is not exported at all, depending on the selected preference. Set to 0 to export the diagram without the image width limit. |
| Export Preference When Limits Exceeded | Export Cropped Diagram | Export Cropped DiagramDo Not Export | Specify the diagram export preference if the diagram image height and/or width limits are exceeded. |
| General |  |  |  |
| General |  |  |  |
| Path to Used Project | <project.dir><install.root>\profiles<install.root>\modelLibraries | Custom | Specify the paths to store used projects and extension elements. |
| Decimal Places | 4 | Number | Specify the maximum number of decimal places to be used when displaying the real number in tables and element Specification windows. |
| SysML v2 |  |  |  |
| SysML v2 |  |  |  |
| Active View Creation Dialog | SysMLViewCreationDialog | Custom | Specify the Active Create View Dialog configuration. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Groups are represented using the tree structure.</p><h3>Opening the Project Options dialog</h3><p>To open the Project Options dialog</p><hr /><ul><li>In the main menu, click <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Project</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="project_options_dialog.png" /></ac:image></li></ul><h3>Modifying project options</h3><p>To modify project options</p><hr /><ol><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Project Options dialog"><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>Locate the option you want to modify.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7672be49-876c-41ce-9a52-f49c111fe1d2"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Use the quick filter to find the needed option in the list. Set to 0 to export the diagram without the image width limit. </td></tr><tr><td>Export Preference When Limits Exceeded</td><td>Export Cropped Diagram</td><td><p>Export Cropped Diagram</p><p>Do Not Export</p></td><td>Specify the diagram export preference if the diagram image height and/or width limits are exceeded.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#deebff" title="Background color : Light blue 35%" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#deebff"><strong>General</strong></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7">General</td></tr><tr><td>Path to Used Project</td><td><p>&lt;project.dir&gt;</p><p>&lt;install.root&gt;\profiles</p><p>&lt;install.root&gt;\modelLibraries</p></td><td>Custom</td><td>Specify the paths to store used projects and extension elements.</td></tr><tr><td>Decimal Places</td><td>4</td><td>Number</td><td>Specify the maximum number of decimal places to be used when displaying the real number in tables and element Specification windows.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#deebff" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#deebff"><strong>SysML v2</strong></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-grey" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="grey">SysML v2</td></tr><tr><td>Active View Creation Dialog</td><td><em>SysMLViewCreationDialog</em></td><td>Custom</td><td>Specify the Active Create View Dialog configuration.</td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
