<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254422018 space=SYSML2P version=2 -->
## PAGE 00391: (2026x) Project Statistics

- page_id: `254422018`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254422018/2026x+Project+Statistics
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-10-14T15:05:42.408+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Project management > (2026x) Working with projects > (2026x) Tools
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The project statistics feature calculates detailed statistics for the entire project and exports the results to a separate file. The exported statistical report will help solve performance problems you may be experiencing. Project statistics are calculated in the main project and, if selected, in used projects. The generated detailed project statistics file includes quantitative data about diagrams, elements, Project/Environment options, hardware & system, Teamwork Cloud projects, and performance.

#### NOTE: Confidential Information

Confidential Information

The exported file contains the names of the main project, used projects (if selected), and used elements. If they are confidential, you should select **Confidential**to keep their names hidden.

##### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Exporting detailed statistics

To export detailed statistics

1. In the main menu, select Tools > Project Statistics .
2. In the Project Statistics dialog, click the Export Detailed Statistics button. The Export Detailed Statistics Options dialog opens.
3. Select data to export along with the OS and hardware, diagrams/elements by types, and project options statistics that are included by default.
4. Specify other export options:
  - Select Include Used Project Statistics to generate and export used project statistics as well.
  - Select Confidential to keep the project element names hidden in the exported statistics report.
5. Click Export . The Select Location dialog opens.
6. Select the location where the file should be exported and click Save .
7. When the statistics file is generated, click Close .

##### The project statistics file

The generated detailed project statistics file includes the following quantitative data:

**Teamwork Cloud projects**

- Teamwork Cloud project local cache size on disk
- Teamwork Cloud project local cache latest blob size
- Teamwork Cloud project local cache blob size by revision
- Teamwork Cloud project local cache blob count by revision
- Teamwork Cloud project history depth
- Teamwork Cloud local project binary cache size
- Teamwork Cloud local project cache dir size
- Root object count by type
- Used projects count

**OS & Hardware**

- Hardware (RAM, OS, disk size and type) and Java info (heap)
- Restricted dir size

**Diagrams**

- Total size of diagrams binaries
- Diagram count by type
- Diagram count by kind

**Elements**

- Total element count
- Total size of strings
- Elements by meta-type
- Features by size
- String features by string length
- KerML based Elements statistics
  - Non-Standard Library Elements
  - Standard Library Elements

**Metadata**

- Diagram statistics
- Element statistics

**Standard Libraries**

- Diagram statistics
- Element statistics

**Symbol Styles**

- Diagram statistics
- Element statistics

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The project statistics feature calculates detailed statistics for the entire project and exports the results to a separate file. The exported statistical report will help solve performance problems you may be experiencing. Project statistics are calculated in the main project and, if selected, in used projects. The generated detailed project statistics file includes quantitative data about diagrams, elements, Project/Environment options, hardware &amp; system, Teamwork Cloud projects, and performance.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="811f00dd-6f47-4a6a-99ee-daa68a80650c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Confidential Information</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The exported file contains the names of the main project, used projects (if selected), and used elements. If they are confidential, you should select <strong>Confidential </strong>to keep their names hidden.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="project_statistics_dialog.png" /></ac:image></h3><h3>Exporting detailed statistics</h3><p>To export detailed statistics</p><hr /><ol><li>In the main menu, select <strong>Tools </strong>&gt; <strong>Project Statistics</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Project Statistics</strong> dialog, click the <strong>Export Detailed Statistics</strong> button. The <strong>Export Detailed Statistics Options </strong>dialog opens.</li><li>Select data to export along with the OS and hardware, diagrams/elements by types, and project options statistics that are included by default.</li><li>Specify other export options:<ul><li>Select <strong>Include Used Project Statistics</strong> to generate and export used project statistics as well.</li><li>Select <strong>Confidential </strong>to keep the project element names hidden in the exported statistics report. </li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>Export</strong>. The <strong>Select Location </strong>dialog opens.</li><li>Select the location where the file should be exported and click <strong>Save</strong>.</li><li>When the statistics file is generated, click <strong>Close</strong>.</li></ol><h3>The project statistics file</h3><p>The generated detailed project statistics file includes the following quantitative data:</p><p><strong>Teamwork Cloud projects</strong></p><ul><li>Teamwork Cloud project local cache size on disk</li><li>Teamwork Cloud project local cache latest blob size</li><li>Teamwork Cloud project local cache blob size by revision</li><li>Teamwork Cloud project local cache blob count by revision</li><li>Teamwork Cloud project history depth</li><li>Teamwork Cloud local project binary cache size</li><li>Teamwork Cloud local project cache dir size</li><li>Root object count by type</li><li>Used projects count</li></ul><p><strong>OS &amp; Hardware </strong></p><ul><li>Hardware (RAM, OS, disk size and type) and Java info (heap)</li><li>Restricted dir size</li></ul><p><strong>Diagrams</strong></p><ul><li>Total size of diagrams binaries</li><li>Diagram count by type </li><li>Diagram count by kind</li></ul><p><strong>Elements</strong></p><ul><li>Total element count</li><li>Total size of strings</li><li>Elements by meta-type</li><li>Features by size</li><li>String features by string length</li><li>KerML based Elements statistics<ul><li>Non-Standard Library Elements</li><li>Standard Library Elements</li></ul></li></ul><p><strong>Metadata</strong></p><ul><li>Diagram statistics</li><li>Element statistics</li></ul><p><strong>Standard Libraries</strong></p><ul><li>Diagram statistics</li><li>Element statistics</li></ul><p><strong>Symbol Styles</strong></p><ul><li>Diagram statistics</li><li>Element statistics</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254422673 space=SYSML2P version=3 -->
## PAGE 00392: (2026x) Quick find

- page_id: `254422673`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254422673/2026x+Quick+find
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-12-03T08:29:55.023+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Project management > (2026x) Working with projects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Use the Quick Find functionality to search for specific elements in the model. The simple and easy-to-use dialog is designed to locate the needed element by its declared or short name.

##### Opening the Quick Find dialog

To open the Quick Find dialog, do one of the following

- On the Containment tab toolbar click the [ATTACHMENT filename='quick_find_button.png'] Quick Find button. **[IMAGE alt='' src='']**
- On the main menu, click Edit > Quick Find .
- On the keyboard, press Ctrl+Alt+F or Cmd+Opt+F .

Search results are displayed in the drop-down list, which opens when you start typing the search phrase. Click the element you need in this list or correct the search query to perform a new search. The element will be selected in the Containment tree and on the active view if the element has symbols on the active view.

##### Quick Find dialog elements

The following table provides descriptions of the **Quick Find** dialog elements.

| Dialog element name/image | Dialog element type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Search for | Options group | Click Any Element to search in all elements of your project.Click View to search only in views. |
| Search By Name | Text box with a drop-down list | Type a phrase to search for in element names. See the search results in the drop-down list.The following elements are excluded from the search results: (e.g., in, abstract, derived, etc.)Element metaclasses (e.g., part, attribute, package)Feature values (owned expressions) (except for Association and Connector)Expressions (except for ConstraintUsage and CalculationUsage)Features owned by MetadataFeature (e.g., Documentation, Comment, TextualRepresentation)Special actions (e.g., ControlNodes, AssignActionUsage, SendActionUsage)Features owned by special actions (parameters) |
|  | Menu button | Click the button and customize search options. Match Text AnywhereTo search for matching phrase in any part of the element name, click the command to add the check mark.To explicitly search from the beginning of element name, click the command to remove the check mark.Use Camel CaseTo search for matching phrase typed in camel case, click the command to add the check mark.To search for matching phrase typed in regular case, click the command to remove the check mark.You can use camel case as follows:Type only the first letters (either capitalized or not) of each word, for example, "and" or "AND" to find "Analysis and Design".Type skipping spacers, for example, "cv3" to find "CV-3". |
| More | Menu button | Click the button to load the next 100 matches. By default, the first 100 matches are shown in the dialog. |
| Expand | Menu button | Click the button to load all model elements. |
| Apply Filter (Ctrl+Space) | Check box | Click to clear the check box, if you need to remove the search scope filter. If you clear the Apply Filter check box once, your selection is remembered for the next time. |
| Filter Options | Menu button | Click the button and customize the search scope filter options. Ensure the Apply Filter check box is selectedHide Standard/System LibrariesReduces the search results list by hiding elements that belong to the Standard/System Libraries. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><br /></p><p>Use the Quick Find functionality to search for specific elements in the model. The simple and easy-to-use dialog is designed to locate the needed element by its declared or short name.</p><h3>Opening the Quick Find dialog</h3><p>To open the Quick Find dialog, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>On the Containment tab toolbar click the <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="quick_find_button.png" /></ac:image> <strong>Quick Find </strong>button.<span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"><strong><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="quick_find_dialog.png" /></ac:image></strong></span></li><li>On the main menu, click <strong>Edit</strong> &gt; <strong>Quick Find</strong>.</li><li>On the keyboard, press<strong> Ctrl+Alt+F</strong> or <strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Cmd+Opt+F</span></strong>.<br /><br /></li></ul><p>Search results are displayed in the drop-down list, which opens when you start typing the search phrase. Click the element you need in this list or correct the search query to perform a new search. The element will be selected in the Containment tree and on the active view if the element has symbols on the active view.</p><h3>Quick Find dialog elements</h3><p>The following table provides descriptions of the <strong>Quick Find</strong> dialog elements.</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 100.0%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 10.5028%;" /><col style="width: 8.65922%;" /><col style="width: 80.838%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Dialog element name/image</strong></p></td><td><p><strong>Dialog element type</strong></p></td><td><p><strong>Description</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Search for</strong></p></td><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Options group</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><ul style="text-align: left;"><li>Click<span> </span><strong>Any Element</strong><span> </span>to search in all elements of your project.</li><li>Click<span> </span><strong>View</strong><span> </span>to search only in views.</li></ul></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Search By Name</strong></p></td><td><p>Text box with a drop-down list</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Type a phrase to search for in element names. See the search results in the drop-down list.</p><p>The following elements are excluded from the search results:</p><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul style="text-align: left;"><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Modifiers" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Modifiers]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (e.g., <em>in</em>, <em>abstract</em>, <em>derived</em>, etc.)</li><li>Element metaclasses (e.g., <em>part</em>, <em>attribute</em>, <em>package</em>)</li><li>Feature values (owned expressions)</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Multiplicity" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Multiplicities]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Relations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Relations]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (except for <em>Association </em>and <em>Connector</em>)</li><li>Expressions (except for <em>ConstraintUsage</em> and <em>CalculationUsage</em>)</li><li>Features owned by <em>MetadataFeature</em></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Annotations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Annotations]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><em> </em>(e.g., <em>Documentation</em>, <em>Comment</em>, <em>TextualRepresentation</em>)</li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Namespace"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Key concepts" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Root namespaces]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li>Special actions (e.g., <em>ControlNodes</em>, <em>AssignActionUsage</em>, <em>SendActionUsage</em>)<ul><li>Features owned by special actions (parameters)</li></ul></li></ul></li></ul></div></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="quick_find_filter_button.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Menu button</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Click the button and customize search options. <br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="filtering_options.png" /></ac:image><br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br class="atl-forced-newline" /><strong>Match Text</strong> <strong>Anywhere</strong></p><ul><li>To search for matching phrase in any part of the element name, click the command to add the check mark.</li><li>To explicitly search from the beginning of element name, click the command to remove the check mark.</li></ul><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" /><strong>Use Camel Case</strong></p><ul><li><p>To search for matching phrase typed in camel case, click the command to add the check mark.</p></li><li><p>To search for matching phrase typed in regular case, click the command to remove the check mark.</p></li></ul><p>You can use camel case as follows:</p><ul><li>Type only the first letters (either capitalized or not) of each word, for example, &quot;and&quot; or &quot;AND&quot; to find &quot;Analysis and Design&quot;.</li><li>Type skipping spacers, for example, &quot;cv3&quot; to find &quot;CV-3&quot;.</li></ul></div></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><strong>More</strong></p></div></td><td>Menu button</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Click the button to load the next 100 matches. By default, the first 100 matches are shown in the dialog.</p></div></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><strong>Expand <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="expand_button.png" /></ac:image></strong></p></div></td><td>Menu button</td><td>Click the button to load all model elements.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Apply Filter (Ctrl+Space)</strong></td><td>Check box</td><td>Click to clear the check box, if you need to remove the search scope filter. If you clear the <strong>Apply Filter</strong> check box once, your selection is remembered for the next time.</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><strong>Filter Options <ac:image ac:title="Filter Options" ac:alt="Filter Options"><ri:attachment ri:filename="filter_options.png" /></ac:image></strong></p></div></td><td>Menu button</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Click the button and customize the search scope filter options. Ensure the <strong>Apply Filter</strong> check box is selected</p><br /><p><strong>Hide Standard/System Libraries</strong></p><p><span>Reduces the search results list by hiding elements that belong to the Standard/System Libraries.</span></p></div></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254422911 space=SYSML2P version=7 -->
## PAGE 00393: (2026x) Quick Properties Panel

- page_id: `254422911`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254422911/2026x+Quick+Properties+Panel
- version_number: 7
- version_date: `2025-12-17T14:27:54.101+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Project management > (2026x) Modeling tool environment > (2026x) User interface
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

TheQuick properties panelhas two tabs, as shown in the figure below.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Quick properties panel

- The**Zoom**tab zooms in on the current view.
- The**Change Sets**tab shows the latest changes compared against the most recent project version saved in the local repository

##### Zoom tab

The Zoom tab allows you to preview and zoom in or out of the active view.

###### Opening the Zoom tab

To open the Zoom tab

- Do one of the following:
  - In the Model Browser, click the Zoom tab.
  - In the main menu, click Window > Zoom .

###### Displaying a view in the Zoom tab

To display a view in the Zoom tab

- Do one of the following:
  - Click the view in the Containment tree.
  - Click the view tab.

###### Zooming in or out of the active view

To zoom in or out of the active view

- In the Zoom tab, drag the corners of the blue square.

###### Quickly accessing any part of the view

To quickly access any part of the view

- In the Zoom tab, drag the blue square to the desired part of the view. The selected view part will be displayed in the view pane.

###### Fitting the view to the window

To fit the view to the window

- In the Zoom tab, on the blue square [ATTACHMENT filename='fit_window_button.png'] , click the Fit in Window button or press CTRL+W .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p style="text-align: left;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The<span> </span>Quick properties panel<span> </span>has two tabs, as shown in the figure below.</span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper image-center-wrapper"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Quick Properties Panel.png" /></ac:image></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Quick properties panel<br /></span></h6><ul style="text-align: left;"><li data-uuid="46fd7003-a8ed-4568-ad0d-9e8c4ab6be83"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The<strong><span> </span>Zoom</strong><span> </span>tab zooms in on the current view.</span></li><li data-uuid="c78a0d05-2041-48e5-b870-ba338c539b38"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The<strong><span> </span>Change Sets</strong><span> </span>tab shows the latest changes compared against the most recent project version saved in the local repository</span><br /></span></li></ul><h3>Zoom tab</h3><p>The Zoom tab allows you to preview and zoom in or out of the active view.</p><h4>Opening the Zoom tab</h4><p>To open the Zoom tab</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="6cf87d6d-7eb0-4d62-b46d-132f671b127b">Do one of the following:<ul><li data-uuid="2c019ebd-4651-4261-95ac-5174af036d50">In the Model Browser, click the <strong>Zoom</strong> tab.</li><li data-uuid="2883cdd8-740d-4568-a3f8-c5dc5916d0bc">In the main menu, click <strong>Window</strong> &gt; <strong>Zoom</strong>.</li></ul></li></ul><h4>Displaying a view in the Zoom tab</h4><p>To display a view in the Zoom tab</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="c6496948-19a1-4644-9c22-ee7c84719d49">Do one of the following:<ul><li data-uuid="3c7d0f43-6b07-45d0-a40f-f928409b1a02">Click the view in the Containment tree.</li><li data-uuid="220fab6f-5a55-4ee3-9d81-ad56b8d04635">Click the view tab.</li></ul></li></ul><h4>Zooming in or out of the active view</h4><p>To zoom in or out of the active view</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="66cde2a1-d593-4f63-a420-bb08b9176757">In the Zoom tab, drag the corners of the blue square.</li></ul><h4>Quickly accessing any part of the view</h4><p>To quickly access any part of the view</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="47f27ae9-f94a-4cbc-9e1d-c5a67592a11b">In the Zoom tab, drag the blue square to the desired part of the view. The selected view part will be displayed in the view pane.</li></ul><h4>Fitting the view to the window</h4><p>To fit the view to the window</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="8c24fcec-4bbc-4800-9d61-b93e782f9c9b">In the Zoom tab, on the blue square <ac:image ac:height="28" ac:width="26"><ri:attachment ri:filename="fit_window_button.png" /></ac:image>, click the <strong>Fit in Window</strong> button or press <strong>CTRL+W</strong>.</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254422301 space=SYSML2P version=4 -->
## PAGE 00394: (2026x) Redefinition

- page_id: `254422301`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254422301/2026x+Redefinition
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2025-12-03T08:51:38.019+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Relations > (2026x) Specializations
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The redefinition relationshipis a kind of subsetting used to replace an inherited feature by redefining.

##### Redefinition

###### Redefining a usage via the symbol shortcut menu in a view

To redefine a usage via the symbol shortcut menu in a view

- Right-click an inherited usage element symbol in a view and in the shortcut menu select**Refactor**, then**Redefine**.

###### Redefining a usage via the Textual Editor

To redefine a usage via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, identify the usage element that you want to be the redefining one.
2. Follow the element name with either one of the following:
  1. keyword redefines
  2. textual symbol :>>
3. Follow the keyword with the name of the redefined usage element.
4. Click the Synchronize button. partdefEngine{ 
partcylinders[4..8] :Cylinders; 
}partdieselEngine:Engine{ 
partdieselEngineCylinders[4] :>>cylinders; 
/* or */ 
partdieselEngineCylinders[4] redefinescylinders; 
}

###### Redefining a usage via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

To redefine a usage via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

- Drag and drop an element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Drag and Drop handler' space=''] on an element symbol executing the Set Redefined Feature command.

###### Redefining a usage via the view palette

To redefine a usage via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Specializations group, select redefinition .
2. Draw the line with the arrowhead going from the redefining element to the inherited one. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Redefining a usage via the Create Relationship command

To redefine a usagevia the Create Relationship command

1. Right-click the element that you want to be the redefining one.
2. In the shortcut menu, click Create Relationship , then in the submenu, click Outgoing , and then in the submenu, click redefinition . The Element Picker dialog opens.
3. In the Element Picker dialog, do one of the following:
  1. In a view, click the element symbol you want to redefine. Hold Shift to select multiple elements.
  2. Click the Add [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The element selection dialog opens. In the element selection dialog, select the element you want to redefine and click OK .
  3. Drag and drop the needed element you want to redefine from the Containment tree onto the Element Picker .
4. In the Element Picker dialog, click OK .

###### Redefining a usage via the Specification panel

To redefine a usage via the Specification panel

- See the procedure [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification panel' space=''] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification panel' space=''] page.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254422305 space=SYSML2P version=4 -->
## PAGE 00395: (2026x) Reference subsetting

- page_id: `254422305`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254422305/2026x+Reference+subsetting
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2025-12-03T08:51:26.255+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Relations > (2026x) Specializations
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A reference subsettingis a kind of subsetting relationship. While a subsetting usage can have multiple subsetted usages, a reference subsetting usage can only have one specific subsetted usage.

##### Reference subsetting

###### Reference subsetting a usage via the Textual Editor

To reference subset a usage via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, identify the usage element that you want to be the referencing one.
2. Follow the element name with either one of the following:
  1. keyword references
  2. textual symbol ::>
3. Follow the keyword with the name of the referenced usage element.
4. Click the Synchronize button. part'drone in delivery mission'{ 
perform action ::>'deliver goods'; 
/* or */ perform action references'deliver goods'; 
} 
action'deliver goods'{ 
action'start mission';action'fly to destination'; 
first'start mission'then'fly to destination'; 
}

###### Reference subsetting a usage via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

To reference subset a usage via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

- Drag and drop an element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Drag and Drop handler' space=''] on an element symbol executing the Set Referenced Feature command.

###### Reference subsetting a usage via the view palette

To reference subset a usage via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Specializations group, select reference subsetting .
2. Draw the line with the arrowhead going from the referencing usage to the referenced one. [ATTACHMENT filename='Reference subsetting.png']

###### Reference subsetting a usage via the Create Relationship command

To reference subset a usagevia the Create Relationship command

1. Right-click the element that you want to be the subsetting one.
2. In the shortcut menu, click Create Relationship , then in the submenu, click Outgoing , and then in the submenu, click reference subsetting . The Element Picker dialog opens.
3. In the Element Picker dialog, do one of the following:
  1. In a view, click the element symbol you want to subset. Hold Shift to select multiple elements.
  2. Click the Add [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The element selection dialog opens. In the element selection dialog, select the element you want to subset and click OK .
  3. Drag and drop the needed element you want to subset from the Containment tree onto the Element Picker .
4. In the Element Picker dialog, click OK .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">A reference subsetting </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">is a kind of subsetting relationship. While a subsetting usage can have multiple subsetted usages, a reference subsetting usage can only have one specific subsetted usage.</span></p><h3>Reference subsetting</h3><h4>Reference subsetting a usage via the Textual Editor</h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To reference subset a usage via the Textual Editor</span></p><hr /><ol><li>In the Textual Editor, identify the usage element that you want to be the referencing one.</li><li>Follow the element name with either one of the following:<ol><li>keyword<em> references</em></li><li>textual symbol <em>::&gt;</em></li></ol></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Follow the keyword with the name of the referenced usage element. </p></li><li>Click the <strong>Synchronize </strong>button.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2ae4392b-249b-48a6-85cf-4e64347e9adc"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'drone in delivery mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">   perform action ::&gt;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'deliver goods'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;<br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">   /* or */<br /></span></span>   <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">perform action references</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'deliver goods'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'deliver goods'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">   action<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'start mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;<br /></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">   action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'fly to destination'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">   first</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'start mission' </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">then</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'fly to destination'</span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h4>Reference subsetting a usage via the Drag and Drop handler in a view</h4><p>To reference subset a usage via the Drag and Drop handler in a view</p><hr /><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Drag and drop an element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's<span> </span></span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Drag and Drop handler" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Drag and Drop handler]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">on an <span>element symbol executing </span></span>the <strong>Set Referenced Feature </strong>command.</li></ul><h4>Reference subsetting a usage via the view palette</h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To reference subset a usage via the view palette</span></p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="42e90683-2094-4b46-90e7-5952d032c41d">In the view palette, under the Specializations group, select <strong>reference subsetting</strong>. </li><li data-uuid="11f02ad9-73d7-45a5-a6a7-02a5418dc525">Draw the line with the arrowhead going from the referencing usage to the referenced one.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Reference subsetting.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><h4>Reference subsetting a usage <span>via the Create Relationship command</span></h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To reference subset a usage </span>via the Create Relationship command</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the element that you want to be the subsetting one.</li><li>In the shortcut menu, click <strong>Create Relationship</strong>, then in the submenu, click <strong>Outgoing</strong>, and then in the submenu, click <strong>reference subsetting</strong>. The <strong>Element Picker </strong>dialog opens.</li><li>In the <strong>Element Picker </strong>dialog, do one of the following:<ol><li>In a view, click the element symbol you want to subset. Hold Shift to select multiple elements. </li><li>Click the <strong>Add <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="add_button.png" /></ac:image> </strong>button. The element selection dialog opens. In the element selection dialog, select the element you want to subset and click <strong>OK</strong>.</li><li>Drag and drop the needed element you want to subset from the Containment tree onto the <strong>Element Picker</strong>.</li></ol></li><li>In the <strong>Element Picker</strong><strong> </strong>dialog, click <strong>OK</strong>. </li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=312247377 space=SYSML2P version=5 -->
## PAGE 00397: (2026x) Removing used projects

- page_id: `312247377`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/312247377/2026x+Removing+used+projects
- version_number: 5
- version_date: `2026-06-10T11:53:20.127+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Project management > (2026x) Working with projects > (2026x) Managing local used projects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### EXPAND: Click here to expand the documentation for the 2026x version...

Click here to expand the documentation for the 2026x version...

Used projects are removed from the main project with all their references, including their used projects.

###### Removing a used project via the Containment tree

To remove a used project via the Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree of the main project, expand the Used Projects node.
2. Right-click a used project and select Remove .
3. When the question dialog opens, click Yes to confirm that you want to remove the used project. [ATTACHMENT filename='Removing a used project via the Containment tree 2026x.png']

###### Removing a used project via the Project Usages dialog 2026xRemoving a used project via the Project Usages dialog

To remove a used project via the Project Usages dialog

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local used projects' space='']Open the **Project Usages**dialog and d o one of the following:
  1. In the dialog's toolbar, click the **Remove with References** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button .
  2. In the dialog's project usages tree, right-click a used project and in the shortcut menu, click the **Remove with References**command.
2. When the question dialog opens, click Yes to confirm that you want to remove the used project.
3. Click the OK button to close the Project Usages dialog.

###### Removing a used project via the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog 2026xRemoving a used project via the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog

To remove a used project via the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local used projects' space='']Open the **Advanced Project Usages Configuration**dialog .
2. In the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog, do one of the following:
  1. S elect a used project and in the dialog's toolbar, click the **Remove with References** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button .
  2. Right-click a used project and in the shortcut menu, click the **Remove with References** command . The cell in the Action column for the removed used project states ' Remove, delete references '. [ATTACHMENT filename='Removing a used project via the Advanced Usages Configuration dialog 2026x.png']
3. (Optional) To undo the action, do one of the following:
  1. In the dialog's toolbar, click the Reset Changes for Selection [ATTACHMENT filename='reset_changes_for_selection_button.png'] button.
  2. Right-click the used project and in the shortcut menu, click the Reset Changes for Selection command.
4. Do one of the following:
  1. To save changes and keep the dialog open, click the Apply button.
  2. To save changes and close the dialog, click the OK button.
  3. To discard changes and close the dialog, click the Cancel button.
5. Click the OK button to close the Project Usages dialog.

#### NOTE: 2026x HotFix1 version

2026x HotFix1 version

This documentation page is for the 2026x HotFix1 version of the SysML v2 Plugin. If you are working with the 2026x version, expand the section above. However, we highly advise upgrading to the 2026x HotFix1 version.

Used projects are removed from the main project with all their references, including their used projects (indirectly used projects).

###### Removing a used project via the Containment tree

To remove a used project via the Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree of the main project, expand the Used Projects node.
2. Right-click a used project and select Remove .
3. When the question dialog opens, click Yes to confirm that you want to remove the used project. [ATTACHMENT filename='Removing a used project via the Containment tree.png']

###### Removing a used project via the Project Usages dialog

To remove a used project via the Project Usages dialog

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local used projects' space='']Open the **Project Usages**dialog and d o one of the following:
  1. In the dialog's toolbar, click the **Remove with References** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button .
  2. In the dialog's project usages tree, right-click a used project and in the shortcut menu, click the **Remove with References**command.
2. When the question dialog opens, click Yes to confirm that you want to remove the used project.
3. Click the OK button to close the Project Usages dialog.

###### Removing a used project via the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog

To remove a used project via the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local used projects' space='']Open the **Advanced Project Usages Configuration**dialog .
2. In the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog, do one of the following:
  1. S elect a used project and in the dialog's toolbar, click the **Remove with References** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button .
  2. Right-click a used project and in the shortcut menu, click the **Remove with References** command . The cell in the Action column for the removed used project states ' Remove, delete references '. [ATTACHMENT filename='Removing a used project via the Advanced Usages Configuration dialog.png']
3. (Optional) To undo the action, do one of the following:
  1. In the dialog's toolbar, click the Reset Changes for Selection [ATTACHMENT filename='reset_changes_for_selection_button.png'] button.
  2. Right-click the used project and in the shortcut menu, click the Reset Changes for Selection command.
4. Do one of the following:
  1. To save changes and keep the dialog open, click the Apply button.
  2. To save changes and close the dialog, click the OK button.
  3. To discard changes and close the dialog, click the Cancel button.
5. Click the OK button to close the Project Usages dialog.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254422761 space=SYSML2P version=2 -->
## PAGE 00398: (2026x) Rendering basics

- page_id: `254422761`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254422761/2026x+Rendering+basics
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-10-14T15:26:05.295+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Views > (2026x) Rendering views
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

See the basic procedures for working with renderings and rendering definitions.

##### Creating rendering usage and definition elements

###### Creating rendering usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor

To create rendering usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the keyword:
  1. *rendering def*for a rendering definition.
  2. *rendering* for a rendering usage.
2. Specify the element name.
3. (Optional) Specify the rendering's features in the element body.
4. Click the Synchronize button.

renderingdefGraphicalRendering;

renderingasTreeDiagram : GraphicalRendering;

##### Rendering views

###### Rendering views via the Textual Editor

To render views via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, within the curly braces of the view's body, declare the keyword render followed by the name of the rendering usage you want to use .
2. Click the Synchronize button.

viewdef'Part Structure View'{ 
renderasTreeDiagram; // references the rendering usage *asTreeDiagram* elsewhere in the model 
}

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>See the basic procedures for working with renderings and rendering definitions.</p><h3>Creating rendering usage and definition elements</h3><h4>Creating rendering usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To create rendering usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the keyword: <ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span> </span><em>rendering def</em><span> </span>for a rendering definition.</p></li><li><span> <em>rendering</em></span><em><span> </span></em>for a rendering usage.</li></ol></li><li>Specify the element name.</li><li>(Optional) Specify the rendering's features in the element body.</li><li>Click the <strong>Synchronize </strong>button.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f1af8d45-8838-424f-8130-2fa40803394a"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">rendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">GraphicalRendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">rendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">asTreeDiagram : <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">GraphicalRendering</span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Rendering views</h3><h4>Rendering views via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To render views via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Textual Editor, within <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">the curly braces of the view's body, </span>declare the keyword<span> </span><em>render </em>followed by the name of the rendering usage you want to use<span>.</span></li><li>Click the <strong>Synchronize </strong>button.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c84fed49-18d6-4037-a45f-e5240f0929d0"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">'Part Structure View'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">render</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">asTreeDiagram</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;    <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// references the rendering usage <em>asTreeDiagram</em> elsewhere in the model</span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254422759 space=SYSML2P version=4 -->
## PAGE 00399: (2026x) Rendering views

- page_id: `254422759`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254422759/2026x+Rendering+views
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2025-12-03T10:30:33.636+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Views
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A rendering feature specifies how a particular view and its exposed elements are supposed to be rendered, i.e., displayed for viewing. Once you have[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating views' space=''], specified the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Exposing elements for views' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Filtering elements for views' space=''] the exposed elements, you can specify the renderer for the view.

##### Renderings, rendering styles, and view definitions

The following table maps renderings and rendering styles available in the modeling tool to corresponding predefined view definitions.

| View rendering kind | Rendering style | Renderings | Subsets of the renderings | Rendering definitions |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Graphical | Nested-view | Views::asInterconnectionDiagramView definitions using the renderer:Standard predefined symbolic view definitions:'General View' (through inheritance from 'Symbolic View' view def) 'Interconnection View' (through inheritance from 'Symbolic View' view def) 'Action Flow View' (through inheritance from 'Symbolic View' view def)'State Transition View' (through inheritance from 'Symbolic View' view def)Predefined symbolic view definitions that use expressions:NestedViewUsagesNestedView (by subclassifying NestedView)PartsNestedView (by subclassifying UsagesNestedView)Parts&PortsNestedView (by subclassifying UsagesNestedView)ActionsNestedView (by subclassifying UsagesNestedView)StatesNestedView (by subclassifying UsagesNestedView) | - | Views::GraphicalRendering |
| Tree-view | Views::asTreeDiagramView definitions using the renderer:Predefined symbolic view definitions that use expressions:TreeViewPartsTreeView (by subclassifying TreeView)ActionsTreeView (by subclassifying TreeView)RequirementTreeView (by subclassifying TreeView) | - | Views::GraphicalRendering |  |
| Tabular | - | Views::asElementTable | DS_Views::CoreViews::asTableView definitions using the renderer:Standard predefined tabular view definitions:'Generic Table' (subsets asTable inherited from 'Base Table' via subclassification)'Requirements Table' (subsets asTable inherited from 'Base Table' via subclassification) | Views::TabularRendering |

##### Rendering views

Ways to render views:

- . You can render views through inheritance, e.g., if the view is defined by a view definition that contains a renderer, the renderer is inherited by and applied to the defined view.
- . You can specify a renderer after the expose and filter declarations directly for the specific view, using the keyword render .

###### Inheriting a renderer

Generally, renderings are specified for view definitions, which are then used to define specific view usages, the latter inheriting the renderer specified for its definition.

See the table for all renderings available in the modeling tool and the predefined view definitions that use them. Choose the view definition based on the renderer specified for it that would be the most suitable for your view, then define the view by the view definition. However, many predefined view definitions carry specific filters and other customizations that will be inherited in addition to the renderer, thus choose accordingly. For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Symbolic views' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Tabular views' space=''] pages.

The corresponding procedures are:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating symbolic views' space=''] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating symbolic views' space=''] page.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating symbolic views' space=''] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating symbolic views' space=''] page.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating tabular views' space=''] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating tabular views' space=''] page.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating tabular views' space=''] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating tabular views' space=''] page.

#### PANEL: Explanation in textual notation

Explanation in textual notation

// explanation and example of a symbolic view *General View*inheriting a renderer through subclassification

abstractviewdef<sv>'Symbolic View':>Visualization{ 
//... 
renderViews::asInterconnectionDiagram; //view definition has the *Views::asInterconnectionDiagram***renderer specified for it 
}

abstractviewdef<bsv>'Base Symbolic View':>sv{ // view definition 'Base Symbolic View' inherits the *Views::asInterconnectionDiagram***renderer through subclassification of 'Symbolic View' 
partbaseViewPalette:ViewPalettes::BasePalette:>>palette; 
}

viewdef<gv>'General View':>bsv,StandardViewDefinitions::gv{// view definition 'General View' inherits the*Views::asInterconnectionDiagram***renderer through subclassification of 'Base Symbolic View' 
partgeneralViewPalette:GeneralPalette:>>baseViewPalette; // view definition also contains a specific view palette 
}

view'General View':DS_Views::SymbolicViews::gv; // the specific view is defined by the view definition 'General View', inheriting its renderer and view palette

// explanation and example of a tabular view *Generic Table* inheriting a renderer through subclassification

abstractviewdef<bt>'Base Table':>bgv{ // view definition *'Base Table'* has the *asTable*renderer specified for it 
renderasTable; 
}

viewdef<gt>'Generic Table':> DS_Views::CoreViews::bt{ // view definition 'Generic Table' subsets the *asTable*renderer inherited through subclassification of *'Base Table'* 
renderrenderingasTable:>>asTable; 
}

view'Generic Table':DS_Views::TabularViews::gt;// the specific view is defined by the view definition 'Generic Table', inheriting its renderer

###### Specifying a renderer

Instead of creating views defined by view definitions that already have specified renderers, you can specify therenderer directly for the new view.

The corresponding procedures are:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating symbolic views' space=''] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating symbolic views' space=''] page.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating tabular views' space=''] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating tabular views' space=''] page.

#### PANEL: Explanation in textual notation

Explanation in textual notation

viewnewView{ // new view element is not defined by any view definition 
renderViews::asInterconnectionDiagram; // the view has a renderer *asInterconnectionDiagram*, rendering it as a 'General View' symbolic view with nested-view rendering style 
}

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'General View'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span> </span>:<span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_Views::SymbolicViews</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">::gv</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;       <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the specific view is defined by the view definition 'General View', inheriting its renderer and view palette </span></span> </p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// explanation and example of a tabular view <em>Generic Table</em> inheriting a renderer through subclassification</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">abstract</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> &lt;bt&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">'Base Table'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">bgv</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> { <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// view definition <em>'Base Table'</em> has the <em>asTable </em>renderer specified for it</span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">render</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">asTable</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> &lt;gt&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">'Generic Table'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_Views::CoreViews::bt</span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// view definition 'Generic Table' subsets the <em>asTable </em>renderer inherited through subclassification of <em>'Base Table'</em></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">render</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">rendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">asTable</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">asTable;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'Generic Table'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_Views::TabularViews::gt</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the specific view is defined by the view definition 'Generic Table', inheriting its renderer </span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Specifying a renderer</h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Instead of creating views defined by view definitions that already have specified renderers, you can specify the<span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">renderer directly for the new view.<br /></span></p><p>The corresponding procedures are:</p><ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Creating undefined symbolic views using renderers via the Textual Editor"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating symbolic views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Creating undefined symbolic views using renderers via the Textual Editor]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating symbolic views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Creating symbolic views]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Creating undefined tabular views using renderers via the Textual Editor"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating tabular views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Creating undefined tabular views using renderers via the Textual Editor]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating tabular views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Creating tabular views]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b729039d-3f68-43b7-a173-7378442669ff"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Explanation in textual notation</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">newView</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {   <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// new view element is not defined by any view definition</span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">render</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">Views::asInterconnectionDiagram</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the view has a renderer <em>asInterconnectionDiagram</em>, rendering it as a 'General View' symbolic view with nested-view rendering style</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=263094430 space=SYSML2P version=3 -->
## PAGE 00400: (2026x) Rendering views basics

- page_id: `263094430`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/263094430/2026x+Rendering+views+basics
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-12-11T20:10:40.675+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Views > (2026x) Rendering views
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

There are two ways a rendering usage can be specified for a view:

50%

- by referencing the rendering usage defined elsewhere in the model.
  - The element symbol is displayed with the render keyword (unnamed element) or the rendering keyword (named element) . [ATTACHMENT filename='rendering.png']
  - See the procedures:
    - :
      - 

50%

- by containing an owned rendering usage, i.e., the view renderer is the owned rendering usage itself.
  - The element symbol is displayed with the rendering keyword. [ATTACHMENT filename='rendering2.png']
  - See the procedures:
    - :
      - 

##### Rendering views by referencing renderings defined elsewhere in the model

###### Rendering views via the Textual Editor

To render views via the Textual Editor

1. In the body of a view for which you want to reference a rendering, declare one of the following:
  1. For an unnamed rendering, declare the render keyword, then follow with the name of the rendering you want to reference.
  2. For a named rendering, declare the rendering keyword, name the new usage, and then [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Reference subsetting' space=''] the rendering you want to reference.
2. Click the Synchronize button.

viewvehicleModel{ 
renderasTreeDiagram;// unnamed rendering 
renderingasNewTreeDiagram::>asTreeDiagram;// named rendering 
}

##### Rendering views via the owned rendering usage itself as the renderer

###### Rendering views via the Textual Editor2Rendering views via the Textual Editor

To render views via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor within the curly braces of the view's body, declare the rendering keyword, specify the element name, and then the rendering's body.
2. Click the Synchronize button.

viewvehicleModel{renderingasCustomTreeDiagram:GraphicalRendering;// // new view-owned rendering usage}

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=261623515 space=SYSML2P version=2 -->
## PAGE 00401: (2026x) Require constraint

- page_id: `261623515`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/261623515/2026x+Require+constraint
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-12-11T19:19:13.345+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Requirements > (2026x) Requirement constraints
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A require constraint states the required constraints imposed on the model, expressed formally or informally in the requirement.

There are two ways these constraints can be required:

50%

- by referencing a constraint defined elsewhere in the model.
  - The element symbol is displayed with the require keyword (unnamed element) or the require constraint keyword (named element). 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - See the procedures:
    - :
      - 
      - 
      - 

50%

- by containing an owned require constraint usage, i.e., the required constraint is the require constraint usage itself.
  - The element symbol is displayed with the require constraint keyword. [ATTACHMENT filename='require constraint 1.png']
  - See the procedures:
    - :
      - 
      - 
      - 

##### Requiring a constraint by referencing a constraint defined elsewhere in the model

###### Requiring constraints via the Textual Editor

To require constraints via the Textual Editor

1. In the body of a requirement for which you want to reference a constraint, declare one of the following:
  1. For an unnamed require constraint, declare the require keyword, then follow with the name of the constraint you want to reference.
  2. For a named require constraint, declare the require constraint keyword, name the new usage, and then [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Reference subsetting' space=''] the constraint you want to reference.
2. Specify the constraint (expression, attributes, etc.) in the element's body.
3. Click the Synchronize button.

constraintmassLimit{ 
attributemass:MassValue; 
attributemassLimit:MassValue; 
mass<=massLimit 
} 
requirementdefMaximumMass{ 
attributemassActual:MassValue; 
attributemassRequired:MassValue;

requiremassLimit{// step 1.a - unnamed usage reference subsets another constraint usage 
:>>mass=massActual; 
:>>massLimit=massRequired; 
}

requireconstraintmLimit::>massLimit{// step 1.b - named usage reference subsets another constraint usage 
:>>mass=massActual; 
:>>massLimit=massRequired; 
} 
}

###### Requiring constraints via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

To require constraints via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

- Drag and drop a constraint element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Drag and Drop handler' space=''] on a requirement element symbol executing the Create Require Constraint command.

###### Requiring constraints via the Specification panel

To require constraints via the Specification panel

- Drag and drop a constraint element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's Drag and Drop handler and drop it on the **Required Constraint** field in the Properties section of the Specification panel for the require constraint element. The appropriate constraint is specified for the element.

##### Requiring a constraint via the owned require constraint usage itself as the required constraint

###### Requiring constraints via the Textual Editor1Requiring constraints via the Textual Editor

To require constraints via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the keyword require constraint for a require constraint usage
2. Specify the element name.
3. Specify the constraint (expression, attributes, etc.) in the element's body.
4. Click the Synchronize button.

requirementdefMaximumMass{ 
attributemassActual:MassValue; 
attributemassRequired:MassValue;

requireconstraintmLimit{// named constraint 
massActual<=massRequired 
} 
}

###### Requiring constraints via the symbol compartment in a view

For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Compartments' space=''] page.

To require constraints via the symbol compartment in a view

1. Do one of the following:
  1. Select an element symbol and click the Create Element button on it. In the dropdown menu, click the require constraint element.
  2. Select an element symbol and on the right side of the require constraints compartment, click the Create require constraint button.
2. Specify the constraint (expression, attributes, etc.). The new element is created and displayed in the require constraints compartment.

###### Requiring constraints via the Specification panel1Requiring constraints via the Specification panel

To require constraints via the Specification panel

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification panel' space=''].
2. Do one of the following:
  1. In the Properties section bar, click the **Create Element** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The shortcut menu appears. Click the **require constraint**element in the shortcut menu.
  2. In the **require constraints**compartment section's bar, click the **Create require constraint**[IMAGE alt='' src=''] button.
3. Specify the constraint (expression, attributes, etc.). The element is created in the require constraints compartment.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

panel]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></li></ul></li></ul></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3>Requiring a constraint by referencing a constraint defined elsewhere in the model</h3><h4>Requiring constraints via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To require following:<ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">For an unnamed require constraint, </span>declare the <em>require </em><span>keyword, then follow with the name of the constraint you want to reference.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">For a named require constraint, </span>declare the <em>require constraint </em><span>keyword, name the new usage, and then <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Reference subsetting" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[reference subset]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> the constraint you want to reference.</span></li></ol></li><li data-uuid="cd3ba99c-9cec-435b-bc3a-72f15dac8d5d"><span>Specify the constraint (expression, attributes, etc.) in the element's body.</span></li><li data-uuid="c0f9e637-1bc9-457a-81d0-b787c5b4bf9a">Click the <strong>Synchronize </strong>button.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1"
