}</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Requiring constraints via the Drag and Drop handler in a view</h4><p>To require constraints via the Drag and Drop handler in a view</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="5023fec0-ec0e-4e65-a48c-59871c02230b">Drag and drop a constraint element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Drag and Drop handler" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Drag and Drop handler]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> on a requirement element symbol executing the <strong>Create Require Constraint</strong><strong> </strong>command. </li></ul><h4>Requiring constraints via the Specification panel</h4><p>To require constraints via the Specification panel</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="650e59a1-4e4e-4c4e-80c6-c755ba8c6b83">Drag and drop a constraint element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's Drag and Drop handler and drop it on the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><strong>Required Constraint</strong></span><strong> </strong>field in the Properties section of the Specification panel for the require constraint element. <span><br /></span>The appropriate constraint is specified for the element.</li></ul><h3>Requiring a constraint via the owned require constraint usage itself as the required constraint</h3><h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bdde8ebe-b12c-4ebf-9b0f-c906eb937ad9"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Requiring constraints via the Textual Editor1</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Requiring constraints via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To require constraints via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="77f2a1f0-d933-4c02-a092-844a84986c6e">In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the keyword <em>require constraint</em><span> for a require constraint usage</span></li><li data-uuid="4d73c64d-9e15-4d49-832f-95fdbc5af808"><span>Specify the element name.</span></li><li data-uuid="1ecd90e0-2492-4a3e-9760-2f066f934170">Specify the constraint (expression, attributes, etc.) in the element's body.</li><li data-uuid="89e390b4-ec24-42a3-9459-caa5534358f7">Click the <strong>Synchronize </strong>button.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="45267177-5718-40d9-a930-045919892f8a"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">requirement</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">MaximumMass</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">massActual</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">MassValue</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">massRequired</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">MassValue</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">require</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">constraint</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">mLimit</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {                   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// named constraint</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">massActual</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> &lt;= </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">massRequired</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Requiring constraints via the symbol compartment in a view</h4><p>For more information, see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Compartments" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Compartments]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</p><p>To require constraints via the symbol compartment in a view</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="22e460e7-4958-4c7e-8a01-3aefeaf2c79c">Do one of the following:<ol><li>Select an element symbol and click the<span> </span><strong>Create Element</strong><span> </span>button on it. In the dropdown menu, click the <strong>require constraint </strong>element.</li><li>Select an element symbol and on the right side of the <strong>require constraints </strong>compartment, click the<span> </span><strong>Create require constraint </strong>button.</li></ol></li><li data-uuid="7a7cac15-fcaf-4c3f-94d4-b97c16d04d64">Specify the constraint (expression, attributes, etc.).<br />The new element is created and displayed in the <strong>require constraints </strong><span>compartment.</span></li></ol><h4 class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d6205a71-0439-496c-8ecc-3e57da454333"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Requiring constraints via the Specification panel1</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Requiring constraints via the Specification panel</h4><p>To require constraints via the Specification panel</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="639e8d05-095e-4677-986c-d213b4692a9a"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Specification panel"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Specification panel" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Open the Specification panel]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li data-uuid="e0c54bdb-fabb-4ff9-8fa9-6721b7afbe46"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Do one of the following:</span><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the Properties section bar, click the <strong>Create Element</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_element_in_specification_pane_button.png" /></ac:image> button. The shortcut menu appears. </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Click the <strong>require constraint </strong>element in the shortcut menu.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the <strong>require constraints </strong>compartment section's bar, click the <strong>Create require constraint </strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_element_in_compartment_via_specification_pane_button.png" /></ac:image> button.</span></li></ol></li><li data-uuid="78dc9366-e6ad-4ec0-9ba0-88b509ebd810">Specify the constraint (expression, attributes, etc.).<br />The element is created in the <strong>require constraints </strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">compartment.</span></li></ol></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254423110 space=SYSML2P version=3 -->
## PAGE 00402: (2026x) Requirement and constraint evaluation

- page_id: `254423110`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254423110/2026x+Requirement+and+constraint+evaluation
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-12-17T15:17:24.747+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Requirements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Using views is the quickest way to evaluate multiple requirements and constraints at once. When the evaluation is complete, the view shapes are highlighted with icons or colors to indicate if requirements and constraints are satisfied or not. Failing and passing values are highlighted as well.You can also use the Containment tree,**SysML v2 Model Evaluation**console, or a view toevaluate individual satisfy requirements and assert constraints.

##### Evaluating requirements and constraints in views

To evaluate requirements and constraints in views

1. Open the view displaying the elements that have related requirements and constraints you want to evaluate.
2. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='evaluate_button.png'] in the view toolbar. To evaluate a single element or it's attribute displayed in a view, select the element shape or an attribute in the shape compartment before completing this step.To evaluate an individual satisfy requirement or assert constraint, select the element shape before completing this step. The evaluation result is *true* or *false*.

The view shapes are highlighted indicating whether related system requirements and constraints pass or fail.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A view with requirement/constraint evaluation results.

###### Evaluation results in views

The following table explains the requirement and constraint evaluation results displayed on view shapes

| Annotation | Description | Tooltip |
| --- | --- | --- |
| ORA shape or its header is colored in green | The annotated elements satisfy related system requirements or constraints. | N/A |
| ORA shape or its header is colored in yellow | The annotated elements indirectly fail related system requirements or constraints. This means that system requirements or constraints are failed by inner parts of the annotated element. | Hover over the icon to see which inner elements fail system requirements or constraints. |
| ORA shape or its header is colored in red | The annotated elements fail related system requirements or constraints. | Hover over the icon to see which requirement or constraint failed. |
|  | The annotated values satisfy related system requirements or constraints. | N/A |
|  | The annotated values fail related system requirements or constraints. | Hover over the value to see the text of the failing requirement. |

###### Clearing evaluation results in views

To clear evaluation results in a view

- In the view toolbar, click an arrow next to [ATTACHMENT filename='evaluate_button.png'] and select Clear Values .

###### Customizing visual representation of evaluation results

You can customize how requirement and constraint verification results are displayed in views.

To customize visual representation of evaluation results in a view

1. In the main menu, select Options > Project .
2. In the Project Options dialog, select the Evaluation option group.
3. Specify the following options:
  - Display Verification Results As - Select Display Icons to add icons to shape headers. Select Paint Headers to color shapes or their headers.
  - Propagate Deep Nested Failures - Set to true to indicate nested requirement and constraint failures. Set to false to only highlight the shapes of the elements that directly fail a requirement or constraint.
4. Click OK to close the Project Options dialog.

##### Evaluating requirements and constraints via the Containment tree

To evaluate a requirement or constraint via the Containment tree

- In the Containment tree, right-click a satisfy requirement or an assert constraint and select Evaluate . [ATTACHMENT filename='evaluating_requirement_via_containment_tree.png']

The evaluation result (*true* or *false*) is displayed in the**SysML v2 Model Evaluation**console.

##### Evaluating requirements and constraints via the SysML v2 Model Evaluation console

To evaluate a requirement or constraint via the **SysML v2 Model Evaluation** console

1. Select Window > SysML v2 Model Evaluation to open the SysML v2 Model Evaluation console.
2. In the input box of the console, type the qualified name of the satisfy requirement or assert constraint you want to evaluate and press Enter. To save time, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] in the toolbar of the **SysML v2 Model Evaluation** console to enable context selection in the Containment tree. When context selection is enabled, select the owner the element you want to evaluate and enter only the element's name to evaluate it. [ATTACHMENT filename='evaluating_requirement_via_console.png']

The evaluation result (*true*or*false*) is displayed in the**SysML v2 Model Evaluation**console.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

<strong>Project</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Project Options</strong>  dialog, select the <strong>Evaluation</strong> option group.</li><li>Specify the following options:<ul><li><strong>Display Verification Results As</strong> - Select <strong>Display Icons</strong> to add icons to shape headers. Select <strong>Paint Headers</strong> to color shapes or their headers.</li><li><strong>Propagate Deep Nested Failures</strong> - Set to <em>true</em> to indicate nested requirement and constraint failures. Set to <em>false</em> to only highlight the shapes of the elements that directly fail a requirement or constraint.</li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> to close the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog.</li></ol><h3>Evaluating requirements and constraints via the Containment tree</h3><p>To evaluate a requirement or constraint via the Containment tree</p><hr /><ul><li>In the Containment tree, right-click a satisfy requirement or an assert constraint and select <strong>Evaluate</strong>.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="evaluating_requirement_via_containment_tree.png" /></ac:image></li></ul><p><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The evaluation result (<em>true</em> or <em>false</em>) is displayed in the </span><strong>SysML v2 Model Evaluation</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span>console.</span></p><h3><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Evaluating requirements and constraints via the SysML v2 Model Evaluation console</span></h3><p>To evaluate a requirement or constraint via the <strong>SysML v2 Model Evaluation</strong> console</p><hr /><ol><li>Select <strong>Window</strong> &gt; <strong>SysML v2 Model Evaluation</strong> to open the <strong>SysML v2 Model Evaluation</strong> console.</li><li>In the input box of the console, type the qualified name of the satisfy requirement or assert constraint you want to evaluate and press Enter.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e7ef7365-c601-472f-a96c-786fb881295e"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To save time, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="16"><ri:attachment ri:filename="context_selection_icon.png" /></ac:image> in the toolbar of the <strong>SysML v2 Model Evaluation</strong> console to enable context selection in the Containment tree. When context selection is enabled, select the owner the element you want to evaluate and enter only the element's name to evaluate it.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><br /><ac:image ac:height="242"><ri:attachment ri:filename="evaluating_requirement_via_console.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><p><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The evaluation result (<em>true</em><span> </span>or<span> </span><em>false</em>) is displayed in the<span> </span></span><strong>SysML v2 Model Evaluation</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span>console.</span></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254423058 space=SYSML2P version=2 -->
## PAGE 00403: (2026x) Requirement constraints

- page_id: `254423058`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254423058/2026x+Requirement+constraints
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-10-10T14:33:19.769+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Requirements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Constraints allow you to specify the requirement eitherinformally using textstatements (commonly known as "shall" statements) or more formally using constraint expressions.

A requirement constraint is built from two sets of other constraints: the assumed and required constraints of the requirement.

- A [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Require constraint' space='']**require** constraint states the required constraints imposed on the model, expressed formally or informally in the requirement.
- An [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Assume constraint' space='']**assume** constraint states the assumed constraints imposed on the model, expressed formally or informally in the requirement.

Required and assumed constraints are declared as composite constraint usages in the body of a requirement definition or usage. The required constraints of a requirement only apply if all the assumed constraints are satisfied. A requirement is satisfied for a subject only when both constraints are true.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span>Constraints allow you to specify the requirement either </span><span>informally using text </span>statements (commonly known as &quot;shall&quot; statements) or more formally using constraint expressions.</p><p>A requirement constraint is built from two sets of other constraints: the assumed and required constraints of the requirement. </p><ul><li data-uuid="ac9a999e-f48d-4a9d-8a79-7e7377c01429">A <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Require constraint" /><ac:link-body><strong>require</strong> constraint</ac:link-body></ac:link> states the required constraints imposed on the model, expressed formally or informally in the requirement.</li><li data-uuid="2a5fc394-b524-4dc0-953a-8022c29f99dd">An <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Assume constraint" /><ac:link-body><strong>assume</strong> constraint</ac:link-body></ac:link> states the assumed constraints imposed on the model, expressed formally or informally in the requirement.</li></ul><p>Required and assumed constraints are declared as composite constraint usages in the body of a requirement definition or usage. The required constraints of a requirement only apply if all the assumed constraints are satisfied. A requirement is satisfied for a subject only when both constraints are true.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254423073 space=SYSML2P version=3 -->
## PAGE 00404: (2026x) Requirement decomposition

- page_id: `254423073`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254423073/2026x+Requirement+decomposition
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-12-03T08:53:19.219+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Requirements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A requirement definition or usage may be decomposed into a requirements tree of nested requirement usages (subrequirements), which may themselves be further decomposed. Any nested requirement usage is automatically considered to be a required constraint of the containing requirement definition or usage, i.e., nested requirements must be satisfied for the containing requirement to be satisfied. Requirements can be grouped either by composition, or reference.

##### Decomposing requirement definitions

You can represent the next level of decomposition of a requirement definition using the feature typing (*defined by*) relationship.

###### Decomposing requirement definitions via the Textual Editor

To decompose requirement definitions via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, identify the requirement usage that you want to define, nested within a requirement definition.
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Feature typing' space=''] . requirementdefVehicleSpecification{ 
requirementfullVehicleMassLimit:MassRequirement;// requirement usage, owned by a requirement definition, defined by another requirement definition 
requirementemptyVehicleMassLimit:MassRequirement; 
}requirementdefMassRequirement;

###### Displaying decompositions of requirement definitions in a view

To display decompositions of requirement definitions in a view

- In a view, select the requirement definition symbol and the requirement usage symbol it defines, then use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Displaying elements in symbolic views' space=''] command under the In-Between Relationships category . The feature typing relationship is displayed.

##### Decomposing composite requirement usages

You can display a requirements tree, i.e., how a requirement is composed of other requirements, which can be done by one of the following options:

- composition - defines owned subrequirements via the feature membership relationship. In the textual notation, subrequirements are nested in the body between the curly braces of the owning requirement.
- reference - defines requirements not owned by the overall requirement, which only references other requirements as subrequirements. In this case, subrequirements are modeled as required constraints in graphical and textual notations.

###### Decomposing composite requirement usages via the Textual Editor

To decompose composite requirement usages via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, within the body of the requirement you want to decompose, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating requirements' space=''] .
2. Click the Synchronize button. requirement vehicleSpecification:VehicleSpecification{ 
 requirement fullVehicleMassLimit : MassRequirement; // the owning requirement usage with nested requirement usages 
 requirement emptyVehicleMassLimit : MassRequirement; 
}

###### Decomposing composite requirement usages via a view by creating a composite requirement

To decompose composite requirement usages via a view by creating a composite requirement

1. In a view, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating requirements' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Displaying elements in symbolic views' space=''] the requirement for which you want to create a composite requirement.
2. Create an owned requirement in one of the following ways:
  1. Via the view palette (requirement symbol option):
    1. In the view palette, under the Requirements group, click the requirement button to create a requirement usage element.
    2. Click on the requirement symbol displayed in the view pane to create a requirement owned by another.
  2. Via the view palette (feature membership option):
    1. In the view palette, under the Other Relationships group, click the feature membership button.
    2. First, click on the requirement symbol displayed in the view pane and then click anywhere in the view pane to create a requirement owned by another.
  3. Via the smart manipulator (feature membership option):
    1. Click the requirement symbol for which you want to create a nested requirement.
    2. In its smart manipulator, click the feature membership icon.
    3. Click anywhere in the view pane to create a requirement owned by another.

###### Decomposing composite requirement usages via a view by designating an existing requirement as a composite one

To decompose composite requirement usages via a view by designating an existing requirement as a composite one

1. In a view, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating requirements' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Displaying elements in symbolic views' space=''] the requirements you want to designate as the owner and the owned.
2. Designate an existing requirement as owned by another in one of the following ways:
  1. Via the view palette (feature membership option):
    1. In the view palette, under the Other Relationships group, click the feature membership button.
    2. First, click on the requirement symbol you want to designate as the owner, then the one you want to designate as the owned one.
  2. Via the smart manipulator (feature membership option):
    1. Click the requirement symbol you want to designate as the owner.
    2. In its smart manipulator, click the feature membership icon, then click on the requirement you want to designate as the owned one.

###### Displaying decompositions of composite requirement usages in a view

To display decompositions of composite requirement usages in a view

- In a view, select the owning requirement usage symbol and use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Displaying elements in symbolic views' space=''] command under the Tree-View Style category. The feature membership and the owned features (requirement usages) are displayed.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Decomposing referencial requirement usages

In addition to having owned subrequirements, a requirement can also reference other requirements.

###### Decomposing referencial requirement usages via the Textual Editor

To decompose referencial requirement usages via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, within the curly braces of the requirement body within which you want to reference other existing requirements, declare the *require* keyword.
2. Follow with the name of the requirement you want to reference.
3. Click the **Synchronize**button. requirementvehicleSpecification:VehicleSpecification{ // the grouping requirement 
doc/* Overall vehicle requirements group */ 
subjectvehicle:Vehicle; 
actorenvironment:Environment; 
requirefullVehicleMassLimit; // the requirement *fullVehicleMassLimit* is grouped by reference via the *require* keyword 
requireemptyVehicleMassLimit; // the requirement *emptyVehicleMassLimit* is grouped by referencevia the *require* keyword 
}// The subject of the following subrequirements are implicitly bound to the subject "vehicle" of the containing requirement.requirement fullVehicleMassLimit : MassRequirement { // the requirement that is grouped via the *require* keyword 
 attribute :>> massRequired = 2000 [kilogram]; 
} 
requirement emptyVehicleMassLimit : MassRequirement { // the requirement that is grouped via the *require* keyword; 
 attribute :>> massRequired = 1500 [kilogram]; 
}

###### Decomposing referencial requirement usages via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

To decompose referencial requirement usages via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

- Drag and drop a requirement element or use its symbol's Drag and Drop handler on another requirement element symbol using the Create Require Constraint command. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Drag and Drop handler' space=''] page.

###### Displaying decompositions of referencial requirement usages in a view

The required constraints are displayed in the requirement symbol's compartment 'require constraints'. However, you can also display them separately.

To display decompositions of referencial requirement usages in a view

- In a view, select the owning requirement usage symbol and use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Displaying elements in symbolic views' space=''] command. The requirements, feature memberships, and required constraints are displayed. **[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

##### Reusing and modifying the requirement tree

A requirement can subset another requirement facilitating requirement tree reuse and modification. The subsetting requirement inherits the requirement decomposition and other features. You can then modify the inherited requirements and features through redefinition and subsetting in addition to adding requirements.

###### Reusing and modifying a requirement tree via the Textual Editor

To reuse and modify a requirement tree via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating requirements' space=''] you want to reuse, then [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Subsetting' space=''] it.
2. (Optional) [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Redefinition' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Subsetting' space=''] the requirement features in the element body.
3. Click the Synchronize button.

requirementvehicleSpecification:VehicleSpecification{// subsetted (reused) requirement 
requirementfullVehicleMassLimit: MassRequirement; 
requirementemptyVehicleMassLimit: MassRequirement; 
}

requirementnewVehicleSpecification:>vehicleSpecification{// a requirement reuses the requirement tree by subsetting another requirement 
requirementfullNewVehicleMassLimit:>fullVehicleMassLimit; 
}

###### Reusing a requirement tree via the view palette

To reuse a requirement tree via the view palette

1. In a view, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating requirements' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Displaying elements in symbolic views' space=''] the requirement whose decomposition you want to reuse.
2. In the view palette, under the Specializations group, click the subsetting button.
3. Click on the requirement you want to denote as the subsetting one and then click on the requirement you want to denote as the subsetted (reused) one.

###### Displaying the requirement tree reuse in a view

To display the requirement tree reuse in a view

1. In a view, select the subsetting and the subsetted requirement usage symbols, then u se the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Displaying elements in symbolic views' space=''] command under the In-Between Relationships category . The subsetting relationship is displayed.
2. (Optional) To display owned features of the subsetting or subsetted requirements, use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Displaying elements in symbolic views' space=''] command. The owned features (requirement usages) are displayed. [ATTACHMENT filename='Displaying the requirement tree reuse on a diagram.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">vehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Vehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">actor</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">environment</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Environment</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">require</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fullVehicleMassLimit</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;         <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the requirement <em>fullVehicleMassLimit</em> is grouped by reference via the <em>require</em> keyword</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">require</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">emptyVehicleMassLimit</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;    <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the requirement <em>emptyVehicleMassLimit</em> is grouped <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">by reference </span>via the <em>require</em> keyword</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}   </span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// The subject of the following subrequirements are implicitly bound to the subject &quot;vehicle&quot; of the containing requirement.<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">requirement</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fullVehicleMassLimit</span> : <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">MassRequirement</span> {         <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the requirement that is grouped via the <em>require</em> keyword</span><br />   <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span> :&gt;&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">massRequired</span> = <span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">2000</span> [<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">kilogram</span>];<br />}<br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">requirement</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">emptyVehicleMassLimit</span> : <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">MassRequirement</span> {    <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the requirement that is grouped via the <em>require</em> keyword; </span><br />   <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span> :&gt;&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">massRequired</span> = <span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">1500</span> [<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">kilogram</span>];<br />}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h4>Decomposing referencial requirement usages via the Drag and Drop handler in a view</h4><p>To decompose referencial requirement usages via the Drag and Drop handler in a view</p><hr /><ul><li>Drag and drop a requirement element or use its symbol's Drag and Drop handler on another requirement element symbol using the<span> </span><strong>Create Require Constraint </strong>command.<span> </span><span>See the</span><span> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Drag and Drop handler" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Drag and Drop handler]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span> </span><span>page.</span></li></ul><h4>Displaying decompositions of referencial requirement usages in a view</h4><p>The required constraints are displayed in the requirement symbol's compartment 'require constraints'. However, you can also display them separately.</p><p>To display decompositions of referencial requirement usages in a view</p><hr /><ul><li>In a view, select the owning requirement usage symbol and use the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying elements in symbolic views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Features]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </strong>command.<br />The requirements, feature memberships, and required constraints are displayed. <br /><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"><strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Displaying decompositions of referencial requirement usages on a diagram 2.png" /></ac:image></strong></span></li></ul><h3>Reusing and modifying the requirement tree</h3><p>A requirement can subset another requirement facilitating requirement tree reuse and modification. The subsetting requirement inherits the requirement decomposition and other features. You can then modify the inherited requirements and features through redefinition and subsetting in addition to adding requirements.</p><h4>Reusing and modifying a requirement tree via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To reuse and modify a requirement tree via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Textual Editor, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating requirements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[declare the requirement usage]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> you want to reuse, then <ac:link ac:anchor="Subsetting a usage via the Textual Editor"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Subsetting" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[subset]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> it.</li><li>(Optional) <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Redefinition" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Redefine]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Subsetting" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[subset]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> the requirement features in the element body. </li><li>Click the <strong>Synchronize<span> </span></strong>button.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8811e49e-ed80-4671-a9c5-db96a9276d25"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">requirement</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">vehicleSpecification </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">: </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">VehicleSpecification </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">{</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);">    <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// subsetted (reused) requirement</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">requirement</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fullVehicleMassLimit</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : MassRequirement;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">   </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">requirement</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">emptyVehicleMassLimit</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : MassRequirement;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">requirement</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">newVehicleSpecification</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">vehicleSpecification</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);">  <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// a requirement reuses the requirement tree by subsetting another requirement</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">requirement</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fullNewVehicleMassLimit</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fullVehicleMassLimit</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);">  </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Reusing a requirement tree via the view palette</h4><p>To reuse a requirement tree via the view palette</p><hr /><ol><li>In a view, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating requirements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[create]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying elements in symbolic views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[display]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> the requirement whose decomposition you want to reuse.</li><li>In the view palette, under the Specializations group, click the <strong>subsetting </strong>button.</li><li>Click on the requirement you want to denote as the subsetting one and then click on the requirement you want to denote as the subsetted (reused) one.</li></ol><h4>Displaying the requirement tree reuse in a view</h4><p>To display the requirement tree reuse in a view</p><hr /><ol><li>In a view, select the subsetting and the subsetted requirement usage symbols, then u<span>se the </span><strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying elements in symbolic views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Specializations]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </strong><span>command under the </span><strong>In-Between Relationships category</strong><span>. The subsetting relationship is displayed.</span></li><li>(Optional) To display owned features of the subsetting or subsetted requirements, use the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying elements in symbolic views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Features]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </strong>command. The owned features (requirement usages) are displayed.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Displaying the requirement tree reuse on a diagram.png" /></ac:image></li></ol>

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This section provides procedures for modeling requirements.

````html
<p>This section provides procedures for modeling requirements. </p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254423086 space=SYSML2P version=8 -->
## PAGE 00406: (2026x) Satisfy requirement

- page_id: `254423086`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254423086/2026x+Satisfy+requirement
- version_number: 8
- version_date: `2025-12-11T19:24:01.008+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Requirements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Requirement satisfaction is modeled via the*satisfy requirement usage*element, which asserts that a requirement is satisfied when a given feature is bound to the subject parameter of the requirement. Other parameters or features of the requirement may also be bound in the body of the satisfy requirement usage.A satisfy requirement usage can also be negated. A negated satisfy requirement usage asserts that some entity does not satisfy the given requirement.

There are two ways a requirement can be satisfied by another element:

50%

- by referencing the requirement usage defined elsewhere in the model.
  - The element symbol is displayed with the satisfy keyword (unnamed element) or the satisfy requirement keyword (named element) . [ATTACHMENT filename='Satisfy_requirement_2.png'] [ATTACHMENT filename='Satisfy_requirement_1.png']
  - See the procedures:
    - :
      - 
      - 

50%

- by containing an owned satisfy requirement usage, i.e., the satisfied requirement is the satisfy requirement usage itself.
  - The element symbol is displayed with the satisfy requirement keyword. [ATTACHMENT filename='Satisfy_requirement_3.png']
  - See the procedures:
    - :
      - 
      - 
      - 

##### Satisfying a requirement by referencing a requirement usage defined elsewhere in the model

###### Satisfying requirements via the Textual Editor

To satisfy a requirement via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor within the curly braces of the part element and do one of the following:
  1. For an unnamed satisfy requirement usage, declare the *satisfy* keyword followed by the name of the requirement the part satisfies.
  2. For a named satisfy requirement usage, declare the *satisfy requirement* keyword, specify the element name, then [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Reference subsetting' space=''] the requirement the part satisfies. To negate a satisfy requirement usage, specify the *not*keyword before the satisfy keyword, e.g., *not satisfy*.
2. (Optional) To specify a requirement with an explicit satisfying feature, state the by keyword and the name of the subject element that satisfies the requirement.
3. Click the **Synchronize**button. requirementvehicleSpecification{ 
subjectvehicle:Vehicle; 
actorenvironment:Environment; 
requirementfullVehicleMassLimit:MassRequirement{ 
subjectvehicle:Vehicle; 
attribute:>>massRequired=2000[kilogram]; 
} 
}// step 1.a - unnamed satisfy requirement usage with an implicit satisfying featurepartvehicle_c1:Vehicle{ 
satisfyvehicleSpecification.fullVehicleMassLimit;// the satisfying feature is implicitly the part *vehicle_c1* 
}// step 1.b - named satisfy requirement usage with an implicit satisfying featurepartvehicle_c1:Vehicle{ 
satisfyrequirementsatisfiedReq::>vehicleSpecification.fullVehicleMassLimit;// the satisfying feature is implicitly the part *vehicle_c1*}// step 2 - unnamed satisfy requirement usage with an explicit satisfying featurepartvehicle:Vehicle{ 
partvehicle_c2:Vehicle; 
satisfyvehicleSpecification.fullVehicleMassLimitbyvehicle_c2; 
}// negated satisfy requirement usage 
partvehicle_c1:Vehicle{ 
notsatisfyvehicleSpecification.fullVehicleMassLimit; 
}

###### Satisfying requirements via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

To satisfy a requirement via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

- Drag and drop a requirement element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Drag and Drop handler' space=''] on a part element symbol that satisfies the requirement executing the Create Satisfy Requirement command.

##### Satisfying a requirementvia the ownedsatisfy requirementusageitselfas thesatisfied requirement

###### Satisfying requirements via the Textual Editor2Satisfying requirements via the Textual Editor

To satisfy a requirement via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor within the curly braces of the part element and do one of the following:
  1. To specify a requirement without the satisfying feature, declare the satisfy requirement keyword followed by the qualified name of the requirement the part satisfies.
  2. To specify a requirement with the satisfying feature declare the satisfy requirement keyword followed by the qualified name of the satisfied requirement, then state the by keyword and the name of the subject element that satisfies the requirement. To negate a satisfy requirement usage, specify the *not* keyword before the satisfy keyword, e.g., *not satisfy*.
2. Click the **Synchronize**button. partvehicle_c1:Vehicle{ 
satisfy requirementvehicleSpecification;// satisfied requirement is the defined satisfy requirement usage itself contained by the parent part that is the implicit satisfying feature 
}

###### Satisfying requirements via the Create Element command

To satisfy a requirement via the Create Element command

1. In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click **Create Element**.
2. In the Create Element dialog, select satisfy requirement .
3. Name the element.
4. (Optional) Specify the satisfy requirement.

###### Satisfying requirements via the view palette

To satisfy a requirement via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Requirements/Constraints group, click satisfy requirement .
2. Click in the view pane where you want to create the element symbol.
3. (Optional) Specify the satisfy requirement.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span>not</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">satisfy</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h4>Satisfying requirements via the Drag and Drop handler in a view</h4><p class="auto-cursor-target">To satisfy a requirement via the Drag and Drop handler in a view</p><hr /><ul><li>Drag and drop a requirement element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Drag and Drop handler" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Drag and Drop handler]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> on a part element symbol that satisfies the requirement executing the <strong>Create Satisfy Requirement</strong> command.</li></ul><h3>Satisfying a requirement<span> </span><span>via the owned </span>satisfy requirement<span> </span><span>usage </span><span>itself </span><span>as the </span><span>satisfied requirement</span></h3><h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d1743b7e-ed24-41e9-8b0d-8ef738c0b766"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Satisfying requirements via the Textual Editor2</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Satisfying requirements via the Textual Editor</h4><p class="auto-cursor-target">To satisfy a requirement via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Textual Editor, place the cursor within the curly braces of the part element and do one of the following:<ol><li>To specify a requirement without the satisfying feature, declare the <em>satisfy requirement </em>keyword followed by the qualified name of the requirement the part satisfies. </li><li>To specify a requirement with the satisfying feature declare the <em>satisfy requirement </em>keyword followed by the qualified name of the satisfied requirement, then state the <em>by </em>keyword and the name of the subject element that satisfies the requirement. <ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="165cb277-93b5-4472-ab56-56acb347e9e1"><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target">To negate a satisfy requirement usage, specify the <em>not</em> keyword before the satisfy keyword, e.g., <em>not satisfy</em>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></li><li><span>Click the <strong>Synchronize </strong>button.</span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="11412740-abbe-4304-92f9-b5eb0e976c7d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">vehicle_c1</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Vehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span>satisfy requirement </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">vehicleSpecification</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// satisfied requirement is the defined satisfy requirement usage itself contained by the parent part that is the implicit satisfying feature</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h4>Satisfying requirements via the Create Element command</h4><p>To satisfy a requirement via the Create Element command</p><hr /><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click <strong>Create Element</strong>.</p></li><li>In the <strong>Create Element</strong> dialog, select <strong>satisfy requirement</strong>.</li><li>Name the element.</li><li>(Optional) Specify the satisfy requirement.</li></ol><h4>Satisfying requirements via the view palette</h4><p>To satisfy a requirement via the view palette</p><hr /><ol><li>In the view palette, under the Requirements/Constraints group, click <strong>satisfy requirement</strong>.<strong> </strong></li><li>Click in the view pane where you want to create the element symbol.</li><li>(Optional) Specify the satisfy requirement.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254422138 space=SYSML2P version=2 -->
## PAGE 00407: (2026x) Saving as image

- page_id: `254422138`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254422138/2026x+Saving+as+image
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-10-14T15:12:12.984+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model management > (2026x) Working with views > (2026x) View image export
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can save an image as an active diagram, selected symbols, or selected diagrams.

Diagrams and symbols that were created in the model can be saved as images in the following formats:

- Enhanced Metafile Format (*.emf)
- Encapsulated PostScript (*.eps)
- Joint Photographic Experts Group (*.jpg, *.jpeg)
- Portable Network Graphics (*.png)
- Scalable Vector Graphics (*.svg)
- Tagged Image File Format (*.tif, *.tiff)
- Windows Metafile Format (*.wmf)

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
The Save As Image dialog when the Selected diagrams option button is selected.

##### Saving an image

To save an image

1. Open the diagram you want to save as an image. If you want to save only particular symbols, select them on the diagram.
2. On the main menu, select File > Save as Image . The Save As Image dialog opens.
3. In the dialog:
  - Select whether to save an **Active diagram**, **Selected symbols**, or **Selected diagrams**. Selected diagrams**Selected diagrams** allow you to search for a particular diagram in your model.
  - Specify the directory you want to save the image to.
  - Select the image format. Click Options to specify image options according to the selected format.
4. Click Save . The image is saved in the directory you have specified.

##### Save As Image dialog elements

| Dialog element | Element type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Active diagram | Option button | Select to save the currently active diagram as an image. The active diagram name is displayed in parentheses next to the option button name. |
| Selected symbols | Option button | Select to save specific symbols that are selected in the diagram as an image. The option is active only if one or multiple symbols are selected in the diagram. |
| Selected diagrams | Option button | Select the option to save the diagrams selected in the diagram list as images. To select multiple diagrams, hold Shift and then select the needed diagrams with the click of the mouse. |
| Select diagrams | Text box | The text box is available only if the Selected diagrams option button is selected.In the Select diagrams box, type the name of the diagram you want to find. Search results are displayed as a list right under the text box. Select the diagram(s) you want to save as image(s).To optimize your search, use . |
| Image File / Working Directory | Text box | Select the working directory in which the image will be saved. |
| Image Format | Drop-down list | Select the image format. |
| Options | Button | Click to open the Properties dialog where you can specify image export properties, such as image resolution and exported image size. The properties provided in the Properties dialog depend on the selected image format. |
| Overwrite existing files | Check box | Select to overwrite already saved image files. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can save an image as an active diagram, selected symbols, or selected diagrams.</p><p>Diagrams and symbols that were created in the model can be saved as images in the following formats:</p><ul><li>Enhanced Metafile Format (*.emf)</li><li>Encapsulated PostScript (*.eps)</li><li>Joint Photographic Experts Group (*.jpg, *.jpeg)</li><li>Portable Network Graphics (*.png)</li><li>Scalable Vector Graphics (*.svg)</li><li>Tagged Image File Format (*.tif, *.tiff)</li><li>Windows Metafile Format (*.wmf)</li></ul><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="save_as_image_dialog.png" /></ac:image><br />The Save As Image dialog when the Selected diagrams option button is selected.</h6><h3>Saving an image</h3><p>To save an image</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the diagram you want to save as an image. If you want to save only particular symbols, select them on the diagram.</li><li>On the main menu, select <strong>File </strong>&gt; <strong>Save as Image</strong>. The <strong>Save As Image</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>In the dialog:<ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Select whether to save an <strong>Active diagram</strong>, <strong>Selected symbols</strong>, or <strong>Selected diagrams</strong>. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f4d6f1f2-7008-40ac-aa9b-912d4c92a548"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Selected diagrams</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Selected diagrams</strong> allow you to search for a particular diagram in your model.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Specify the directory you want to save the image to.</li><li>Select the image format. Click <strong>Options </strong>to specify image options according to the selected format.</li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>Save</strong>. The image is saved in the directory you have specified.</li></ol><h3>Save As Image dialog elements</h3><table class="wrapped"><colgroup class=""><col class="" /><col class="" /><col class="" /></colgroup><tbody class=""><tr class=""><th>Dialog element</th><th colspan="1">Element type</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>Active diagram</strong></td><td colspan="1">Option button</td><td><p>Select to save the currently active diagram as an image. The active diagram name is displayed in parentheses next to the option button name.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>Selected symbols</strong></td><td colspan="1">Option button</td><td><p>Select to save specific symbols that are selected in the diagram as an image. The option is active only if one or multiple symbols are selected in the diagram.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p><strong>Selected diagrams</strong></p></td><td colspan="1">Option button</td><td><p>Select the option to save the diagrams selected in the diagram list as images. To select multiple diagrams, hold Shift and then select the needed diagrams with the click of the mouse.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>Select diagrams</strong></td><td colspan="1">Text box</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>The text box is available only if the <strong>Selected diagrams</strong> option button is selected.</p><p>In the <strong>Select diagrams</strong> box, type the name of the diagram you want to find. Search results are displayed as a list right under the text box. Select the diagram(s) you want to save as image(s).</p><p>To optimize your search, use <ac:link ac:anchor="Quick Find dialog elements"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Quick find" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[the search filters]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></div></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p><strong>Image File / Working Directory</strong></p></td><td colspan="1">Text box</td><td>Select the working directory in which the image will be saved.</td></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>Image Format</strong></td><td colspan="1">Drop-down list</td><td>Select the image format.</td></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>Options</strong></td><td colspan="1">Button</td><td><p>Click to open the <strong>Properties dialog</strong> where you can specify image export properties, such as image resolution and exported image size. The properties provided in the <strong>Properties</strong> dialog depend on the selected image format.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p><strong>Overwrite existing files</strong></p></td><td colspan="1">Check box</td><td>Select to overwrite already saved image files.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254422795 space=SYSML2P version=2 -->
## PAGE 00408: (2026x) Send action

- page_id: `254422795`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254422795/2026x+Send+action
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-10-14T15:21:55.567+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Behavior > (2026x) Actions > (2026x) Transfers between actions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A send action usage is declared as an action usage whose behavior is to transfer the payload from the sender to the receiver. It has three input parameters:

1. A set of payload values, identified by the send keyword.
2. A sender occurrence, identified by the via keyword.
3. A receiver occurrence, identified by the to keyword.

**Sending context**

A send action usage can specify both a sender (*via*) and receiver (*to*), but it will generally give only one or the other:

- If a send action usage is directly or indirectly a composite feature of a part definition or usage, then the default for the sender ( via ) of the send action usage is the containing part.
- If a send action usage is not a composite feature of a part definition or usage, then the default for the sender ( via ) of the send action usage is the highest-level containing action usage.
- If a send action usage is contained by a perform action, then the default for the sender ( via ) of the send action usage is the perform action, not the part or the higher-level action.
- If sending through a port, the port usage will usually be the sender ( via ). The receiver ( to ) is then determined by the port interface connection.

##### Specifying send actions for actions

###### Specifying send actions for actions via the Textual Editor

To specify send actions for actions via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the send action and do one of the following:
  1. For a named send action usage, declare the keyword action , specify the element name, and then the keyword send .
  2. For an unnamed send action usage, declare the keyword send .
2. Follow with the expression for the payload.
3. Follow with the needed option(s):
  1. Specify the via keyword and the expression for the sender.
  2. Specify the to keyword and the expression for the receiver.
4. Click the Synchronize button.

// ------------ sending without ports; the sender is the part 'camera', the receiver is the part 'screen'

partcamera:Camera{ 
actiontakePicture:TakePicture{ 
actiontriggeracceptscene:Scene; 
thenactionfocus:Focus{ 
inscene=trigger.scene; 
outimage; 
} 
flowfocus.imagetoshoot.image; 
thenactionshoot:Shoot{ 
inimage; 
outpicture; 
} 
// named send action 'displayPicture' transferring the payload 'shoot.picture' from the sender 'camera' to the receiver 'screen': 
thenactiondisplayPicturesendshoot.picturetoscreen; 
// unnamed send action usage: 
 then send shoot.picture to screen; 
} 
}

// ------------ sending through ports; the sender is the port 'displayPort'

partcamera:Camera{ 
portviewPort; 
portdisplayPort;actiontakePicture:TakePicture{ 
actiontriggeracceptscene:SceneviaviewPort; 
thenactionfocus:Focus{ 
inscene=trigger.scene; 
outimage; 
} 
flowfocus.imagetoshoot.image; 
thenactionshoot:Shoot{ 
inimage; 
outpicture; 
} 
 // named send action 'displayPicture' transferring the payload 'shoot.picture' from the sender 'displayPort':thenactiondisplayPicturesendshoot.picturevia displayPort;// unnamed send action usage:then send shoot.picture via displayPort; 
} 
}

###### Specifying send actions for actions via the view palette

To specify send actions for actions via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Other Actions group, click the send button to create a send action usage element.
2. Click on an action symbol in the view pane where you want to create the send symbol.
3. (Optional) Specify the element's parameters via the Textual Editor.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A send action usage is declared as an action usage whose behavior is to transfer the payload from the sender to the receiver. It has three input parameters:</p><ol><li>A set of <strong>payload</strong> values, identified by the <em>send </em>keyword. </li><li>A <strong>sender</strong> occurrence, identified by the <em>via </em>keyword. </li><li>A <strong>receiver</strong> occurrence, identified by the <em>to </em>keyword. </li></ol><p><strong>Sending context</strong></p><p>A send action usage can specify both a sender (<em>via</em>) and receiver (<em>to</em>), but it will generally give only one or the other:</p><ul><li>If a send action usage is directly or indirectly a composite feature of a part definition or usage, then the default for the sender (<em>via</em>) of the send action usage is the containing part.</li><li>If a send action usage is not a composite feature of a part definition or usage, then the default for the sender (<em>via</em>) of the send action usage is the highest-level containing action usage.</li><li>If a send action usage is contained by a perform action, then the default for the sender (<em>via</em>) of the send action usage is the perform action, not the part or the higher-level action.</li><li>If sending through a port, the port usage will usually be the sender (<em>via</em>). The receiver (<em>to</em>) is then determined by the port interface connection.</li></ul><h3>Specifying send actions for actions</h3><h4>Specifying send actions for actions via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To specify send actions for actions via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target" style="text-align: left;">In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the send action and do one of the following:</p><ol><li>For a named send action usage, declare the keyword <em>action</em>, specify the element name, and then the keyword <em>send</em>.</li><li>For an unnamed send action usage, declare the keyword <em>send</em>.</li></ol></li><li>Follow with the expression for the payload.</li><li>Follow with the needed option(s):<ol><li>Specify the <em>via </em>keyword and the expression for the sender.</li><li>Specify the <em>to </em>keyword and the expression for the receiver.</li></ol></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Click the </span><strong style="text-align: left;">Synchronize<span> </span></strong><span the view palette</h4><p>To specify send actions for actions via the view palette</p><hr /><ol><li>In the view palette, under the Other Actions group, click the <strong>send </strong>button to create a send action usage element.</li><li>Click on an action symbol in the view pane where you want to create the send symbol.</li><li>(Optional)<span> </span>Specify the element's parameters via the Textual Editor.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=314182528 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00409: (2026x) Snapshot

- page_id: `314182528`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/314182528/2026x+Snapshot
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-07-01T14:57:21.851+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Common elements > (2026x) Occurrences
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A snapshot is a type of suboccurrence. It is a time slice with zero duration, representing an occurrence at a specific point in time.

##### Specifying or creating elements as snapshots

###### Specifying or creating elements as snapshotsvia the Textual Editor

To specify or create elements as snapshots via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, declare the *snapshot* keyword prior to the declaration of the element that you want to specify as a snapshot.
2. Click the **Synchronize**button. snapshotpartcarInMotion;

###### Specifying elements as snapshots via the shortcut menu

To specify elements as snapshots via the shortcut menu

- Right-click a usage element in the Containment tree or its symbol in a view and in its shortcut menu, in the Properties group, click **Portion Kind**, and in its submenu, click **snapshot** .

###### Creating elements assnapshots via the view palette

To create elements as snapshots via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Items/Ports/Attributes group, do one of the following:
  1. To create an occurrence usage snapshot or one that will correspond to the element kind for which the snapshot will be created:
    1. Click the**snapshot** button. If it is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to the **timeslice** button and click the **snapshot** button in the dropdown menu.
    2. Click in the view pane or the element symbol for which you want the snapshot to be created:
      1. Creating an element on the empty space of the view pane creates an occurrence usage element as a snapshot by default.
      2. Creating an element on another element symbol creates a snapshot for it:
        1. If the element symbol is a definition, the created snapshot is also a usage (e.g. part) that corresponds to the definition element for which the snapshot is created (e.g. part def).
        2. If the element is a usage, the created snapshot is also a usage of the same kind as the element for which the snapshot is created.
  2. To specify the snapshot's element kind:
    1. Click the down-facing arrow next to the **timeslice** button, and in its dropdown menu, click **any snapshot...** and select the specific element kind you need.
    2. Click in the view pane or the element symbol for which you want to create the snapshot.

If a snapshot element is created for an element specified as an individual, the snapshot is also created as an individual by default.

class="auto-cursor-target">To create an occurrence usage snapshot or one that will correspond to the element kind for which the snapshot will be created:</p><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Click the<strong> snapshot</strong> button. If it is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to the <strong>timeslice</strong> button and click the <strong>snapshot</strong> button in the dropdown menu.</span></p></li><li>Click in the view pane or the element symbol for which you want the snapshot to be created:<ol><li>Creating an element on the empty space of the view pane creates an occurrence usage element as a snapshot by default.</li><li>Creating an element on another element symbol creates a snapshot for it:<ol><li>If the element symbol is a definition, the created snapshot is also a usage (e.g. part) that corresponds to the definition element for which the snapshot is created (e.g. part def). </li><li>If the element is a usage, the created snapshot is also a usage of the same kind as the element for which the snapshot is created. </li></ol></li></ol></li></ol></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">To specify the snapshot's element kind: </span><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Click the down-facing arrow next to the <strong>timeslice</strong> button, and in its dropdown menu, click <strong>any snapshot...</strong> and select the specific element kind you need.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Click in the view pane or the element symbol for which you want to create the snapshot.</span></li></ol></li></ol></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="02a032b8-b933-44d0-8db4-da5b6716c2db"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If a snapshot element is created for an element specified as an individual, the snapshot is also created as an individual by default.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254422247 space=SYSML2P version=2 -->
## PAGE 00410: (2026x) Specializations

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This section provides procedures for modeling element specializations.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">This section provides procedures for modeling element specializations.</span><span> </span></p><p><br /></p>
````
