<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254422649 space=SYSML2P version=4 -->
## PAGE 00426: (2026x) Subjects, objectives, and verdicts for verification cases

- page_id: `254422649`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254422649/2026x+Subjects+objectives+and+verdicts+for+verification+cases
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2025-12-03T10:39:49.097+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Analysis & Verification > (2026x) Verification case
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A verification case is a kind of case whose result is a verdict on whether the subject of the case satisfies certain requirements.

- The subject of a verification case is an input parameter that identifies the system or other entity that is being evaluated as to whether it satisfies certain requirements.
- The objective of a verification case is to verify that the verification subject satisfies one or more specific requirements, which are specified as a special kind of required constraint in the objective.
  - In addition to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Requirement constraints' space=''] usages allowed in any requirement usage, the objective of a verification case may also have **requirement verification usages**, which indicate the requirements to be verified by the verification case.
- The return parameter called a verdict, which can be:
  - Pass indicates that the subject has been determined to satisfy the requirements to be verified.
  - Fail indicates that the subject has been determined not to satisfy the requirements to be verified.
  - Inconclusive indicates that a determination could not be made as to whether the subject satisfies the requirements to be verified.
  - Error indicates that an error occurred during the performance of the verification.

- A verification case can have only one subject, objective, and return parameter.
- A verification case always returns a verdict of type VerdictKind (in the library package VerificationCases ), which is an enumeration with allowed values pass , fail , inconclusive , and error .

#### Specifying subject, objective, and verdictfor verification case

###### Specifying subjects, objectives, and verdicts for verification cases via the Textual Editor

To specify subjects, objectives, and verdicts for verification cases via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, within the curly braces of the case's body, do the following:
  1. To specify a subject, declare the subject keyword, specify the element name , [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specializations' space=''] (optional).
  2. To specify an objective, declare the objective keyword, then in the element body, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Annotations' space=''] .
  3. To specify a verdict, declare the return keyword named verdict, defined by VerdictKind .
2. Click the Synchronize button. verificationdefVehicleMassTest{ 
subjecttestVehicle:Vehicle; 
objectivevehicleMassVerificationObjective{ 
verifyvehicleMassRequirement; 
} 
returnverdict:VerificationCases::VerdictKind; 
}

###### Specifying subjects and objectives for verification cases via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

To specify subjects and objectives for verification cases via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

- Drag and drop an element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's Drag and Drop handler on a verification case element symbol executing the **Create Subject**,**Create Objective**, or **Create Require Constraint of Objective** command.

###### Specifying subjects and objectives for verification cases via the symbol compartment in a view

For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Compartments' space=''] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Compartments' space=''] page.

To specify subjects and objectives for verification cases via the symbol compartment in a view

- Select a verification case symbol and click the Create Element button on it. In the dropdown menu, select the element you want to create. The new element is created and displayed in its appropriate compartment.

###### Specifying subjects and objectives for verification cases via the Specification panel

To specify subjects and objectives for verification cases via the Specification panel

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification panel' space=''].
2. In the Properties section bar, click the **Create Element** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The shortcut menu appears. Click the needed element kind in the shortcut menu. The element is created in the appropriate compartment.

#### Specifying requirement verification usages for verification case objective

In addition to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Requirement constraints' space=''] usages allowed in any requirement usage, the objective of a verification case may also have **requirement verification usages**, which indicate the requirements to be verified by the verification case. Since the requirement verification usage is a subrequirement of the objective, it is automatically considered a required constraint of its containing objective.

##### Creating new verification case objective-owned requirement verification usages

###### Creating new verification case objective-owned requirement verification usages via the Textual Editor

To create new verification case objective-owned requirement verification usages via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor within the curly braces of the verification case objective's body, declare the verify requirement keyword, specify the element name, then the requirement body.
2. Click the Synchronize button. verificationdefVehicleMassTest{ 
subjecttestVehicle:Vehicle; 
objectivevehicleMassVerificationObjective{ 
verifyrequirementvehicleMassRequirement{// new verification case objective-owned requirement verification usage 
subjectvehicle:Vehicle; 
inmassActual:>ISQ::mass=vehicle.mass; 
doc/* The vehicle mass shall be less than or equal to 2500 kg. */ 
requireconstraint{ 
massActual<=2500[SI::kg] 
} 
} 
} 
returnverdict:VerificationCases::VerdictKind; 
}

##### Reusing existing requirements as requirement verification usages for a verification case objective

###### Reusing existing requirements as requirement verification usages for a verification case objective via the Textual Editor

Reusing existing requirements as requirement verification usages for a verification case objective via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor within the curly braces of the verification case objective's body, do one of the following :
  1. To reuse a requirement usage, declare the verify keyword followed by the name of the requirement you want to reuse.
    1. If you want to name the new constraint usage, declare the verify requirement keyword, name the new usage, and then[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Reference subsetting' space='']the requirement you want to reuse.
  2. To reuse a requirement definition, declare the verify requirement keyword, name the new requirement usage (optional), and then [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Feature typing' space=''] it by the needed requirement definition.
2. (Optional) Specify the requirement in the element's body.
3. Click the Synchronize button. requirementdefVehicleMassRequirement{ 
//... 
}requirementvehicleMassRequirement{ 
subjectvehicle:Vehicle; 
inmassActual:>ISQ::mass=vehicle.mass; 
doc/* The vehicle mass shall be less than or equal to 2500 kg. */ 
requireconstraint{ 
massActual<=2500[SI::kg] 
} 
} 
verificationdefVehicleMassTest{ 
subjecttestVehicle:Vehicle; 
objectivevehicleMassVerificationObjective{ 
verifyvehicleMassRequirement;// step 1.a. unnamed requirement verification usage reference subsets another requirement usage 
} 
returnverdict:VerificationCases::VerdictKind; 
} 
verificationdefVehicleMassTest{ 
subjecttestVehicle:Vehicle; 
objectivevehicleMassVerificationObjective{ 
verifyrequirementvehicleMassRequirementForTest::>vehicleMassRequirement;// step 1.i. named requirement verification usage reference subsets another requirement usage 
} 
returnverdict:VerificationCases::VerdictKind; 
} 
verificationdefVehicleMassTest{ 
subjecttestVehicle:Vehicle; 
objectivevehicleMassVerificationObjective{ 
verifyrequirementvehicleMassRequirementForTest:VehicleMassRequirement;// step 1.b. named requirement verification usage is defined by a requirement definition 
} 
returnverdict:VerificationCases::VerdictKind; 
}

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">A verification case is a kind of case whose result is a verdict on whether the subject of the case satisfies certain requirements.</span></p><ul><li>The <strong>subject </strong>of a verification case is an input parameter that identifies the system or other entity that is being evaluated as to whether it satisfies certain requirements.</li><li>The <strong>objective </strong>of a verification case is to verify that the verification subject satisfies one or more specific requirements, which are specified as a special kind of required constraint in the objective.<ul><li><p>In addition to <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Requirement constraints" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[assumed and required constraint]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> usages allowed in any requirement usage, the objective of a verification case may also have <strong>requirement verification usages</strong>, which indicate the requirements to be verified by the verification case.</p></li></ul></li><li>The return parameter called a verdict, which can be:<ul><li><em>Pass </em>indicates that the subject has been determined to satisfy the requirements to be verified.</li><li><em>Fail </em>indicates that the subject has been determined not to satisfy the requirements to be verified.</li><li><em>Inconclusive </em>indicates that a determination could not be made as to whether the subject satisfies the requirements to be verified.</li><li><em>Error </em>indicates that an error occurred during the performance of the verification.</li></ul></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4006e721-ee11-43b3-bc6a-f9a27dcbd813"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>A verification case can have only one subject, objective, and return parameter.</li><li>A verification case always returns a verdict of type <em>VerdictKind</em> (in the library package <em>VerificationCases</em>), which is an enumeration with allowed values <em>pass</em>, <em>fail</em>, <em>inconclusive</em>, and <em>error</em>. </li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>Specifying subject, objective, <span style="letter-spacing: -0.006em;">and verdict </span><span style="letter-spacing: -0.006em;">for verification case</span></h2><h4>Specifying subjects, objectives, and verdicts for verification cases via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To specify subjects, objectives, and verdicts for verification cases via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Textual Editor, within the curly braces of the case's body, do the following:<ol><li>To specify a subject, declare the <em>subject </em>keyword, <span>specify the element name</span>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Specializations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[specialization]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (optional).</li><li>To specify an objective, declare the <em>objective </em><span>keyword, then in the element body, </span><ac:link ac:anchor="Creating documentation"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Annotations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[declare a documentation]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span><br /></span></span></li><li>To specify a verdict, declare the <em>return </em>keyword named verdict, defined by <em>VerdictKind</em>.</li></ol></li><li>Click the <strong>Synchronize<span> </span></strong>button.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3887c4b7-aa6f-4315-989f-b1b00b8a9f77"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">verification</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> shall be less than or equal to 2500 kg. */</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">require</span><span <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254422293 space=SYSML2P version=4 -->
## PAGE 00427: (2026x) Subsetting

- page_id: `254422293`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254422293/2026x+Subsetting
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2025-12-03T08:51:13.657+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Relations > (2026x) Specializations
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Use the subsetting relationshipto specialize a usage element. Inherited features can be subsetted by multiple features. The specialized definition inherits the features of the more general definition element and can add other features.

##### Subsetting

###### Subsetting a usage via the Textual Editor

To subset a usage via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, identify the usage element that you want to be the subsetting one.
2. Follow the element name with either one of the following:
  1. keyword subsets
  2. textual symbol :>
3. Follow the keyword with the name of the subsetted usage element.
4. Click the Synchronize button. partengine{ 
partvalves; 
} 
partdieselEngine:>engine;/* or */partdieselEnginesubsetsengine;

###### Subsetting a usage via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

To subset a usage via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

- Drag and drop an element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Drag and Drop handler' space=''] on an element symbol executing the Set Subsetted Feature command.

###### Subsetting a usage via the view palette

To subset a usage via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Specializations group, select subsetting .
2. Draw the line with the arrowhead going from the subsetting usage to the subsetted one. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Subsetting a usage via the Create Relationship command

To subset a usagevia the Create Relationship command

1. Right-click the element that you want to be the subsetting one.
2. In the shortcut menu, click Create Relationship , then in the submenu, click Outgoing , and then in the submenu, click subsetting . The Element Picker dialog opens.
3. In the Element Picker dialog, do one of the following:
  1. In a view, click the element symbol you want to subset. Hold Shift to select multiple elements.
  2. Click the Add [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The element selection dialog opens. In the element selection dialog, select the element you want to subset and click OK .
  3. Drag and drop the needed element you want to subset from the Containment tree onto the Element Picker .
4. In the Element Picker dialog, click OK .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254423150 space=SYSML2P version=4 -->
## PAGE 00428: (2026x) Succession

- page_id: `254423150`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254423150/2026x+Succession
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2025-12-11T20:05:10.392+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Behavior
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A succession is a binary connection that requires its two related usages to have values that are occurrences that happen completely separated in time. A succession asserts that the first action must complete before the second can begin. Successions can be used to assert the ordering of any kind of occurrences in time, but are particularly useful for event occurrences and performances of actions.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']The image displays succession connectors, where one action succeeds another.Additionally, the *startTrip*succession connector is also [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Displaying elements in symbolic views' space=''].

##### Creating succession usage elements

###### Creating succession usage elements via the Textual Editor

To create succession usage elements via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the succession keyword.
2. Specify the element name (optional), [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specializations' space=''] (optional), then the keyword first , followed by the name of the first element you want to connect via a succession , then the keyword then , followed by the name of the second element you want to connect. If the succession has no name and no specialization, the keyword *succession*can be omitted.
3. Click the Synchronize button.

50%

// The two related features of a succession are identified after its declaration part and before its body,following the keyword *succession*and separated by the keyword *then*.Actions can be declared separately from the successions:

actiontransportPassenger{ 
actionenterVehicle;// action declarations 
actiondriveVehicle; 
actionstopVehicle; 
actionexitVehicle;

successionstartTripfirstenterVehiclethendriveVehicle;// named successions with source (*first*) and target (*then*) 
successionexecuteTripfirstdriveVehiclethenstopVehicle; 
successionfinishTripfirststopVehiclethenexitVehicle;

// OR

// The keyword *succession* may be omitted for unnamed successions:

firstenterVehiclethendriveVehicle;// unnamed successions with source (*first*) and target (*then*) 
firstdriveVehiclethenstopVehicle; 
firststopVehiclethenexitVehicle; 
}

50%

// Action declarations can be combined with successions:

actiontransportPassenger{ 
actionenterVehicle;// action declaration 
thenactiondriveVehicle;// unnamed successions (*then*) with action declarations (*action <action_name>*) 
thenactionstopVehicle; 
thenactionexitVehicle; 
}

// If a succession is placed lexically directly between the two occurrence usages that are its related elements, then the declaration of the succession can be shortened to just the keyword *then*, prepended to the declaration of the second occurrence usage:

actiontransportPassenger{ 
actionenterVehicle;// action declaration 
firstenterVehiclethendriveVehicle;// unnamed succession with source (*first*) and target (*then*) 
actiondriveVehicle;// action declaration 
firstdriveVehiclethenstopVehicle; 
actionstopVehicle; 
firststopVehiclethenexitVehicle; 
actionexitVehicle; 
}

#### NOTE: Succession shorthands for actions

In addition to the regular full notation for successions, you can also use textual notation succession shorthands within the body of an action definition or usage.

#### EXPAND: Succession shorthands for actions

Succession shorthands for actions

// The source and target actions can be specified separately (first ...; then...;) instead of a single single succession (first ... then...;):

actiontransportPassenger{ 
actionenterVehicle;// action declarations 
actiondriveVehicle; 
actionstopVehicle; 
actionexitVehicle;

firstenterVehicle; 
thendriveVehicle;// unnamed successions 
firstdriveVehicle; 
thenstopVehicle; 
firststopVehicle; 
thenexitVehicle; 
}

// The *then* keyword may also be followed by a complete action usage declaration if source and target actions are specified separately:

actiontransportPassenger{ 
actionenterVehicle; 
firstenterVehicle; 
thenactiondriveVehicle; 
firstdriveVehicle; 
thenactionstopVehicle; 
firststopVehicle; 
thenactionexitVehicle; 
}

// The action preceding the *then* keyword is the source of the succession:

actiontransportPassenger{ 
actionenterVehicle; 
firstenterVehicle; 
thenactiondriveVehicle; 
thenactionstopVehicle; 
thenactionexitVehicle; 
}

###### Creating succession usage elements via the smart manipulator in a view

To create succession usage elements via the smart manipulator in a view

1. In a view, select an element symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the succession command.
2. Click on the element you want to connect via a succession. A succession path is created between the elements.

###### Creating succession usage elements via the view palette

To create succession usage elements via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click the **succession** button to create a succession usage.
2. In a view, click on the first of the two elements you want to connect via a succession, then on the second. A succession is created between the elements.

###### Creating succession usage elements via the Create Relationship command

To create succession usage elements via the Create Relationship command

1. Right-click one of the elements you want to connect via a succession.
2. In the shortcut menu, click Create Relationship , then in the submenu, click Outgoing , and then in the submenu, click succession . The Element Picker dialog opens.
3. In the Element Picker dialog, do one of the following:
  1. In a view, click the element symbol you want to connect. Hold Shift to select multiple elements.
  2. Click the Add [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The element selection dialog opens. In the element selection dialog, select the element you want to connect and click OK .
  3. Drag and drop the needed element you want to connect from the Containment tree onto the Element Picker .
4. In the Element Picker dialog, click OK .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">A succession is a binary connection that requires its two related usages to have values that are occurrences that happen completely separated in time. A succession asserts that the first action must complete before the second can begin. Successions can be used to assert the ordering of any kind of occurrences in time, but are particularly useful for event occurrences and performances of actions.</span></p><h6 style="text-align: </span><span>Additionally, the <em>startTrip </em>succession connector is also <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying elements in symbolic views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[elaborated]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></h6><h3>Creating succession usage elements</h3><h4>Creating succession usage elements via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To create succession usage elements via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the <em>succession </em>keyword.</li><li>Specify the element name (optional), <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Specializations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[specialization]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (optional), then the keyword <em>first</em>, followed by the name of the <span>first element you want to connect via a succession</span>, then the keyword <em>then</em>, followed by the name of the second element you want to connect.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="242ae7b3-a28c-4bb8-aa32-a6c470c46322"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the succession has no name and no specialization, the keyword <em>succession </em>can be ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="section" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="74a8cf73-59ae-49ac-a247-54c0060bd04d"><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bae3e9ea-00dd-464d-9bc4-295d540d59b0"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="51269e39-1d93-443f-a69a-acd6f64d63bb"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// The two related features of a succession are identified after its declaration part and before its body, </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">following the keyword <em>succession </em>and separated by the keyword <em>then</em>. </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">Actions can be declared separately from the successions:</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">transportPassenger</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">enterVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;  </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// action declarations</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">driveVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">stopVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">exitVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">succession</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">startTrip</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">first</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">enterVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">then</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">driveVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// named successions with source (<em>first</em>) and target (<em>then</em>)</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">succession</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">executeTrip</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">first</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">driveVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">then</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">stopVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">succession</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">finishTrip</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">first</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">stopVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">then</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">exitVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span></p><p>    <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// OR</span></p><p>    <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// The keyword <em>succession</em> may be omitted for unnamed successions:</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">first</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">enterVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">then</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">driveVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// unnamed successions with source (<em>first</em>) and target (<em>then</em>)</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">first</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">driveVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">then</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">stopVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">first</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">stopVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">then</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">exitVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="69db49b7-31b6-4886-94d9-e0e835d435ec"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b27dfc04-647d-44c9-8be8-e71cb59d40ff"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// Action declarations can be combined with successions:</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">transportPassenger</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">enterVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;  </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// action declaration</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">then</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">driveVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// unnamed successions (<em>then</em>) with action declarations (<em>action &lt;action_name&gt;</em>)</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">then</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">stopVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">then</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">exitVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="51298067-55c4-406b-a249-e7a463f3a7ac"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// If a succession is placed lexically directly between the two occurrence usages that are its related elements, then the declaration of the succession can be shortened to just the keyword <em>then</em>, prepended to the declaration of the second occurrence usage:</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">transportPassenger</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">enterVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;  </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// action declaration</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">first</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">enterVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">then</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">driveVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// unnamed succession with source (<em>first</em>) and target (<em>then</em>)</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">driveVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;  </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// action declaration</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">first</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">driveVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">then</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">stopVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">stopVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">first</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">stopVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">then</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">exitVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">exitVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f86e499b-ee8a-4b74-854b-6192c8c78f60"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>In addition to the regular full notation for successions, you can also use textual notation succession shorthands within the body of an action definition or usage.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="79e5ce2c-7e23-4ebc-95ee-11da8f27ca3b"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Succession shorthands for actions</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="85429dd3-fe6e-4ae9-9959-9eff70ebfb31"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// The source and target actions can be specified separately (first ...; then...;) instead of a single single succession (first ... then...;):</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">transportPassenger</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">enterVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;  </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// action declarations</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">driveVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">stopVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">exitVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">first</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">enterVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">then</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">driveVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// unnamed successions</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">first</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">driveVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">then</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">stopVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">first</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">stopVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">then</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">exitVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// The <em>then</em> keyword may also be followed by a complete action usage declaration i</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">f source and target actions are specified separately</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">:</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">transportPassenger</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">enterVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">first</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">enterVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">then</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">driveVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">first</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">driveVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">then</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">stopVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">first</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">stopVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">then</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// The action preceding the <em>then</em> keyword is the source of the succession:</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">transportPassenger</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> { </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">enterVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">first</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">enterVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">then</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">driveVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">then</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">stopVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">then</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">exitVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h4>Creating succession usage elements via the smart manipulator in a view</h4><p>To create succession usage elements via the smart manipulator in a view</p><hr /><ol><li>In a view, select an element symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the <strong>succession </strong>command.</li><li>Click on the element you want to connect via a succession. A succession path is created between the elements.</li></ol><h4>Creating succession usage elements via the view palette</h4><p>To create succession usage elements via the view palette</p><hr /><ol><li>In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click the<span> <strong>succession </strong></span><span>button to create a succession usage.</span></li><li>In a view, click on the first of the two elements you want to connect via a succession, then on the second. A succession is created between the elements.</li></ol><h4>Creating succession usage elements <span>via the Create Relationship command</span></h4><p>To create succession usage elements via the Create Relationship command</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click one of the elements you want to connect via a succession.</li><li>In the shortcut menu, click <strong>Create Relationship</strong>, then in the submenu, click <strong>Outgoing</strong>, and then in the submenu, click <strong>succession</strong>. The <strong>Element Picker </strong>dialog opens.</li><li>In the <strong>Element Picker </strong>dialog, do one of the following:<ol><li>In a view, click the element symbol you want to connect. Hold Shift to select multiple elements. </li><li>Click the <strong>Add <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="add_button.png" /></ac:image> </strong>button. The element selection dialog opens. In the element selection dialog, select the element you want to connect and click <strong>OK</strong>.</li><li>Drag and drop the needed element you want to connect from the Containment tree onto the <strong>Element Picker</strong>.</li></ol></li><li>In the <strong>Element Picker</strong><strong> </strong>dialog, click <strong>OK</strong>. </li></ol><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254423160 space=SYSML2P version=3 -->
## PAGE 00429: (2026x) Succession flow

- page_id: `254423160`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254423160/2026x+Succession+flow
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-12-17T15:42:55.110+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Behavior
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A succession flow is a transfer that only occurs after the source action ends and before the target action starts. A succession flow connection is specified in the same way as a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Flow' space=''], but it adds the further constraint that the transfer source must complete before the transfer starts, and the transfer must complete before the target can start.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']The image displays the *lightFlow* succession flow connector defined by *LightFlow*from *produceDirectedLight.light*to*reflectLight.light***with the payload *light*typed by *Light*.Additionally, the connector is also [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Displaying elements in symbolic views' space=''].

##### Creating succession flow usage elements

###### Creating succession flow usage elements via the Textual Editor

To create succession flow usage elements via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the succession flow keyword.
2. Specify the element name (optional), [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specializations' space=''] (optional), (optional), then the from keyword and the name of the source element, followed by the keyword to and the name of the target element . If the succession flow has no name and no specialization, the keyword *from*can be omitted.
3. Click the Synchronize button. successionflowlightFlow:LightFlowfromproduceDirectedLight.lighttoreflectLight.light; // named and specializedsuccession flowsuccessionflow:LightFlowfromproduceDirectedLight.lighttoreflectLight.light; // unnamed specializedsuccession flowsuccessionflowlightFlowfromproduceDirectedLight.lighttoreflectLight.light; // named unspecializedsuccession flowsuccessionflowproduceDirectedLight.lighttoreflectLight.light; // unnamed unspecializedflow can omit the keyword *from*

###### Creating succession flow usage elements via the smart manipulator in a view

To create succession flow usage elements via the smart manipulator in a view

1. In a view, select an element symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the succession flow command.
2. Click on the element you want to connect via a succession flow. A succession flow path is created between the elements.

###### Creating succession flow usage elements via the view palette

To create succession flow usage elements via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click the **succession flow** button to create a succession flow usage.
2. In a view, click on the source element. A dropdown menu appears. Select the output element in the menu.
3. Then click on the target element. A dropdown menu appears. Select the compatible input element in the menu. A flow usage is created between the elements.

##### Setting the payload for the flow

While the payload name and sign are displayed on the flow in a view by default, you can hide them using the **Show Payload** [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Modifying styles of individual symbols' space=''].

###### Setting the payload for the succession flow via the Textual Editor

To set the payload for the succession flow via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor before the keyword *from*within the succession flow for which you want to set a payload.
2. Declare the keyword of followed by either of the following:
  1. The name of the payload specialized by a definition element.
  2. The name of the definition element you want to set as payload for the flow.
3. Click the Synchronize button. successionflow:LightFlowofLightfromproduceDirectedLight.lighttoreflectLight.light; // specialized(feature typing) succession flow with payload set as a definition elementsuccession flow lightFlow : LightFlow of light : Light from produceDirectedLight.light to reflectLight.light; // specialized(feature typing) succession flow with a named and specialized (feature typed) payload

###### Setting the payload for the succession flow via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

To set the payload for the succession flow via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

- Drag and drop a definition element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's on a succession flow element symbol executing the Set Payload command.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// specialized<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"> (feature typing) <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">succession</span></span></span></span> flow with a named and specialized (feature typed) <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">payload</span></span></span></span></span></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h4>Setting the payload for the succession flow via the Drag and Drop handler in a view</h4><p>To set the payload for the succession flow via the Drag and Drop handler in a view</p><hr /><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Drag and drop a definition element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's<span> </span></span><ac:link><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Drag and Drop handler]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">on a succession flow element <span>symbol executing the </span></span><strong>Set Payload </strong>command.</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254422513 space=SYSML2P version=4 -->
## PAGE 00430: (2026x) Swimlane grid

- page_id: `254422513`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254422513/2026x+Swimlane+grid
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2025-12-17T15:37:42.021+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Behavior > (2026x) Actions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The swimlane grid symbol allows you to model and organize actions based on the elements responsible for performing those actions.Each swimlane in the swimlane grid representsthe system element responsible for performing actions displayed in the corresponding swimlane. The represented elements are easily identifiable as their names are indicated in the headers of each swimlane with the keyword «performer», indicating their role of beingresponsible for performing actions displayed in their corresponding swimlanes.

The swimlane grid symbol is comprised of two sections:

- The swimlane headers display each swimlane referent's name, i.e., the names of the elements responsible for performing actions displayed in the specific swimlanes.
- The swimlane pool is comprised of individual swimlanes . The swimlane pool allows you to easily model actions and their sequencing based on their performers, i.e., the elements that perform these actions, displayed as separate swimlanes.
  - The individual swimlanes display actions performed by the swimlane referents whose names are indicated in the swimlane headers with the keyword «performer» .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The swimlane grid symbol can be displayed for the following elements:

- perform action . Its swimlanes represent the **subparts** of the part that owns and/or inherits the perform action. The perform action element symbol owns the swimlane grid symbol.
  - For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Modeling actions with swimlanes' space=''] page.
- include use case . Its swimlanes represent the subject and actor parameters owned and/or inherited by the include use case. The include use case element symbol owns the swimlane grid symbol.
  - For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Use case scenario' space=''] page.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Examples of the swimlane grid symbol in include use case and perform action element symbols. 
The *find person in distress* include use case's subject and actors are represented by the swimlanes. 
The *drone in any mission* part's subparts are represented by the swimlanes displayed for the perform action owned by the containing part.

##### Working with the swimlane grid

To perform the following procedures, the perform action or the include use case that contains the actions (or would contain if they are not created yet)performed by swimlane referents must be displayed in the view.

###### Displaying swimlane grid and creating additional swimlanes

To display swimlane grid and create additional swimlanes

- To display the swimlane grid:
  - Right-click the perform action/include use case symbol and in the shortcut menu, click [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Displaying elements in symbolic views' space=''] , then Display Swimlanes . The swimlane grid is displayed with swimlanes (along with any actions and connectors that may already be defined in the model), representing elements owned or inherited by the element that owns or inherits the perform action/include use case.
- To create additional swimlanes:
  - Select the header of an existing swimlane and click the + button on the left or the right side of the swimlane, to create a swimlane to the left or the right of the existing one. Creating a new swimlane creates:
    - a subpart for the element that owns or inherits the perform action, represented by the new swimlane.
    - an actor for the include use case, represented by the new swimlane. However, no related subpart is created for the part that owns or inherits the include use case.

###### Displaying actions in swimlanes

To display actions in swimlanes

- Select one swimlane by right-clicking its header or all swimlanes by right-clicking the swimlane pool. In the shortcut menu, click [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Displaying elements in symbolic views' space='']**Display** , then Display Actions in the dropdown menu . The specific swimlane's shortcut menu contains the same commands as the element symbol it represents.

###### Displaying parameters, connectors, or other features for element symbols in swimlanes

To display parameters, connectors, or other features for element symbols in swimlanes

- Right-click the element symbol(s) in the swimlane(s). In the shortcut menu, click [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Displaying elements in symbolic views' space='']**Display** , then click the appropriate command in its dropdown menu.

###### Rearranging swimlanes

To rearrange swimlanes

- Click the header of a swimlane, then the displayed control arrows to move the swimlane left or right. [ATTACHMENT filename='Rearranging_Swimlanes.png']

###### Moving the swimlane grid symbol

To move the swimlane grid symbol

1. Click anywhere on the swimlane pool. The **Move** icon at the top left of the swimlane grid symbol appears.
2. Click the Move icon and move the swimlane grid symbol. [ATTACHMENT filename='Moving_Swimlanes.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The represented elements are easily identifiable as their names are indicated in the headers of each swimlane with the keyword «performer», indicating their role of being </span><span>responsible for performing actions displayed in their corresponding swimlanes. </span></p><p><span>The swimlane grid symbol is comprised of two sections:</span></p><ul><li>The <strong>swimlane headers</strong> display each swimlane referent's name, i.e., the names of the elements<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> responsible for performing actions displayed in the specific swimlanes.</span></li><li>The <strong>swimlane pool</strong> is comprised of <strong>individual swimlanes</strong>. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=312247219 space=SYSML2P version=5 -->
## PAGE 00431: (2026x) Switching between the original used project and its subsequent imports

- page_id: `312247219`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/312247219/2026x+Switching+between+the+original+used+project+and+its+subsequent+imports
- version_number: 5
- version_date: `2026-06-10T11:52:59.221+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Project management > (2026x) Working with projects > (2026x) Managing local used projects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### EXPAND: Click here to expand the documentation for the 2026x version...

Click here to expand the documentation for the 2026x version...

In addition to versions, used projects may also have multiple imports of the same project. For example, you might [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space=''] a project that is used in the main project from the local repository to your file system, share it with a colleague who makes changes while you continue working on the main project, and then later [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing projects in the local repository' space=''] your colleague’s modified copy back into your local repository. In this case, the local repository will display both projects: the originally used project and the subsequent import of the same project. For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing project imports in the local repository' space=''] page.

Although the main project continues to use the specific project (and its version) that was originally used, you can switch the used project to its different import if needed. Both the original and the import can be modified and saved as a new version, allowing you to update the used project to its latest version (as described in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Updating used projects to their latest versions' space=''] page).

You can view details about the used project (original or its imports) in the **Project Usages**and the **Advanced Project Usages Configuration**dialogs:

- If the used project was created in the local repository, it is displayed as trunk .
- If the used project was imported into the local repository, it is displayed as <imported_file_name> .
  - If there already is an import with the same name, a number is added: <imported_file_name<number>> .

To ensure the used project import and/or version information displayed in the Containment tree and the rest of the tool is up to date, press **F5**or click **View**> **Refresh** in the main menu.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Switching between the original used project and its subsequent imports

To switch between the original used project and its subsequent imports

See the following procedures:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Updating used projects to their latest versions' space='']
- 
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Updating used projects to their latest versions' space='']

#### NOTE: 2026x HotFix1 version

2026x HotFix1 version

This documentation page is for the 2026x HotFix1 version of the SysML v2 Plugin. If you are working with the 2026x version, expand the section above. However, we highly advise upgrading to the 2026x HotFix1 version.

In addition to versions, used projects may also have multiple imports of the same project. For example, you might [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing projects in the local repository' space=''] a project that is used in the main project from the local repository to your file system, share it with a colleague who makes changes while you continue working on the main project, and then later [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing projects in the local repository' space=''] your colleague’s modified copy back into your local repository. In this case, the local repository will display both projects: the originally used project and the subsequent import of the same project. For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing project imports in the local repository' space=''] page.

Although the main project continues to use the specific project (and its version) that was originally used, you can switch the used project to its different import if needed. Both the original and the import can be modified and saved as a new version, allowing you to update the used project to its latest version (as described in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Updating used projects to their latest versions' space=''] subsection).

You can view details about the used project (original or its imports) in the **Project Usages**and the **Advanced Project Usages Configuration**dialogs:

- If the used project was created in the local repository, it is displayed only by indicating the project name and version ( Latest/Oldest Version ).
- If the used project was imported into the local repository, it is displayed using the following structure: <[<file_name>.mdszip imported @<date and time of the import>] <version (Latest/Oldest Version)>> .

To ensure the used project import and/or version information displayed in the Containment tree and the rest of the tool is up to date, press **F5**or click **View**> **Refresh** in the main menu.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Switching between the original used project and its subsequent imports via the Containment tree

The Containment tree only displays the project version information (Latest/Oldest). You can view more details about the import in the **Project Usages**and the **Advanced Project Usages Configuration**dialogs.

To switch between the original used project and its subsequent imports via the Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, expand the Used Projects node, then right-click a used project. In the shortcut menu, click Switch To.. . The Switch To menu opens.
2. In the Switch To menu, select the project you want to use. The used project is updated. [ATTACHMENT filename='Switching to a different instance of a used project via the Containment tree.png']

###### Switching between the original used project and its subsequent imports via the Project Usages dialog

To switch between the original used project and its subsequent imports via the Project Usages dialog

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local used projects' space='']Open the **Project Usages**dialog and d o one of the following:
  1. In the dialog's toolbar, click the **Change Version** [ATTACHMENT filename='change_version_button.png'] button , and in its dropdown menu, click **Switch To...** . The Switch To menu opens.
  2. In the dialog's project usages tree, right-click a used project and in the shortcut menu, click the **Change Version >** **Switch To...******command. The Switch To menu opens.
2. In the Switch To menu, select the project you want to use.
3. Click the OK button to close the Project Usages dialog. The used project is updated.

###### Switching between the original used project and its subsequent imports via the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog

To switch between the original used project and its subsequent imports via the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local used projects' space='']Open the **Advanced Project Usages Configuration**dialog .
2. In the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog, do one of the following:
  1. S elect a used project and in the dialog's toolbar, click the **Change Version****[IMAGE alt='' src='']**button, then in its submenu, click **Switch To...** . The Switch To menu opens.
  2. Right-click a used project and in the shortcut menu, click the **Change Version** command , then in its submenu, click **Switch To...** . The Switch To menu opens.
3. In the Switch To menu, select the project you want to use. The cell in the Action column for the removed used project states ' Switch to <project version> <project import> '. [ATTACHMENT filename='Switching to a different instance of a used project via the Advanced Usages Configuration dialog.png']
4. (Optional) To undo the action, do one of the following:
  1. In the dialog's toolbar, click the Reset Changes for Selection [ATTACHMENT filename='reset_changes_for_selection_button.png'] button.
  2. Right-click the used project and in the shortcut menu, click the Reset Changes for Selection command.
5. Do one of the following:
  1. To save changes and keep the dialog open, click the Apply button.
  2. To save changes and close the dialog, click the OK button.
  3. To discard changes and close the dialog, click the Cancel button.
6. Click the OK button to close the Project Usages dialog.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
