In this case, the local repository will display both projects: the originally used project and the subsequent import of the same project. For more information, see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing project imports in the local repository" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Managing project imports in the local repository]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</span></p><p>Although the main project continues to use the specific project (and its version) that was originally used, you can switch the used project to its different import if needed. Both the original and the import can be modified and saved as a new version, allowing you to update the used project to its latest version (as described in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Updating used projects to their latest versions" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Updating used projects to their latest versions]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> subsection).</p><p>You can view details about the used project (original or its imports) in the <strong>Project Usages </strong>and the <strong>Advanced Project Usages Configuration </strong>dialogs:</p><ul><li data-uuid="caa73de5-cecb-4e46-a0d1-66b9d2c4ad32">If the used project was created in the local repository, it is displayed only by indicating the project name and version (<em>Latest/Oldest Version</em>). </li><li data-uuid="d21cca26-d712-4a15-97bf-7522917126ab">If the used project was imported into the local repository, it is displayed using the following structure: <em>&lt;[&lt;file_name&gt;.mdszip imported @&lt;date and time of the import&gt;] &lt;version (Latest/Oldest Version)&gt;&gt;</em>.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dbb3f07b-06c6-4c63-a5bd-125968cf30b3"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To ensure the used project import and/or version information displayed in the Containment tree and the rest of the tool is up to date, press <strong>F5 </strong>or click <strong>View </strong>&gt; <strong>Refresh</strong> in the main menu.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Switching to a different revision of a used project.png" /></ac:image></p><h4>Switching between the original used project and its subsequent imports via the Containment tree </h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2a60eb6c-e308-4df6-b7c3-a12ef1425039"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The Containment tree only displays the project version information (Latest/Oldest). You can view more details about the import in the <strong>Project Usages </strong>and the <strong>Advanced Project Usages Configuration </strong>dialogs.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To switch between the original used project and its subsequent imports via the Containment tree</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="b76f6ccf-a757-4668-9535-ba36a94b598d">In the Containment tree, expand the<span> </span><strong>Used Projects</strong><span> </span>node, then right-click a used project. In the shortcut menu, click <strong>Switch To..</strong>. The <strong>Switch To </strong>menu opens.</li><li data-uuid="fafab55b-96d1-4435-92ca-062a7dd90d51">In the <strong>Switch To </strong>menu, select the project you want to use.<br />The used project is updated.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Switching to a different instance of a used project via the Containment tree.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><h4>Switching between the original used project and its subsequent imports via the Project Usages dialog</h4><p>To switch between the original used project and its subsequent imports via the Project Usages dialog</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="734fa276-6ddc-4c47-9b62-6fc7320b493f"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Project Usages dialog"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Project Usages </strong>dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> and d<span>o one of the following:</span><ol><li><span>In the dialog's toolbar, click the <strong>Change Version </strong></span><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="change_version_button.png" /></ac:image><span> button</span>, and in its dropdown menu, click <span><strong>Switch To...</strong></span><span>. The </span><strong>Switch To </strong>menu opens.</li><li><span>In the dialog's project usages tree, right-click a used project and in the shortcut menu, click the <strong>Change Version &gt;</strong> <strong>Switch To...</strong><strong> </strong>command. </span><span>The </span><strong>Switch To </strong>menu opens.</li></ol></li><li data-uuid="4c5b9af8-018f-438f-8672-2c9f61bc6019">In the <strong>Switch To </strong>menu, select the project you want to use.</li><li data-uuid="de1778a0-3463-44b8-a635-d01a97a9159f">Click the <strong>OK </strong>button to close the <strong>Project Usages </strong>dialog.<br />The used project is updated.</li></ol><h4>Switching between the original used project and its subsequent imports via the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog</h4><p>To switch between the original used project and its subsequent imports via the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="a8d8e603-935b-4320-a5b1-b514b5d93225"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local used projects" /><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Advanced Project Usages Configuration </strong>dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li><li data-uuid="60409c82-500f-4cca-8fd7-f7bfcdf1d924">In the <strong>Advanced Project Usages Configuration</strong> dialog, do one of the following:<ol><li>S<span>elect a used project and in the dialog's toolbar, click the <strong>Change Version</strong><strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="change_version_button.png" /></ac:image> </strong>button, then in its submenu, click <strong>Switch To...</strong></span><span>. The </span><strong>Switch To </strong>menu opens.</li><li>Right-click <span>a used project and in the shortcut menu, click the <strong>Change Version</strong> command</span><span>, then in its submenu, click <strong>Switch To...</strong></span><span>. </span><span>The </span><strong>Switch To </strong>menu opens.</li></ol></li><li>In the <strong>Switch To </strong>menu, select the project you want to use.<span><br /></span>The cell in the <strong>Action</strong> column for the removed used project states '<strong>Switch to &lt;project version&gt; &lt;project import&gt;</strong>'.  <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Switching to a different instance of a used project via the Advanced Usages Configuration dialog.png" /></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="4da4a7b6-1046-4bd6-bf11-5841b58dbe92">(Optional) To undo the action, do one of the following:<ol><li>In the dialog's toolbar, click the <strong>Reset Changes for Selection</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="reset_changes_for_selection_button.png" /></ac:image> button.</li><li>Right-click the used project and in the shortcut menu, click the <strong>Reset Changes for Selection </strong>command.</li></ol></li><li data-uuid="e2d79801-9308-4d57-9ebd-4258e43dc6ac">Do one of the following:<ol><li>To save changes and keep the dialog open, click the <strong>Apply </strong>button.</li><li>To save changes and close the dialog, click the <strong>OK </strong>button.</li><li>To discard changes and close the dialog, click the <strong>Cancel</strong> button.</li></ol></li><li data-uuid="00efa913-4221-4fc8-bae0-425900dddf33">Click the <strong>OK </strong>button to close the <strong>Project Usages </strong>dialog.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254422610 space=SYSML2P version=2 -->
## PAGE 00432: (2026x) Switching to System Engineer SysML v2 perspective

- page_id: `254422610`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254422610/2026x+Switching+to+System+Engineer+SysML+v2+perspective
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-27T20:45:25.719+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Project management > (2026x) Modeling tool environment
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When working with SysML v2 features, you should switch the modeling tool's perspective to the System Engineer SysML v2 perspective.

Generally, when you create a new SysML v2 project, a question dialog is prompted, asking you to switch the perspective. Clicking Yes changes the modeling tool's perspective to the System Engineer SysML v2 perspective.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Otherwise, see the procedures below on how to switch the perspective.

##### Switching to the System Engineer SysML v2 perspective

To switch to the System Engineer SysML v2 perspective

- Do one of the following:
  - In the main menu, select**Options**>**Perspectives**>**Perspectives**. In the**Select Perspective**dialog, select the**System Engineer SysML v2**perspective, and click the**Apply**button.
  - In the main toolbar, click the**Perspective**drop-down box and select the**System Engineer SysML v2**perspective.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>When working with SysML v2 features, you should switch the modeling tool's perspective to the System Engineer SysML v2 perspective. </p><p>Generally, when you create a new SysML v2 project, a question dialog is prompted, asking you to switch the perspective. Clicking Yes changes the modeling tool's perspective to the System Engineer SysML v2 perspective.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="question_dialog.png" /></ac:image></p><p>Otherwise, see the procedures below on how to switch the perspective.</p><h3>Switching to the System Engineer SysML v2 perspective</h3><p>To switch to the System Engineer SysML v2 perspective</p><hr /><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Do one of the following:</span></span><ul style="text-align: left;"><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">In the main menu, select<span> </span><strong>Options<span> </span></strong>&gt;<span> </span><strong>Perspectives<span> </span></strong>&gt;<span> </span><strong>Perspectives</strong>. In the<span> </span><strong>Select Perspective</strong><span> </span>dialog, select the<span> </span><strong>System Engineer SysML v2</strong><span> </span>perspective, and click the<span> </span><strong>Apply<span> </span></strong>button.</span></span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">In the main toolbar, click the<span> </span><strong>Perspective</strong><span> </span>drop-down box and select the<span> </span><strong>System Engineer SysML v2</strong><span> </span>perspective.</span></span></li></ul></li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254422689 space=SYSML2P version=3 -->
## PAGE 00433: (2026x) Symbol styles

- page_id: `254422689`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254422689/2026x+Symbol+styles
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-12-17T15:50:39.862+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Views
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Element symbols, including shapes and paths, are displayed on views based on the symbol styles defined in the style sheets in the *3DS SysML Customization*library that is loaded in every project. You can modify element symbol styles according to your preferences by working with style sheets.

Types of style sheet specification:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Predefined style sheets' space=''] Each SysML v2 project is automatically loaded with the*3DS SysML Customization* library that contains predefined style sheets comprised of sets of rules defining symbol styles based on element metaclass. The *DefaultStyleSheet*style sheet contains model-based styles that apply to all element symbols in SysML v2 projects by default based on element metaclass.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Custom style sheets' space=''] Instead of or in addition to using the existing style sheets, you can also create custom style sheets with your own sets of rules for symbol styles.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Modifying styles of individual symbols' space=''] Whether using a default or a custom style, y ou can customize the styles of individual symbols. Using the Symbol Style dialog, you can specify the style properties for specific symbols, determining their appearance. Changing the styles for an individual symbol via the Symbol Style dialog overwrites predefined and custom model-based symbol styles; the modifications persist even after updating [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Activating style sheets' space=''] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Modifying styles of individual views' space=''] Whether using a default or a custom style, y ou can customize the styles of individual views. Using the View Style dialog, you can specify the style properties for specific views, determining their appearance. Changing the styles for an individual view via the View Style dialog overwrites predefined and custom model-based symbol styles; the modifications persist even after updating [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Activating style sheets' space=''] .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### On the left: symbol styles using a predefined style sheet *DefaultStyleSheet*. 
In the middle: symbol styles using a predefined style sheet*DefaultStyleSheet*with the customized style for the individual attribute symbol. 
On the right: symbol styles using a custom style sheet.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

You can modify element symbol styles according to your preferences by working with style sheets. </span></p><p>Types of style sheet specification:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Predefined style sheets" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Predefined style sheets]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Each SysML v2 project is automatically loaded with the<span> <em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">3DS SysML Customization</span></em></span> library that contains predefined style sheets comprised of sets of rules defining symbol styles based on element metaclass. The <em>DefaultStyleSheet </em>style sheet contains </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">model-based styles that apply to all element symbols in SysML v2 projects by default based on element metaclass. </span><br /></span></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Custom style sheets" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Custom style sheets]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><br />Instead of or in addition to using the existing style sheets, you can also create custom style sheets with your own sets of rules for symbol styles. </li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Modifying styles of individual symbols" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Modifying styles of individual symbols]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Whether using a default or a custom style, y</span></span>ou can customize the styles of individual symbols. Using the Symbol Style dialog, you can specify the style properties for specific symbols, determining their appearance. Using the View Style dialog, you can specify the style properties for specific views, determining their appearance. Changing the styles for an individual view via the View Style dialog overwrites predefined and custom model-based symbol styles; the modifications persist even after updating <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Activating style sheets" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[active style sheets]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. </li></ul><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="symbol_styles.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">On the left: symbol styles using a predefined style sheet <em>DefaultStyleSheet</em>.<br />In the middle: <span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">symbol styles using a predefined style sheet </span><em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">DefaultStyleSheet </em>with the customized style for the individual attribute symbol.<br />On the right: symbol styles using a custom style sheet.</h6>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254422816 space=SYSML2P version=5 -->
## PAGE 00434: (2026x) Symbolic views

- page_id: `254422816`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254422816/2026x+Symbolic+views
- version_number: 5
- version_date: `2025-12-17T14:53:37.426+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model management > (2026x) Working with views > (2026x) View kinds > (2026x) Graphical views
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Symbolic views allow you to model via symbolic model representation.In a symbolic view, elements are represented as shapes, and relationships and connectors as paths, both shapes and paths are referred to as symbols.Youcan use symbolic views for modeling and representing parts interconnection, action flow, requirements, etc.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The image displays a standard predefined symbolic view in the View Editor tab with the toolbar, pane, and palette. Elements and connectors are displayed as shapes and paths on the pane.

##### Symbolic views

Specific symbolic views you create in the modeling tool are predefined symbolic view usages defined by appropriate predefined symbolic view definitions. These predefined symbolic view definitions can differ in the palettes they provide, renderings and/or element filters they specify.

- For symbolic view creation procedures, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating symbolic views' space=''] page.
- Additionally, you can create [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Custom symbolic view definitions' space=''] .

Available symbolic view definitions are differentiated as follows:

- **Standard predefined symbolic view definitions**
  - Do not have any specified filters, meaning, any elements can be exposed by these views. Once you have created such a view, you may want to specify [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Filtering elements for views' space=''] to limit the displayed elements.
  - Can be created via the Textual Editor or the Create View command in the Containment tree.
- Predefined symbolic view definitions that use expressions
  - Contain element filters specified in the*DS_Views::SymbolicViewsByExpression**::NonStandardLibraryElementFilter*and *DS_Views::SymbolicViewsByExpression**::***EssentialElementsFilter**view definitions, narrowing down what elements can be exposed by the view by discarding various standard library elements and other non-essential elements that are unlikely to be relevant for display in views.
  - Additionally, most of the predefined symbolic view definitions that use expressions contain additional filters to narrow down the exposed elements, e.g., *PartsTreeView*view definition contains filters specifying that only part usage elements can be exposed.
  - Can be created only via the Textual Editor.

See the table below listing all predefined symbolic view definitions.

| View definitions | Description | Palette | Availability |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Standard predefined symbolic view definitions |  |  |  |
| General View | Standard predefined multi-purpose general symbolic view for element representation and modeling. | Includes a view palette with all available button categories. | Via Textual EditorVia the Create View command dialog/submenu |
| Interconnection View | Standard predefined symbolic view for element interconnection representation and modeling. | Includes a view palette with button categories optimized for interconnections modeling. |  |
| Action Flow View | Standard predefined symbolic view for action flow representation and modeling. | Includes a view palette with button categories optimized for action flow modeling. |  |
| State Transition View | Standard predefined symbolic view for state transition representation and modeling. | Includes a view palette with button categories optimized for state transition modeling. |  |
| Predefined symbolic view definitions that use expressions |  |  |  |
| TreeView | Predefined symbolic view definition that uses expressions to display common elements in tree-view rendering style. It subclassifies the NonStandardLibraryElementFilter and EssentialElementsFilter view definitions, which include filters to exclude various standard library elements and other non-essential elements that are unlikely to be relevant for display in views. | Includes a view palette with all available button categories. | Via Textual Editor |
| NestedView | Predefined symbolic view definition that uses expressions to display common elements in nested-view rendering style.It subclassifies the NonStandardLibraryElementFilter and EssentialElementsFilter view definitions, which include filters to exclude various standard library elements and other non-essential elements that are unlikely to be relevant for display in views. | Includes a view palette with all available button categories. |  |
| UsagesNestedView | Predefined symbolic view definition that uses expressions to display usage elements in nested-view rendering style.It subclassifies the NestedView view definition. | Includes a view palette with all available button categories. |  |
| PartsNestedView | Predefined symbolic view definition that uses expressions to display parts as nested shapes, with the connections between them represented as paths.It subclassifies the UsagesNestedView view definition. | Includes a view palette with all available button categories. |  |
| Parts&PortsNestedView | Predefined symbolic view definition that uses expressions to display parts and ports as nested shapes, with the connections between them represented as paths.It subclassifies the UsagesNestedView view definition. | Includes a view palette with all available button categories. |  |
| PartsTreeView | Predefined symbolic view definition that uses expressions to display parts in a decomposition tree style.It subclassifies the TreeView view definition. | Includes a view palette with all available button categories. |  |
| ActionsNestedView | Predefined symbolic view definition that uses expressions to display actions as nested shapes, parameters as shapes on border, and in-between connections represented as paths.It subclassifies the UsagesNestedView view definition. | Includes a view palette with all available button categories. |  |
| ActionsTreeView | Predefined symbolic view definition that uses expressions to display actions in a decomposition tree style. It subclassifies the TreeView view definition. | Includes a view palette with all available button categories. |  |
| RequirementTreeView | Predefined symbolic view definition that uses expressions to display the exposed requirements in a decomposition tree style. It subclassifies the TreeView view definition. | Includes a view palette with all available button categories. |  |
| StatesNestedView | Predefined symbolic view definition that uses expressions to display states as nested shapes, with transitions between them represented as paths.It subclassifies the UsagesNestedView view definition. | Includes a view palette with all available button categories. |  |

##### Symbolic view areas

Views are displayed in the View Editor tab.

- Toolbar allows you to quickly access the options for managing element symbols in the view. See the subsection.
- Pane allows you to model and represent elements as symbols. For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Displaying elements in symbolic views' space=''] page.
- Palette allows you to model elements. See the subsection.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Symbolic view palette

The palette contains buttons for the majority of the elements you can create in the view pane, as well as additional buttons for working with symbols in the view pane.

- The buttons are ordered into categories: Common, Items/Ports/Attributes, Connectors, Specializations, Other Relationships, Actions, Other Actions, States, Cases, Requirements/Constraints. Click the category title to expand the contained element buttons.
- Some element buttons have a down-facing arrow button allowing you to view the button's submenu of other element kinds (e.g., part and part def or in ref , in item , and in attribute ).
- The available buttons and their categories depend on the symbolic view definition.
- You can create [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Custom symbolic view definitions' space=''] .

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']View palette's Items/Ports/Attributes category with the *part*button's submenu.

###### Symbolic view toolbar buttons

The table below shows the buttons available in the symbolic view toolbar.

| Button | Shortcut keys | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Navigation toolbar |  |  |
| Select in Containment tree | Alt+B | Click the button to highlight the element of the selected symbol in the Containment tab of the Model Browser. |
| Layout toolbar |  |  |
| Quick Diagram Layout | Ctrl+Q | Click the arrow button to display the dropdown menu. In it, select the Layout SysML v2 Style option. |
| Align | - | Click the button to display the dropdown menu with the symbol alignment options. |
| Reset Label Positions | - | Click the button to reset the labels' position to default. Make sure the labels are selected first. |
| Edit toolbar |  |  |
| Copy | Ctrl+C | Click to copy the selected symbol. |
| Paste | Ctrl+V | Click to paste the copied symbol. |
| Delete | Ctrl+D | Click to remove selected elements both from the view and the model. |
| Remove From Diagram | Delete | Click to remove the selected element from the view. |
| Shape Editing toolbar |  |  |
| Make Preferred Size | - | Click to resize the selected symbol(s) to the most compact size. |
| Display toolbar |  |  |
| Display | - | Click the arrow button to display the dropdown menu. In that menu, select the kind of symbols you want to display in the view for the selected symbol. You can also choose the style in which the symbols can be displayed, i.e., tree-view or nested. |
| Style Sheets toolbar |  |  |
| Style Sheets | - | Click to activate style sheets for the view or the entire project. >]]> |
| Other buttons |  |  |
| Back | Alt+Left Arrow | Click to navigate to the previously opened view. |
| Forward | Alt+Right Arrow | Click to navigate to the previously opened view. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Symbolic views allow you to model via symbolic model representation. </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">In a symbolic view, elements are represented as shapes, and relationships and connectors as paths, both shapes and paths are referred to as symbols. </span></span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">You </span>can use symbolic views for modeling and representing <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">parts interconnection, action flow, requirements, etc</span>. </span></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="symbolic_view_example.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The image displays a standard predefined symbolic view in the View Editor tab with the toolbar, pane, and palette. Elements and connectors are displayed as shapes and paths on the pane.  </h6><h3>Symbolic views</h3><p>Specific symbolic views you create in the modeling tool are predefined symbolic view usages defined by appropriate predefined symbolic view definitions. These predefined symbolic view definitions can differ in the palettes they provide, renderings and/or element filters they specify. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2cfe40a7-b52d-49a2-98b4-83b0292b7f7c"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><span>For symbolic view creation procedures, see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating symbolic views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Creating symbolic views]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</span></li><li><span>Additionally, you can create </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Custom symbolic view definitions" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[custom symbolic view definitions with customized palettes]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Available symbolic view definitions are differentiated as follows:</p><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><strong>Standard predefined symbolic view definitions</strong> </span><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Do not have any specified filters, meaning, any elements can be exposed by these views. Once you have created such a view, you may want to specify <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Filtering elements for views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[filters]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to limit the displayed elements.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Can be created via the Textual Editor or the Create View command in the Containment tree.</span></li></ul></li><li><strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Predefined symbolic view definitions that use expressions</span></strong><ul><li data-uuid="ad2b1ac9-2819-4de9-aacf-e253ec9ca4b1"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Contain element filters specified in the<span> <em>DS_Views::SymbolicViewsByExpression</em></span><em>::NonStandardLibraryElementFilter<span> </span></em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">and <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span><em>DS_Views::SymbolicViewsByExpression</em></span><em>::</em></span></span><em><span><em style="text-align: left;">EssentialElementsFilter </em></span></em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">view definitions</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">, narrowing down what elements can be exposed by the view by discarding various standard library elements and other non-essential elements that are unlikely to be relevant for display in views. </span></span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Additionally, most of the predefined symbolic view definitions that use expressions contain additional filters to narrow down the exposed elements, e.g., <em>PartsTreeView<span> </span></em>view definition contains filters specifying that only part usage elements can be exposed.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Can be created only via the Textual Editor.</span></li></ul></li></ul><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">See the table below listing all predefined symbolic view definitions.</span></p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 88.1841%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 14.4643%;" /><col style="width: 53.4242%;" /><col style="width: 20.4617%;" /><col style="width: 11.642%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" style="text-align: center;" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"><strong title=""><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);" title="">View definitions</span></strong></td><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" style="text-align: center;" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"><strong title=""><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);" title="">Description</span></strong></td><td class="highlight-grey" style="text-align: center;" title="Background color : " data-highlight-colour="grey"><strong title=""><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);" title="">Palette</span></strong></td><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" style="text-align: center;" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"><strong title=""><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);" title="">Availability</span></strong></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#deebff" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#deebff"><strong title=""><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);" title="">Standard predefined symbolic view definitions</span></strong></td></tr><tr><td><p>General View</p></td><td><p>Standard predefined multi-purpose general symbolic view for element representation and modeling. </p></td><td><ul><li>Includes a view palette with all available button categories.</li></ul></td><td rowspan="4"><ul style="text-align: left;"><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Via Textual Editor</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Via the Create View command dialog/submenu</span></li></ul><p><br /></p><p><br /></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Interconnection View </p></td><td><p>Standard predefined symbolic view for element interconnection representation and modeling.</p></td><td><ul><li>Includes a view palette with button categories optimized for interconnections modeling.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><p>Action Flow View</p></td><td><p>Standard predefined symbolic view for action flow representation and modeling.</p></td><td><ul><li>Includes a view palette with button categories optimized for action flow modeling.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><p>State Transition View</p></td><td>Standard predefined symbolic view for state transition representation and modeling.</td><td><ul><li>Includes a view palette with button categories optimized for state transition modeling.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#deebff" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#deebff"><strong title=""><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);" title="">Predefined symbolic view definitions that use expressions</span></strong><strong title=""><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);" title=""><br /></span></strong></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">TreeView</span></td><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Predefined symbolic view definition that uses expressions to display common elements in tree-view rendering style. </span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">It subclassifies the <em>NonStandardLibraryElementFilter<span> </span></em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">and </span><span><em style="text-align: left;">EssentialElementsFilter </em></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">view definitions</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">, <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">which include filters to exclude various standard library elements <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">and other non-essential elements </span>that are unlikely to be relevant for display in views.</span></span></span></span></p></td><td><ul><li>Includes a view palette with all available button categories.</li></ul></td><td rowspan="10"><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Via Textual Editor</span></li></ul><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><br /></span></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">NestedView</span></td><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Predefined symbolic view definition that uses expressions to display common elements in nested-view rendering style.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">It subclassifies the <em>NonStandardLibraryElementFilter<span> </span></em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">and </span><span><em style="text-align: left;">EssentialElementsFilter </em></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">view definitions</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">, <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">which include filters to exclude various standard library elements <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">and other non-essential elements </span>that are unlikely to be relevant for display in views.</span></span></span></p></td><td><ul><li>Includes a view palette with all available button categories.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">UsagesNestedView</span></p></td><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Predefined symbolic view definition that uses expressions to display usage elements in nested-view rendering style.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">It subclassifies the <em>NestedView</em> view definition.</span></span></p></td><td><ul><li>Includes a view palette with all available button categories.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">PartsNestedView</span></td><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Predefined symbolic view definition that uses expressions to display parts as nested shapes, with the connections between them represented as paths.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">It subclassifies the <em>UsagesNestedView</em> view definition.</span></p></td><td><ul><li>Includes a view palette with all available button categories.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Parts&amp;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">PortsNestedView</span></td><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Predefined symbolic view definition that uses expressions to display parts and ports as nested shapes, with the connections between them represented as paths.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">It subclassifies the <em>UsagesNestedView</em> view definition.</span></p></td><td><ul><li>Includes a view palette with all available button categories.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">PartsTreeView</span></td><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Predefined symbolic view definition that uses expressions to display parts in a decomposition tree style.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">It subclassifies the <em>TreeView</em> view definition.</span></p></td><td><ul><li>Includes a view palette with all available button categories.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">ActionsNestedView</span></td><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Predefined symbolic view definition that uses expressions to display actions as nested shapes, parameters as shapes on border, and in-between connections represented as paths.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">It subclassifies the <em>UsagesNestedView</em> view definition.</span></p></td><td><ul><li>Includes a view palette with all available button categories.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">ActionsTreeView</span></td><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Predefined symbolic view definition that uses expressions to display actions in a decomposition tree style. </span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">It subclassifies the <em>TreeView</em> view definition.</span></p></td><td><ul><li>Includes a view palette with all available button categories.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">RequirementTreeView</span></td><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Predefined symbolic view definition that uses expressions to display the exposed requirements in a decomposition tree style. </span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">It subclassifies the <em>TreeView</em> view definition.</span></p></td><td><ul><li>Includes a view palette with all available button categories.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">StatesNestedView <br /></span></td><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Predefined symbolic view definition that uses expressions to display states as nested shapes, with transitions between them represented as paths.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">It subclassifies the <em>UsagesNestedView</em> view definition.</span></span></p></td><td><ul><li>Includes a view palette with all available button categories.</li></ul></td></tr></tbody></table><h3>Symbolic view areas</h3><p>Views are displayed in the View Editor tab.</p><ul><li><strong>Toolbar</strong> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">allows you to quickly access the options for managing element symbols in the view. See the <ac:link ac:anchor="Symbolic view toolbar buttons" /> subsection.</span></li><li><strong>Pane</strong> allows you to model and represent elements as symbols. For more information, see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying elements in symbolic views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Displaying elements in symbolic views]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">page.</span></li><li><strong>Palette</strong> allows you to model elements. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">See the <ac:link ac:anchor="Symbolic view palette" /> subsection.</span></li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="symbolic_view_areas.png" /></ac:image></p><h4>Symbolic view palette</h4><p>The palette contains buttons for the majority of the elements you can create in the view pane, as well as additional buttons for working with symbols in the view pane.</p><ul><li>The buttons are ordered into categories: Common, Items/Ports/Attributes, Connectors, Specializations, Other Relationships, Actions, Other Actions, States, Cases, Requirements/Constraints. Click the category title to expand the contained element buttons.</li><li>Some element buttons have a down-facing arrow button allowing you to view the button's submenu of other element kinds (e.g., <em>part </em>and <em>part def </em>or <em>in ref</em>, <em>in item</em>, and <em>in attribute</em>).</li><li>The available buttons and their categories depend on the symbolic view definition.</li><li>You can create <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Custom symbolic view definitions" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[custom symbolic view definitions with customized palettes]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. </li></ul><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="diagram_palette_button_submenu.png" /></ac:image>View palette's Items/Ports/Attributes category with the <em>part </em>button's submenu.</h6><h4>Symbolic view toolbar buttons</h4><p>The table below shows the buttons available in the symbolic view toolbar.</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 90.189%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 21.5206%;" /><col style="width: 12.5233%;" /><col style="width: 66.0207%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th style="text-align: left;">Button</th><th style="text-align: left;">Shortcut keys</th><th style="text-align: left;">Description</th></tr><tr><td class="highlight-blue" colspan="3" data-highlight-colour="blue"><strong>Navigation toolbar</strong></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image><ri:attachment in the Containment tab of the Model Browser.</span></p></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#deebff" title="Background color : Light blue 35%" colspan="3" data-highlight-colour="#deebff"><div symbol.</p></div></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="paste_button.png" /></ac:image> Paste</p></div></td><td>Ctrl+V</td><td><div Delete</p></div></td><td>Ctrl+D</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Click to remove selected elements both from the view and the model.</p></div></td></tr><tr><td><div size. </p></div></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-blue" colspan="3" data-highlight-colour="blue"><strong>Display toolbar</strong></td></tr><tr><td><div button to display the dropdown menu. You can also choose the style in which the symbols can be displayed, i.e., tree-view or nested.</p></div></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#deebff" colspan="3" data-highlight-colour="#deebff"><div class="content-wrapper" title=""><p><strong>Style Sheets toolbar</strong></p></div></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="stylesheetsbutton.png" /></ac:image> Style Sheets</p></div></td><td><span>-</span></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Click to activate style sheets for the view or the entire project. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Activating style sheets" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p></div></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-blue" colspan="3" data-highlight-colour="blue"><strong>Other buttons</strong></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:alt="Back" ac:width="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="back_diagram_toolbar.png" /></ac:image> Back</p></div></td><td>Alt+Left Arrow</td><td><p>Click to navigate to the previously opened view.</p></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:alt="Forward" ac:width="20"><ri:attachment ri:filename="forward_diagram_toolbar.png" /></ac:image> Forward</p></div></td><td><p>Alt+Right Arrow</p></td><td><p>Click to navigate to the previously opened view.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254422926 space=SYSML2P version=5 -->
## PAGE 00435: (2026x) Synchronizing custom style sheet modifications

- page_id: `254422926`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254422926/2026x+Synchronizing+custom+style+sheet+modifications
- version_number: 5
- version_date: `2026-04-02T14:35:10.078+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Views > (2026x) Symbol styles > (2026x) Custom style sheets
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Once you have created a style sheet and either customized the *DefaultStyleSheet* components or created custom ones, you need to synchronize the modifications in the Textual Editor and update all views for the modifications to be reflected in model views. Make sure to check whether you also need to assess layout-sensitive changes, as otherwise the updated styles will not be implemented.

##### Synchronizing modifications

Synchronization and do not affect customizations made to individual symbols via the **Symbol Style** dialog. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Modifying styles of individual symbols' space=''] procedure on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Modifying styles of individual symbols' space=''] page.

To synchronize modifications

1. Do one of the following:
  1. In the Textual Editor's toolbar, click the**Synchronize**button[IMAGE alt='' src=''].
  2. Use the Alt+S shortcut keys.
2. Ensure the new [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Activating style sheets' space=''].
3. Press the **F5**key on the keyboard to update all affected views.
4. (Conditional) . You need to assess layout-sensitive changes if you already have views in your project and the style sheet contains updated values of the following attributes:General style attributes: *font*, *wrap, textVerticalPosition, textHorizontalPosition, lineStyle, orientation, isRounded, pathStyle, showPayload.*Compartment style attributes:*showCompartment, showShortName,showName, showFullText, showSpecialization, showQualifiedNameForSpecialization, showModifiers, showValue, showInheritedSign, showInherited, showOnlyOwned.*

##### Assessing layout-sensitive changes

Certain style attributes are layout-sensitive as changing their values can affect already-created views' layout. Whether you have [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Customizing style sheet components' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating style sheet components' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Activating style sheets' space=''] a new one if the updated style sheet contains styles with attribute values that are different from those that were used prior, you need to follow the procedure below to assess and either confirm or deny the changes.

Omitting this procedure means the modeling tool will not implement the updates of the new style and retain the previous attribute values representing symbols in the old style.

Follow the procedure below if you already have views in your project and the style sheet contains updated values of the following attributes:

- General style attributes: *font*, *wrap, textVerticalPosition, textHorizontalPosition, lineStyle, orientation, isRounded, pathStyle, showPayload.*
- Compartment style attributes: showCompartment, showShortName,showName, showFullText, showSpecialization, showQualifiedNameForSpecialization, showModifiers, showValue, showInheritedSign, showInherited, showOnlyOwned.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']The Symbol Style dialog displays the highlighted rows of the updated attributes after activating a new style sheet within which the values of layout-sensitive attributes are updated. 
At the top of the dialog, a notification provides commands for either using the new style or retaining the old one.

###### Assessing layout-sensitive changes

To assess layout-sensitive changes

1. In a view, right-click an affected symbol. The procedure must be performed for each affected symbol individually.
2. In the shortcut menu, click Symbol Style . The Symbol Style dialog opens.
3. Review the affected attributes in the highlighted rows. The rows display the old attribute values.
4. In the notification at the top of the dialog, click one of the following commands:
  1. Use the new style - implements the updated attribute values and displays the symbol in the new style.
  2. Retain the old style - does not implement the updated attribute values and displays the symbol in the old style. Undoing the commandTo undo the **Retain the old style** command for a selected attribute: 
If you retain the old style, the check boxes in the **Default**column are deselected because the affected attributes are not inherited from the current style sheet and are considered individually modified.You can undo the retained style and switch to the new one for the specific attribute by selecting the check box in the **Default**column for the appropriate attribute.To undo either the **Retain the old style**or**Use the new style**command for all attributes, do one of the following:Press **Ctrl+Z** on the keyboard.In the **main menu**, click **Edit**> **Undo**.
5. Click Close .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Once you have created a style sheet and either customized the <em>DefaultStyleSheet</em> components or created custom ones, you need to synchronize the modifications in the Textual Editor and update all views for the modifications to be reflected in model views. Make sure to check whether you also need to assess layout-sensitive changes, as otherwise the updated styles will not be implemented.</p><h3>Synchronizing modifications</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="05d69a55-58e0-4d19-a0d3-c60210eb75c1"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span>Synchronization and <ac:link ac:anchor="Assessing layout-sensitive changes"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[assessment]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> do not affect customizations made to individual symbols via the <strong>Symbol Style</strong> dialog. See the <ac:link ac:anchor="Resetting changes made in the Symbol Style dialog"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Modifying styles of individual symbols" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Resetting changes made in the Symbol Style dialog]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> procedure on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Modifying styles of individual symbols" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Modifying styles of individual symbols]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</span></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">To synchronize modifications</span></p><hr /><ol><li><p>Do one of the following:</p><ol><li><p><span>In the Textual Editor's toolbar, click the</span><span> </span><strong>Synchronize</strong><span> </span><span>button</span><span> </span><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="sync_button.png" /></ac:image></span><span>.</span></p></li><li>Use the <strong>Alt+S </strong>shortcut keys.</li></ol></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Ensure the new <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Activating style sheets" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[style sheet is activated]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Press the <strong>F5 </strong>key on the keyboard to update all affected views.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">(Conditional) <ac:link ac:anchor="Assessing layout-sensitive changes"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Assess layout-sensitive changes]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.<br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0bab5942-63c2-4d88-a8d0-2b2667c02932"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You need to assess layout-sensitive changes if y<span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">ou already have views in your project and t</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span>he style sheet contains updated values of the following attributes:</span></span></p><ul><li data-uuid="4ab32a96-d61e-4a7a-8c6f-c51ac4dfc3a1"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span>General style attributes: <em>font</em>, <em>wrap, textVerticalPosition, textHorizontalPosition, lineStyle, orientation, isRounded, pathStyle, showPayload.</em></span></span></li><li data-uuid="28efb5ad-dc86-4e36-8e60-2cf3bc9fcfbe"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span>Compartment style attributes: </span></span><em><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">showCompartment, showShortName, </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">showName, showFullText, showSpecialization, showQualifiedNameForSpecialization, showModifiers, showValue, showInheritedSign, showInherited, showOnlyOwned.</span></em></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h3>Assessing layout-sensitive changes</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span>Certain style attributes are layout-sensitive as changing their values can affect already-created views' layout. Whether you have <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Customizing style sheet components" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[modified an existing style sheet]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating style sheet components" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[created]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Activating style sheets" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[activated]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> a new one if the updated style sheet contains styles with attribute values that are different from those that were used prior, you need to follow the procedure below to assess and either confirm or deny the changes. <br /></span></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="972dee1a-e08e-4af3-ba26-fc9cd3f2886d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span>Omitting this procedure means the modeling tool will not implement the updates of the new style and retain the previous attribute values representing symbols in the old style. </span></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span>Follow the procedure below if you already have views in your project and the style sheet contains updated values of the following attributes:</span></span></p><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span>General style attributes: <em>font</em>, <em>wrap, textVerticalPosition, textHorizontalPosition, lineStyle, orientation, isRounded, pathStyle, showPayload.</em></span></span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span>Compartment style attributes: </span></span><em><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">showCompartment, showShortName, </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">showName, showFullText, showSpecialization, showQualifiedNameForSpecialization, showModifiers, showValue, showInheritedSign, showInherited, showOnlyOwned.<br /></span></em></li></ul><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Assessing_layout_sensitive_changes.png" /></ac:image>The Symbol Style dialog displays the highlighted rows of the updated attributes after activating a new style sheet within which the values of layout-sensitive attributes are updated. <br />At the top of the dialog, a notification provides commands for either using the new style or retaining the old one.</h6><h4>Assessing layout-sensitive changes</h4><p>To assess layout-sensitive changes</p><hr /><ol><li>In a view, right-click an affected symbol.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="643cc83b-0e0a-4ac6-8787-818644a7392a"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The procedure must be performed for each affected symbol individually.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>In the shortcut menu, click <strong>Symbol Style</strong>. The <strong>Symbol Style </strong>dialog opens.</li><li>Review the affected attributes in the highlighted rows. The rows display the old attribute values. </li><li>In the notification at the top of the dialog, click one of the following commands: <ol><li><strong>Use the new style</strong> - implements the updated attribute values and displays the symbol in the new style.</li><li><strong>Retain the old style</strong> - does not implement the updated attribute values and displays the symbol in the old style.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="aeb809d7-11d6-495c-a54b-9826072206b2"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Undoing the command</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>To undo the <strong>Retain the old style</strong> command for a selected attribute:<br />If you retain the old style, the check boxes in the <strong>Default </strong>column are deselected because the affected attributes are not inherited from the current style sheet and are considered individually modified.<ul><li>You can undo the retained style and switch to the new one for the specific attribute by selecting the check box in the <strong>Default </strong>column for the appropriate attribute.</li></ul></li><li>To undo either the <strong>Retain the old style </strong>or<strong> Use the new style </strong>command for all attributes, do one of the following:<ul><li>Press <strong>Ctrl+Z</strong> on the keyboard.</li><li>In the <strong>main menu</strong>, click <strong>Edit </strong>&gt; <strong>Undo</strong>.</li></ul></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></li><li>Click <strong>Close</strong>.</li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254421938 space=SYSML2P version=9 -->
## PAGE 00436: (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation

- page_id: `254421938`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254421938/2026x+SysML+v2+Plugin+Documentation
- version_number: 9
- version_date: `2026-01-30T13:29:23.355+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### EXCERPT: CATIA Magic/Cameo SysML v2 Community Edition is out now!

INLINE

#### NOTE: CATIA Magic/Cameo SysML v2 Community Edition is out now!

false

CATIA Magic/Cameo SysML v2 Community Edition is out now!

Explore our SysML v2 solution with the free **CATIA Magic/Cameo SysML v2 Community Edition**. Click [here](https://discover.3ds.com/free-catia-sysmlv2-community-edition) to learn more.

SysML v2 is a next-generation systems modeling language. Compared to SysML v1, SysML v2 intends to provide improved precision, expressiveness, and usability of the modeling language.

To fully take advantage of SysML v2, two primary plugins are available for you:

- The SysML v2 Plugin provides core SysML v2 features and includes the SysML v2 Textual Editor, enabling you to model using both textual and graphical notations.
- The [SysML v2 Evaluation Plugin](https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2EP/?contextKey=sysml-v2-evaluation-plugin-documentation&version=2026x) allows you to conduct static evaluations of SysML v2 models .

For more information, please see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Prerequisites' space=''] page.

For your convenience, you can download the **SysML v2 Quick Sheet** as a concise reference to the SysML v2 language on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SysML v2 Quick Sheet' space=''] page.

The following topics explain how to install and use the SysML v2 Plugin:

true

Click <a href="https://discover.3ds.com/free-catia-sysmlv2-community-edition">here</a> to learn more.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>SysML v2 is a next-generation systems modeling language. Compared to SysML v1, SysML v2 intends to provide improved precision, expressiveness, and usability of the modeling language.  </p><p>To fully take advantage of SysML v2, two primary plugins are available for you:</p><ul><li>The <strong>SysML v2 Plugin</strong> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">provides core SysML v2 features and includes the SysML v2 Textual Editor, enabling you to model using both textual and graphical notations.</span></li><li>The <strong><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2EP/?contextKey=sysml-v2-evaluation-plugin-documentation&amp;version=2026x">SysML v2 Evaluation Plugin</a></strong> allows you to conduct static <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">evaluations of SysML v2 models</span>.</li></ul><p>For more information, please see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Prerequisites" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Prerequisites]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6c410765-848a-4ccb-813e-84a0df3820e9"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For your convenience, you can download the <strong>SysML v2 Quick Sheet</strong> as a concise reference to the SysML v2 language on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SysML v2 Quick Sheet" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SysML v2 Quick Sheet]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The following topics explain how to install and use the SysML v2 Plugin:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="149f91e3-4bfa-409d-a019-a2676875ad4c"><ac:parameter ac:name="all">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
